basmala: In the name of God, the All-Merciful, the Compassionate.
intro: Āl ʿImrān — The Family of ʿImrān — is a great Medinan sūra of 200 verses that continues al-Baqara's meditation on faith under trial. It opens with the Names of the Living, the Self-Subsisting (al-Ḥayy al-Qayyūm) and the Book sent down in Truth, then unfolds the sacred history of ʿImrān's family: Maryam consecrated to the Temple, Zakariyyā and the secret of provision, the annunciation of Yaḥyā and ʿĪsā. Qushayrī reads here the divine pedagogy of trial: the reverses at Uḥud were not abandonment but unveiling, for God tests hearts to draw forth their hidden sincerity. The sūra culminates in the call of those endowed with understanding (ūlū l-albāb), who meditate upon creation standing, sitting, and lying down, and in the promise made to those who call upon Him without ceasing: no sincere striving toward Him is ever lost, for He does not let the work of any worker go to waste.
themes: the Book sent down in Truth and the clear verses
the family of ʿImrān: Maryam, Zakariyyā, ʿĪsā
the trial of faith (Uḥud) and patience
the unity of believers and the rope of God
the meditation of those endowed with understanding

versets.ar: الم
versets.tr: alif lām mīm
versets.trad: Alif, Lām, Mīm.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī reads in these three isolated letters a discreet address to the heart. The Alif designates the One who stands surety for whatever suffices you in every state: prisoner of heedlessness, you know not how to find your own good, yet He suffices you without your asking, and gives to you without your seeking. The Lām points to His subtle grace hidden in your innermost secret, so veiled that you do not even perceive the place of His favour. The Mīm seals the accord of the whole decree with what those near to Him desire: nothing stirs in the universe, not an atom appears, without their consent to it. And it is said further: as the heart listens to these broken letters, foreign to the speech of men, every conventional knowledge dissolves from it, every habit, every imagining, until, emptied of the known and the supposed, the purified secret receives this Name: “Allāh.”

mots.def: three opening letters, in which Qushayrī finds a secret address to the heart (sufficiency, hidden subtlety, consent).

versets.ar: اللَّهُ لَا إِلَٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَ الْحَيُّ الْقَيُّومُ
versets.tr: allāhu lā ilāha illā huwa l-ḥayyu l-qayyūm
versets.trad: God (Allāh)! There is no god but He: the Living, the Self-Subsisting!
versets.commentaire: When the heart has stripped itself of all otherness, this Name descends upon it, “Allāh,” upon a heart sanctified of every other-than-Him. Qushayrī: He is the One who is never distracted from you to occupy Himself with another, who is never absent so that you should remain without Him; He watches over your secret in every state. “Whether you are alone, He keeps you; whether you are amid the creatures, He keeps you; and in any case, whatever turn your states take, He is your Beloved.”

mots.racine: أ ل ه
mots.def: the proper Name of the Essence, which descends upon the heart purified of all else.

mots.racine: أ ل ه
ح ي ي / ق و م
mots.def: al-Qayyūm: the One who subsists by Himself and by whom all things subsist.

versets.ar: نَزَّلَ عَلَيْكَ الْكِتَابَ بِالْحَقِّ مُصَدِّقًا لِمَا بَيْنَ يَدَيْهِ وَأَنزَلَ التَّوْرَاةَ وَالْإِنجِيلَ
versets.tr: nazzala ʿalayka l-kitāba bi-l-ḥaqqi muṣaddiqan li-mā bayna yadayhi wa-anzala t-tawrāta wa-l-injīl
versets.trad: He has sent down upon you the Book in all Truth, confirming what came before it, and He has sent down the Torah and the Gospel,
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī addresses the Prophet: you did not know, O Muḥammad, what the Book was, nor the story of the beloved ones; yet a choice from all eternity reached you and cast you into an affair of admirable destiny, of dazzling proof, of precious rank. And he adds, concerning the confirmation of the earlier Scriptures: even the Books revealed to the messengers before you, We left none of them empty of your mention; as We crowned through you the lights of the prophets, so We adorned with your remembrance all that We had sent down.

mots.racine: ن ز ل / ك ت ب
mots.sens: He has sent down upon you the Book
mots.def: the Qurʾān sent down upon the Prophet by a pre-eternal choice.

mots.racine: ح ق ق
mots.def: according to the veracious reality, without any admixture.

mots.racine: ص د ق
mots.def: fulfilling the promise made by the mouth of the earlier messengers.
the two Scriptures revealed before the Qurʾān.

versets.ar: مِن قَبْلُ هُدًى لِّلنَّاسِ وَأَنزَلَ الْفُرْقَانَ إِنَّ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا بِآيَاتِ اللَّهِ لَهُمْ عَذَابٌ شَدِيدٌ وَاللَّهُ عَزِيزٌ ذُو انتِقَامٍ
versets.tr: min qablu hudan li-n-nāsi wa-anzala l-furqāna inna lladhīna kafarū bi-āyāti llāhi lahum ʿadhābun shadīdun wa-llāhu ʿazīzun dhū ntiqām
versets.trad: [which were] formerly a guidance for mankind. And He has sent down the Criterion. Truly, those who refuse to believe in the signs of God incur a severe chastisement. God is mighty, Master of retribution.
versets.commentaire: The severe chastisement promised to those who reject the signs, Qushayrī unveils in its inner sense: it is the humiliation of the veil — but they do not sense it. As for the two Names that seal the verse, he turns them toward the friends and the enemies: “God is mighty” toward those near to Him, “Master of retribution” toward His adversaries; a Mighty One whom every being seeks, yet whom few, alas, ever reach.

mots.racine: ه د ي
mots.def: guidance offered by the earlier Scriptures.

mots.racine: ف ر ق
mots.def: that which separates the true from the false, another name for the Qurʾān.

mots.racine: ك ف ر / أ ي ي
mots.sens: they rejected the signs of God
mots.def: the denial of God's verses and signs.

mots.racine: ع ز ز / ن ق م
mots.def: al-ʿAzīz toward those near to Him, avenger toward His enemies (Qushayrī).

versets.ar: إِنَّ اللَّهَ لَا يَخْفَىٰ عَلَيْهِ شَيْءٌ فِي الْأَرْضِ وَلَا فِي السَّمَاءِ
versets.tr: inna llāha lā yakhfā ʿalayhi shayʾun fī l-arḍi wa-lā fī s-samāʾ
versets.trad: Nothing, assuredly, is hidden from God, neither on earth nor in heaven.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī unfolds the omniscience in two registers. On the universal plane: no servant draws a single breath without God counting it; no atom takes form in heaven or on earth without His being its author; none is clothed in an attribute without His being its master. And on the plane of the elect, more intimate still: none lifts toward Him a need without His fulfilling it, none turns to Him in trial without His sufficing him.

mots.racine: خ ف ي
mots.def: the divine knowledge embraces all without exception.

mots.racine: أ ر ض / س م و
mots.def: the two orders of the created, the totality of the existent.

versets.ar: هُوَ الَّذِي يُصَوِّرُكُمْ فِي الْأَرْحَامِ كَيْفَ يَشَاءُ لَا إِلَٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَ الْعَزِيزُ الْحَكِيمُ
versets.tr: huwa lladhī yuṣawwirukum fī l-arḥāmi kayfa yashāʾu lā ilāha illā huwa l-ʿazīzu l-ḥakīm
versets.trad: It is He who forms you in the wombs as He wills. There is no god but He, the All-Powerful, the All-Wise!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī distinguishes two moments of His sovereignty: this touches the realm of becoming, on the side of creation — but it is also He who measured out your states in pre-eternity as He willed, and that belongs to the eternal without beginning, on the side of the decree and the apportionment. And he comments on the two Names: none can break His judgement, none can thwart His measure through forgetting or rejection.

mots.racine: ص و ر / ر ح م
mots.sens: He fashions you in the wombs
mots.def: the molding of the human form, image of the pre-eternal decree.

mots.racine: ش ي أ
mots.def: according to His will alone, without constraint.

mots.racine: ع ز ز / ح ك م
mots.def: whose judgement is neither broken nor thwarted.

versets.ar: هُوَ الَّذِي أَنزَلَ عَلَيْكَ الْكِتَابَ مِنْهُ آيَاتٌ مُّحْكَمَاتٌ هُنَّ أُمُّ الْكِتَابِ وَأُخَرُ مُتَشَابِهَاتٌ فَأَمَّا الَّذِينَ فِي قُلُوبِهِمْ زَيْغٌ فَيَتَّبِعُونَ مَا تَشَابَهَ مِنْهُ ابْتِغَاءَ الْفِتْنَةِ وَابْتِغَاءَ تَأْوِيلِهِ وَمَا يَعْلَمُ تَأْوِيلَهُ إِلَّا اللَّهُ وَالرَّاسِخُونَ فِي الْعِلْمِ يَقُولُونَ آمَنَّا بِهِ كُلٌّ مِّنْ عِندِ رَبِّنَا وَمَا يَذَّكَّرُ إِلَّا أُولُو الْأَلْبَابِ
versets.tr: huwa lladhī anzala ʿalayka l-kitāba minhu āyātun muḥkamātun hunna ummu l-kitābi wa-ukharu mutashābihātun fa-ammā lladhīna fī qulūbihim zayghun fa-yattabiʿūna mā tashābaha minhu btighāʾa l-fitnati wa-btighāʾa taʾwīlihi wa-mā yaʿlamu taʾwīlahu illā llāhu wa-r-rāsikhūna fī l-ʿilmi yaqūlūna āmannā bihi kullun min ʿindi rabbinā wa-mā yadhdhakkaru illā ulū l-albāb
versets.trad: It is He who has sent down upon you the Book. In it are explicit verses: these are the Mother of the Book; and others that are allegorical. Those in whose hearts there is a distortion cling to what is allegorical, for they seek discord and crave its interpretation; yet none but God knows its interpretation. Those firmly rooted in knowledge say: “We believe in it; all is from our Lord!” But none reflect save those endowed with understanding.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī makes of the Book's twofold register a mercy and a protection. He varied the discourse for them: here a manifest sense, of clear disclosure; there a veiled sense, of difficult interpretation. The first unfolds the Law and guides the people of the outward; the second shelters the secrets from the gaze of strangers. To the learned he assigns the way of rootedness: to seek the sense according to the principles, to receive what is established, and to entrust to the Knower of the unseen what resists thought. To the people of allusion and inner comprehension he assigns another way: to lend the ear with the heart's presence, and to build upon the gleams that offer themselves to them the indications of unveiling.

mots.racine: أ ي ي / ح ك م
mots.def: verses of clear and established meaning, foundation of the Law.

mots.racine: أ م م / ك ت ب
mots.def: the matrix of the Book, to which the rest is referred.

mots.racine: ش ب ه
mots.def: verses of veiled sense, which shelter the secrets.

mots.racine: ز ي غ
mots.def: deviation that drives one to exploit the ambiguous to sow discord.

mots.racine: ر س خ / ع ل م
mots.def: those whose faith springs from a limpid certitude, without wavering.

mots.racine: ل ب ب
mots.def: lit. “the possessors of marrow,” those who truly reflect.

versets.ar: رَبَّنَا لَا تُزِغْ قُلُوبَنَا بَعْدَ إِذْ هَدَيْتَنَا وَهَبْ لَنَا مِن لَّدُنكَ رَحْمَةً إِنَّكَ أَنتَ الْوَهَّابُ
versets.tr: rabbanā lā tuzigh qulūbanā baʿda idh hadaytanā wa-hab lanā min ladunka raḥmatan innaka anta l-wahhāb
versets.trad: Our Lord! Let not our hearts swerve after You have guided us; grant us a mercy from Yourself. You are the Supreme Bestower.
themes: the humility of those near to God
versets.commentaire: This prayer is, for Qushayrī, the sign of a paradoxical delicacy of soul. The closer they draw, the more they redouble their courtesy; and to take refuge in the sense of remoteness is among the surest ways of observing that courtesy. Far from supposing themselves arrived, those near to Him still beg for guidance, aware that it rests not on their works but on grace alone. And he adds: because they were sincere in the beauty of their supplication, they were succoured by the lights of the divine sufficiency.

mots.racine: ز ي غ
mots.def: a plea for preservation against straying after guidance.

mots.racine: ه د ي
mots.def: acknowledgement of the guidance already received.

mots.racine: ر ح م
mots.def: pure grace issuing from His favour alone.

mots.racine: و ه ب
mots.def: the One who gives unceasingly and without return.

versets.ar: رَبَّنَا إِنَّكَ جَامِعُ النَّاسِ لِيَوْمٍ لَّا رَيْبَ فِيهِ إِنَّ اللَّهَ لَا يُخْلِفُ الْمِيعَادَ
versets.tr: rabbanā innaka jāmiʿu n-nāsi li-yawmin lā rayba fīhi inna llāha lā yukhlifu l-mīʿād
versets.trad: Our Lord! Truly, You will gather mankind on a Day in which there is no doubt, for God does not fail His promise.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī superimposes two gatherings, one already present, the other to come. Today the beloved ones are gathered upon the carpet of nearness; tomorrow the totality will be gathered at the place of recompense and chastisement. And more inwardly still: today the secrets are gathered for the unveiling of majesty and beauty; tomorrow the bodies will be gathered for the contemplation of the states.

mots.racine: ج م ع
mots.def: the universal gathering on the Day of Resurrection.

mots.racine: ر ي ب
mots.sens: for a Day in which there is no doubt
mots.def: the certain appointed time of the Resurrection.

mots.racine: خ ل ف / و ع د
mots.def: the absolute fidelity of God to His word.

versets.ar: إِنَّ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا لَن تُغْنِيَ عَنْهُمْ أَمْوَالُهُمْ وَلَا أَوْلَادُهُم مِّنَ اللَّهِ شَيْئًا وَأُولَٰئِكَ هُمْ وَقُودُ النَّارِ
versets.tr: inna lladhīna kafarū lan tughniya ʿanhum amwāluhum wa-lā awlāduhum mina llāhi shayʾan wa-ulāʾika hum waqūdu n-nār
versets.trad: Truly, as for the deniers, neither their wealth nor their children will avail them anything against God; those are the fuel of the Fire.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī enumerates, in an implacable cascade, the doors that close upon the denier: no ransom profits them, no riches save them, no good is accepted from them, no veil is lifted for them, no word is heard in their favour; through them Gehenna is kindled, theirs is the grievous banishment, the estrangement and the scalding water.

mots.racine: غ ن ي
mots.def: the impotence of human means before God.

mots.racine: م و ل / و ل د
mots.def: the two supports of worldly life, here annulled.

mots.racine: و ق د / ن و ر
mots.def: the combustible by which Gehenna is kindled.

versets.ar: كَدَأْبِ آلِ فِرْعَوْنَ وَالَّذِينَ مِن قَبْلِهِمْ كَذَّبُوا بِآيَاتِنَا فَأَخَذَهُمُ اللَّهُ بِذُنُوبِهِمْ وَاللَّهُ شَدِيدُ الْعِقَابِ
versets.tr: ka-daʾbi āli firʿawna wa-lladhīna min qablihim kadhdhabū bi-āyātinā fa-akhadhahumu llāhu bi-dhunūbihim wa-llāhu shadīdu l-ʿiqāb
versets.trad: Such was the case of the people of Pharaoh and of those before them. They treated Our signs as lies; God seized them for their sins. God is severe in chastisement.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī reads in the fall of Pharaoh a law of hardened hearts. They persisted in their arrogance after their custom, and We prolonged for them Our custom in chastisement: they did not renounce their obstinacy, they coveted nothing good. And on their belated remorse: indeed they repented and lamented over what they had done — but when they found the door shut, and repentance refused.

mots.racine: د أ ب
mots.sens: such was the case of Pharaoh's people
mots.def: the custom, the manner of acting of the ancient deniers.

mots.racine: ك ذ ب / أ ي ي
mots.def: the obstinate denial of the divine proofs.

mots.racine: ش د د / ع ق ب
mots.def: the severity of the divine sanction.

versets.ar: قُل لِّلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا سَتُغْلَبُونَ وَتُحْشَرُونَ إِلَىٰ جَهَنَّمَ وَبِئْسَ الْمِهَادُ
versets.tr: qul li-lladhīna kafarū sa-tughlabūna wa-tuḥsharūna ilā jahannama wa-biʾsa l-mihād
versets.trad: Say to the deniers: “You shall be overcome; you shall be herded into Gehenna.” What a wretched resting-place!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī pierces beneath the outward defeat to a more essential privation. Announce to them that they will miss, in the hereafter, the speech of the Real, and that they will have here below no sweetness of life; and what they will meet in the other world — the violence of torment by burning — is above what reaches them in this world: the absence of God and separation. Yet, he adds, their inner sight being diseased, they did not even perceive the pain of the chastisement.

mots.racine: غ ل ب
ح ش ر
mots.sens: you shall be herded into Gehenna
mots.def: the final gathering toward the fire.

mots.racine: م ه د
mots.def: al-mihād: the couch, the abode — here detestable.

versets.ar: قَدْ كَانَ لَكُمْ آيَةٌ فِي فِئَتَيْنِ الْتَقَتَا فِئَةٌ تُقَاتِلُ فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ وَأُخْرَىٰ كَافِرَةٌ يَرَوْنَهُم مِّثْلَيْهِمْ رَأْيَ الْعَيْنِ وَاللَّهُ يُؤَيِّدُ بِنَصْرِهِ مَن يَشَاءُ إِنَّ فِي ذَٰلِكَ لَعِبْرَةً لِّأُولِي الْأَبْصَارِ
versets.tr: qad kāna lakum āyatun fī fiʾatayni ltaqatā fiʾatun tuqātilu fī sabīli llāhi wa-ukhrā kāfiratun yarawnahum mithlayhim raʾya l-ʿayni wa-llāhu yuʾayyidu bi-naṣrihi man yashāʾu inna fī dhālika la-ʿibratan li-ulī l-abṣār
versets.trad: You had a sign in the two hosts that met. One of them fought in the way of God, while the other was of deniers who, with their own eyes, saw the believers as twice their own number. God reinforces with His aid whom He wills. Truly, in this is a lesson for those endowed with clear sight.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī draws from Badr a lesson on the sovereign freedom of the divine gaze. When God wills to accomplish a design, He makes the many appear few to the eyes of some, and the few appear many to the eyes of others. Then he transposes it to the plane of hearts: when He veils the inner sight of a group, the keenness of their gaze avails them nothing; and when He opens the secrets of others, the obstruction of their eyes does them no harm.

mots.racine: ف أ و / ل ق ي
mots.def: the two camps at the battle of Badr.

mots.racine: ق ت ل / س ب ل
mots.sens: fights in the way of God
mots.def: the struggle for the divine cause.

mots.racine: أ ي د / ن ص ر
mots.sens: reinforces with His aid whom He wills
mots.def: the sovereign freedom of the divine succour.

mots.racine: ع ب ر / ب ص ر
mots.def: ʿibra: the lesson drawn by one who “crosses over” from the outward.

versets.ar: زُيِّنَ لِلنَّاسِ حُبُّ الشَّهَوَاتِ مِنَ النِّسَاءِ وَالْبَنِينَ وَالْقَنَاطِيرِ الْمُقَنطَرَةِ مِنَ الذَّهَبِ وَالْفِضَّةِ وَالْخَيْلِ الْمُسَوَّمَةِ وَالْأَنْعَامِ وَالْحَرْثِ ذَٰلِكَ مَتَاعُ الْحَيَاةِ الدُّنْيَا وَاللَّهُ عِندَهُ حُسْنُ الْمَآبِ
versets.tr: zuyyina li-n-nāsi ḥubbu sh-shahawāti mina n-nisāʾi wa-l-banīna wa-l-qanāṭīri l-muqanṭarati mina dh-dhahabi wa-l-fiḍḍati wa-l-khayli l-musawwamati wa-l-anʿāmi wa-l-ḥarthi dhālika matāʿu l-ḥayāti d-dunyā wa-llāhu ʿindahu ḥusnu l-maʾāb
versets.trad: Men have been allured by the love of things desirable: women, children, heaped-up hoards of gold and silver, thoroughbred horses, cattle, and tilled lands. Such are the enjoyments of the life of this world; but with God is the fairest return.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī broadens the enumeration to take in every form of veil. He mentions a few of the appetites, standing for all that resemble them; in short, whatever veils you from contemplation belongs among them. Then he points to the subtlest snare: the most formidable of obstacles on this way is the hidden desire — to perform acts of obedience for the savour one finds in them counts among these hidden desires. And subtler still: the repose taken in the favours with which He showers you; as though He flattered you by entrusting you — and beneath every delicacy lurk His stratagems.

mots.racine: ز ي ن / ش ه و
mots.sens: the love of appetites has been made fair
mots.def: the embellishment of desires in the eyes of men.

mots.racine: ق ن ط ر
mots.def: qinṭār: a great quantity of accumulated wealth.

mots.racine: خ ي ل / س و م
mots.def: horses branded, of noble lineage or set to pasture.

mots.racine: م ت ع / ح ي ي / د ن و
mots.sens: enjoyment of the life of this world
mots.def: the passing goods of the lower world.

mots.racine: ح س ن / أ و ب
mots.def: the beauty of the place of return, with God.

versets.ar: قُلْ أَؤُنَبِّئُكُم بِخَيْرٍ مِّن ذَٰلِكُمْ لِلَّذِينَ اتَّقَوْا عِندَ رَبِّهِمْ جَنَّاتٌ تَجْرِي مِن تَحْتِهَا الْأَنْهَارُ خَالِدِينَ فِيهَا وَأَزْوَاجٌ مُّطَهَّرَةٌ وَرِضْوَانٌ مِّنَ اللَّهِ وَاللَّهُ بَصِيرٌ بِالْعِبَادِ
versets.tr: qul a-unabbiʾukum bi-khayrin min dhālikum li-lladhīna ttaqaw ʿinda rabbihim jannātun tajrī min taḥtihā l-anhāru khālidīna fīhā wa-azwājun muṭahharatun wa-riḍwānun mina llāhi wa-llāhu baṣīrun bi-l-ʿibād
versets.trad: Say: “Shall I tell you of what is better than all that? For those who fear their Lord there are, with Him, Gardens beneath which the rivers flow; therein they shall abide forever, with pure spouses and the good pleasure of God.” And God sees His servants perfectly,
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī contrasts the two destinies without confounding their rewards. He showed the merit of the God-fearing over the people of this world: to these, the satisfying of their wish and the indulgence of their whim; to those, the sublime degrees. And he seals upon the justice of the divine gaze: “God sees His servants perfectly” — He has lodged each people in its dwelling and led each to where it was worthy to be led.

mots.racine: ن ب أ / خ ي ر
mots.sens: shall I tell you of something better
mots.def: a question opening onto the superior reward.

mots.racine: و ق ي
mots.def: the God-fearing, keepers of reverent fear.

mots.racine: ز و ج / ط ه ر
ر ض و
mots.def: the divine satisfaction, summit of the gifts.

versets.ar: الَّذِينَ يَقُولُونَ رَبَّنَا إِنَّنَا آمَنَّا فَاغْفِرْ لَنَا ذُنُوبَنَا وَقِنَا عَذَابَ النَّارِ
versets.tr: alladhīna yaqūlūna rabbanā innanā āmannā fa-ghfir lanā dhunūbanā wa-qinā ʿadhāba n-nār
versets.trad: those who say: “Our Lord, truly we have believed! So forgive us our sins and shield us from the chastisement of the Fire,”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī hears in this prayer a total severance from the world. They break away toward Us entirely, they supplicate before Us, recalling their trials and their destitution; those obtain from Us nearness and election, the sublime degrees and the portion that is pleasing.

mots.racine: أ م ن
mots.def: the affirmation of faith that founds the request.

mots.racine: غ ف ر / ذ ن ب
mots.def: the request for pardon of faults.

mots.racine: و ق ي / ع ذ ب
mots.sens: shield us from the chastisement of the Fire
mots.def: a plea for protection from the fire.

versets.ar: الصَّابِرِينَ وَالصَّادِقِينَ وَالْقَانِتِينَ وَالْمُنفِقِينَ وَالْمُسْتَغْفِرِينَ بِالْأَسْحَارِ
versets.tr: aṣ-ṣābirīna wa-ṣ-ṣādiqīna wa-l-qānitīna wa-l-munfiqīna wa-l-mustaghfirīna bi-l-asḥār
versets.trad: those who are patient, truthful, devoutly obedient, who give in alms, and who, at the break of dawn, implore forgiveness.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī climbs the degrees of each virtue. Patience is the restraint of the soul, in three ranks: patience in what is commanded, patience before what is forbidden, and patience under the unfolding of His decree — whether in the loss of the beloved or in the onslaught of what one cannot bear; and should you rise beyond this, feeling neither pain nor ease, that is contentment, no longer patience. He declines them further: the patient by their hearts, the truthful by their spirits, the obedient by their souls, the imploring by their tongues. And of the “imploring at dawn”: they excuse themselves for all they have done, seeing their shortcoming before God — at the dawn of hearts, not the dawn that breaks on the horizon.

mots.racine: ص ب ر
mots.def: restraint of the soul in the command, the prohibition, and the decree.

mots.racine: ص د ق
mots.def: sincere in their engagement toward God.

mots.racine: ق ن ت
mots.def: those who stand constant in the love of God.

mots.racine: ن ف ق
mots.def: generous of their goods, then of their souls (Qushayrī).

mots.racine: غ ف ر / س ح ر
mots.def: al-asḥār: the last moments of the night, the time of imploring.

versets.ar: شَهِدَ اللَّهُ أَنَّهُ لَا إِلَٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَ وَالْمَلَائِكَةُ وَأُولُو الْعِلْمِ قَائِمًا بِالْقِسْطِ لَا إِلَٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَ الْعَزِيزُ الْحَكِيمُ
versets.tr: shahida llāhu annahu lā ilāha illā huwa wa-l-malāʾikatu wa-ulū l-ʿilmi qāʾiman bi-l-qisṭi lā ilāha illā huwa l-ʿazīzu l-ḥakīm
versets.trad: God bears witness that there is no god but He; so too do the angels and those who possess knowledge and are firm in equity. There is no god but He, the All-Powerful, the All-Wise!
versets.commentaire: For Qushayrī, this verse is the summit of attestation: it is the witness of the Real for the Real that He is the Real. The first to bear witness that God is God is God — He bore witness in pre-eternity by His word, attesting to His unique existence, to His absolute sufficiency. As for the angels and the learned, He does not reinforce His witness by theirs: rather He has favoured and sustained them, bringing them into accord with His own witness, guiding them to the knowledge of His oneness. And of “those endowed with knowledge”: they are the friends among the children of Adam; they have known the majesty of His power, and He honoured them by joining His witness to theirs — a witness of contemplation, not of conjecture.

mots.racine: ش ه د
mots.def: the primal witness of the Real to Himself.

mots.racine: ل أ ك
mots.def: witnesses sustained and brought into accord with His witness.

mots.racine: ع ل م
mots.def: the knowers, friends of God, witnesses through contemplation.

mots.racine: ق و م / ق س ط
mots.def: the justice to which the witness holds firm.

versets.ar: إِنَّ الدِّينَ عِندَ اللَّهِ الْإِسْلَامُ وَمَا اخْتَلَفَ الَّذِينَ أُوتُوا الْكِتَابَ إِلَّا مِن بَعْدِ مَا جَاءَهُمُ الْعِلْمُ بَغْيًا بَيْنَهُمْ وَمَن يَكْفُرْ بِآيَاتِ اللَّهِ فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ سَرِيعُ الْحِسَابِ
versets.tr: inna d-dīna ʿinda llāhi l-islāmu wa-mā khtalafa lladhīna ūtū l-kitāba illā min baʿdi mā jāʾahumu l-ʿilmu baghyan baynahum wa-man yakfur bi-āyāti llāhi fa-inna llāha sarīʿu l-ḥisāb
versets.trad: Truly, the religion with God is Submission [to His will: al-islām]. Those to whom the Scripture was given fell into disagreement only after knowledge had come to them, out of mutual jealousy. With him who rejects His signs, God, truly, is swift at the reckoning.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī brings islām back to its inner heart. The religion He accepts, for which He has decreed that He will reward, raise up, and favour the one who holds to it — that is islām: pure surrender and total handing-over. All that is not this is rejected, and the way of salvation is closed to whoever turns from it. On the discord of the People of the Book: the knowledge that came to them was a proof against them, not the knowledge that illumines; they persisted in denial because they were veiled from the place of contemplation.

mots.racine: د ي ن / س ل م
mots.sens: the religion with God is islām
mots.def: islām: pure surrender and handing of oneself over to God.

mots.racine: خ ل ف
mots.def: discord arising after the coming of knowledge.

mots.racine: ب غ ي
mots.def: the transgression born of mutual envy.

mots.racine: س ر ع / ح س ب
mots.def: the swiftness of the divine judgement.

versets.ar: فَإِنْ حَاجُّوكَ فَقُلْ أَسْلَمْتُ وَجْهِيَ لِلَّهِ وَمَنِ اتَّبَعَنِ وَقُل لِّلَّذِينَ أُوتُوا الْكِتَابَ وَالْأُمِّيِّينَ أَأَسْلَمْتُمْ فَإِنْ أَسْلَمُوا فَقَدِ اهْتَدَوا وَّإِن تَوَلَّوْا فَإِنَّمَا عَلَيْكَ الْبَلَاغُ وَاللَّهُ بَصِيرٌ بِالْعِبَادِ
versets.tr: fa-in ḥājjūka fa-qul aslamtu wajhiya li-llāhi wa-mani ttabaʿani wa-qul li-lladhīna ūtū l-kitāba wa-l-ummiyyīna a-aslamtum fa-in aslamū fa-qadi htadaw wa-in tawallaw fa-innamā ʿalayka l-balāghu wa-llāhu baṣīrun bi-l-ʿibād
versets.trad: If they dispute with you, say: “I have surrendered my whole being to God, I and those who follow me.” And say to those given the Scripture and to the unlettered: “Have you surrendered to God?” If they surrender, they are rightly guided; and if they turn away, your charge is only to convey [the message]. And God sees His servants perfectly!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī addresses to the Prophet a counsel of inner vision. Regard them with the eye that sees the divine acting, so that your state may not scatter in contemplating their disputes and their failings; for whoever looks upon beings with the eye of Power knows that the One who upholds the whole — each in its singularity — is One. And he continues: call them openly, but contemplate in secret how We fashion them; occupy your tongue with counselling them, empty your heart of their affair, and keep your secret free of their presence: what We have charged you with is only the conveyance; the One who leads and brings things to appear is We.

mots.racine: ح ج ج
mots.def: the argument, the controversy directed at the Prophet.

mots.racine: س ل م / و ج ه
mots.sens: I have surrendered my face to God
mots.def: the total surrender of the whole being, the “face” denoting the entire person.

mots.racine: أ م م
mots.def: those who received no Scripture, the pagan Arabs.

mots.racine: ب ل غ
mots.sens: your charge is only the conveyance
mots.def: the Prophet's task is limited to proclaiming the message.

versets.ar: إِنَّ الَّذِينَ يَكْفُرُونَ بِآيَاتِ اللَّهِ وَيَقْتُلُونَ النَّبِيِّينَ بِغَيْرِ حَقٍّ وَيَقْتُلُونَ الَّذِينَ يَأْمُرُونَ بِالْقِسْطِ مِنَ النَّاسِ فَبَشِّرْهُم بِعَذَابٍ أَلِيمٍ
versets.tr: inna lladhīna yakfurūna bi-āyāti llāhi wa-yaqtulūna n-nabiyyīna bi-ghayri ḥaqqin wa-yaqtulūna lladhīna yaʾmurūna bi-l-qisṭi mina n-nāsi fa-bashshirhum bi-ʿadhābin alīm
versets.trad: To those who reject the signs of God, who kill the prophets without right and kill those among men who enjoin justice, announce a painful chastisement.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī reads in their crime the seal of an eternal forsaking. Those whom We have bound to abandonment and marked with the seal of deprivation — announce to them that Our turning away from them is perpetual, and that Our decree preceded their transfer from the abode of the Gardens to the abode of abasement, of abandonment and deprivation, unto the torment and the flames.

mots.racine: ق ت ل / ن ب أ
mots.sens: they kill the prophets without right
mots.def: the unjustified killing of God's messengers.

mots.racine: أ م ر / ق س ط
mots.def: those who prescribe equity among men.

mots.racine: ب ش ر / ع ذ ب / أ ل م
mots.sens: announce to them a painful chastisement
mots.def: an (ironic) announcement of a stinging torment.

versets.ar: أُولَٰئِكَ الَّذِينَ حَبِطَتْ أَعْمَالُهُمْ فِي الدُّنْيَا وَالْآخِرَةِ وَمَا لَهُم مِّن نَّاصِرِينَ
versets.tr: ulāʾika lladhīna ḥabiṭat aʿmāluhum fī d-dunyā wa-l-ākhirati wa-mā lahum min nāṣirīn
versets.trad: Of those, the deeds are made vain in this world and the next, and they find no helpers.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī traces their ruin to a deprivation of contemplation. Those have today no success in their works, nor tomorrow any fulfilment of their hopes; and this only because they lost Our succour in both abodes, and contemplated neither Our glory nor Our power.

mots.racine: ح ب ط / ع م ل
mots.def: the annihilation of the worth of their acts.

mots.racine: د ن و / أ خ ر
mots.sens: in this world and the next
mots.def: the vanity of their works in both abodes.

mots.racine: ن ص ر
mots.def: a total absence of aid or defender.

versets.ar: أَلَمْ تَرَ إِلَى الَّذِينَ أُوتُوا نَصِيبًا مِّنَ الْكِتَابِ يُدْعَوْنَ إِلَىٰ كِتَابِ اللَّهِ لِيَحْكُمَ بَيْنَهُمْ ثُمَّ يَتَوَلَّىٰ فَرِيقٌ مِّنْهُمْ وَهُم مُّعْرِضُونَ
versets.tr: a-lam tara ilā lladhīna ūtū naṣīban mina l-kitābi yudʿawna ilā kitābi llāhi li-yaḥkuma baynahum thumma yatawallā farīqun minhum wa-hum muʿriḍūn
versets.trad: Have you not seen how those who were given a portion of the Scripture were summoned to the Book of God, that it might judge between them? Then a party of them turned away and became opposers.
themes: the summons to the Book refused
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī addresses to the Prophet a consolation upon the trial of the call. We tried you by the summoning of those whom Our pre-eternal knowledge knew would not respond; be patient, then, in what is commanded you concerning them, and know the ugliness of their states: they are people of refusal, because they lost from Us the beauty of manifestation, through the priority of the Will.

mots.racine: أ ت ي / ن ص ب / ك ت ب
mots.sens: they were given a portion of the Scripture
mots.def: the People of the Book, holders of a portion of the revelation.

mots.racine: د ع و
mots.sens: they are summoned to the Book of God
mots.def: the invitation to judge according to the Scripture.

mots.racine: و ل ي / ف ر ق
mots.sens: a party of them turns away
mots.def: the withdrawal of a portion of them.

mots.racine: ع ر ض
mots.def: opposers who shy from the call.

versets.ar: ذَٰلِكَ بِأَنَّهُمْ قَالُوا لَن تَمَسَّنَا النَّارُ إِلَّا أَيَّامًا مَّعْدُودَاتٍ وَغَرَّهُمْ فِي دِينِهِم مَّا كَانُوا يَفْتَرُونَ
versets.tr: dhālika bi-annahum qālū lan tamassanā n-nāru illā ayyāman maʿdūdātin wa-gharrahum fī dīnihim mā kānū yaftarūn
versets.trad: That is because they said: “The Fire will touch us only for a number of counted days!” They let themselves be deluded in their religion by all that they invented.
themes: the illusion of a brief chastisement
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī unveils the ruse of the “granted respite” (istidrāj). We chastised them in this world by the reprieve, until they judged themselves saved and the torment eased; but soon they will know the redoubling of the trial. They think they hold something — whereas in truth they are the liars.

mots.racine: م س س / ن و ر
mots.sens: the Fire will not touch us
mots.def: their illusory claim to be spared.

mots.racine: ي و م / ع د د
mots.def: the brief span they imagine for the chastisement.

mots.racine: غ ر ر / د ي ن
mots.def: the blindness born of their own inventions.

mots.racine: ف ر ي
mots.def: the lying fabrications that led them astray.

versets.ar: فَكَيْفَ إِذَا جَمَعْنَاهُمْ لِيَوْمٍ لَّا رَيْبَ فِيهِ وَوُفِّيَتْ كُلُّ نَفْسٍ مَّا كَسَبَتْ وَهُمْ لَا يُظْلَمُونَ
versets.tr: fa-kayfa idhā jamaʿnāhum li-yawmin lā rayba fīhi wa-wuffiyat kullu nafsin mā kasabat wa-hum lā yuẓlamūn
versets.trad: How will it be when We gather them on a Day in which there is no doubt, when every soul shall be repaid in full for what it has earned, and none shall be wronged?
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī reads in this “how?” a seizing of the soul. It is a word of astonishment before the immensity of the affair and the solemnity of the event: the stupor of the minds, the dread of the secrets, the cutting-off of every recourse, the wrenching of hearts out of their niches. And he distinguishes the two resurrections: the resurrection of the deniers is on the Day of gathering; the resurrection of the beloved ones is at every instant.

mots.racine: ج م ع
mots.def: the universal gathering of the Day.

mots.racine: و ف ي / ك س ب
mots.sens: every soul is repaid in full for its earning
mots.def: exact recompense according to the deeds.

mots.racine: ظ ل م
mots.def: the absence of any injustice in the judgement.

versets.ar: قُلِ اللَّهُمَّ مَالِكَ الْمُلْكِ تُؤْتِي الْمُلْكَ مَن تَشَاءُ وَتَنزِعُ الْمُلْكَ مِمَّن تَشَاءُ وَتُعِزُّ مَن تَشَاءُ وَتُذِلُّ مَن تَشَاءُ بِيَدِكَ الْخَيْرُ إِنَّكَ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ
versets.tr: quli llāhumma mālika l-mulki tuʾtī l-mulka man tashāʾu wa-tanziʿu l-mulka mimman tashāʾu wa-tuʿizzu man tashāʾu wa-tudhillu man tashāʾu bi-yadika l-khayru innaka ʿalā kulli shayʾin qadīr
versets.trad: Say: “O God, Possessor of the Kingdom! You give the Kingdom to whom You will and You strip the Kingdom from whom You will. You exalt whom You will and You abase whom You will. In Your hand is the good. You are, in truth, powerful over all things.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī makes of this verse a teaching of praise itself. It is God teaching how He is to be praised: describe Me by what I deserve of majesty. Then he interiorizes the giving and the stripping of the kingdom: the true kingship of the created lies in its humility before the Real; men become beautiful in their abasement before Him, glorious in their effacement in Him, subsisting in their extinction through Him. He then declines the inner exaltation and abasement: You exalt whom You will by setting him at peace through You, and You abase whom You will by startling him away from You; You exalt whom You will by occupying him with You, and You abase whom You will by occupying him away from You. And on the verse's silence regarding evil: He did not mention evil, out of courtesy of speech, for the good augury of the beautiful, out of aversion to evoking the worse.

mots.racine: م ل ك
mots.sens: O God, Possessor of the Kingdom
mots.def: the absolute Master of all kingship, without partner.

mots.racine: أ ت ي / م ل ك
mots.sens: You give the kingdom to whom You will

mots.racine: ع ز ز
ي د ي / خ ي ر
mots.sens: in Your hand is the good
mots.def: the good alone mentioned, out of courtesy (Qushayrī).

versets.ar: تُولِجُ اللَّيْلَ فِي النَّهَارِ وَتُولِجُ النَّهَارَ فِي اللَّيْلِ وَتُخْرِجُ الْحَيَّ مِنَ الْمَيِّتِ وَتُخْرِجُ الْمَيِّتَ مِنَ الْحَيِّ وَتَرْزُقُ مَن تَشَاءُ بِغَيْرِ حِسَابٍ
versets.tr: tūliju l-layla fī n-nahāri wa-tūliju n-nahāra fī l-layli wa-tukhriju l-ḥayya mina l-mayyiti wa-tukhriju l-mayyita mina l-ḥayyi wa-tarzuqu man tashāʾu bi-ghayri ḥisāb
versets.trad: You cause the night to enter into the day and You cause the day to enter into the night. You bring forth the living from the dead and You bring forth the dead from the living. You provide for whom You will without measure.”
themes: the interweaving of day and night
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī transposes the cosmic alternations into the life of the heart. You cause the night to enter into the day until the reign of the light of oneness prevails, and nothing remains of the darkness of the soul; and You cause the day to enter into the night as though the suns of hearts were eclipsed, as though the night endured and the dawn vanished. Likewise for life and death: You bring forth the living from the dead as though the torpor had never been, as though the covenant of union returned young, and the wood of hearts grew tender and fresh again.

mots.racine: و ل ج / ل ي ل / ن ه ر
mots.sens: You cause the night to enter the day
mots.def: the interlacing of times, image of the reign of light.

mots.racine: خ ر ج / ح ي ي / م و ت
mots.sens: You bring forth the living from the dead
mots.def: life drawn from death, image of the heart revived.

mots.racine: ر ز ق / ح س ب
mots.sens: You provide for whom You will without measure

versets.ar: لَّا يَتَّخِذِ الْمُؤْمِنُونَ الْكَافِرِينَ أَوْلِيَاءَ مِن دُونِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ وَمَن يَفْعَلْ ذَٰلِكَ فَلَيْسَ مِنَ اللَّهِ فِي شَيْءٍ إِلَّا أَن تَتَّقُوا مِنْهُمْ تُقَاةً وَيُحَذِّرُكُمُ اللَّهُ نَفْسَهُ وَإِلَى اللَّهِ الْمَصِيرُ
versets.tr: lā yattakhidhi l-muʾminūna l-kāfirīna awliyāʾa min dūni l-muʾminīna wa-man yafʿal dhālika fa-laysa mina llāhi fī shayʾin illā an tattaqū minhum tuqātan wa-yuḥadhdhirukumu llāhu nafsahu wa-ilā llāhi l-maṣīr
versets.trad: Let not the believers take the deniers as protecting friends in preference to the believers. Whoever does so has nothing to hope from God, unless you have to guard yourselves against them as a precaution. God warns you of Himself, and to God is the final return.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī makes of friendship and enmity in God a reality of faith, and points to the most intimate enemy: among the realities of faith are alliance in God and enmity in God; and the first you must treat as an outcast, the first to turn your back on before the deniers, is your own soul — for it is steeped in pretension, saying: I, mine, by me. As for “God warns you of Himself,” he sees in it a word addressed to the elect: it is the warning to the knowers; for the hidden stratagems lie in wait even for the greatest — and whoever imagines himself nearest to Him is in truth the farthest.

mots.racine: أ خ ذ / و ل ي
mots.sens: let not the believers take the deniers as allies
mots.def: the prohibition of preferential alliance with the deniers.

mots.racine: و ق ي
mots.sens: to guard yourselves against them as a precaution
mots.def: the exception: lawful prudence in case of danger.

mots.racine: ح ذ ر / ن ف س
mots.def: a warning addressed to the knowers (Qushayrī).

mots.racine: ص ي ر
mots.def: the final return of all things to God.

versets.ar: قُلْ إِن تُخْفُوا مَا فِي صُدُورِكُمْ أَوْ تُبْدُوهُ يَعْلَمْهُ اللَّهُ وَيَعْلَمُ مَا فِي السَّمَاوَاتِ وَمَا فِي الْأَرْضِ وَاللَّهُ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ
versets.tr: qul in tukhfū mā fī ṣudūrikum aw tubdūhu yaʿlamhu llāhu wa-yaʿlamu mā fī s-samāwāti wa-mā fī l-arḍi wa-llāhu ʿalā kulli shayʾin qadīr
versets.trad: Say: “Whether you conceal what is in your breasts or reveal it, God knows it. He knows what is in the heavens and what is on the earth. God is powerful over all things.”
themes: the secret of the breasts known
the consolation of the near one
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī turns the total knowledge into a motive for confidence rather than fear. No known thing escapes His knowing; so do not be troubled by a trial that afflicts you, for soon succour and answer will come to you, soon the trial and the pain will dissipate, and reinforcement and sufficiency will hasten toward you.

mots.racine: خ ف ي / ص د ر
mots.sens: you conceal what is in your breasts

mots.racine: ب د و
mots.def: the outward expression of the feeling.

mots.racine: ع ل م
mots.def: the divine knowledge embraces the hidden and the manifest.

versets.ar: يَوْمَ تَجِدُ كُلُّ نَفْسٍ مَّا عَمِلَتْ مِنْ خَيْرٍ مُّحْضَرًا وَمَا عَمِلَتْ مِن سُوءٍ تَوَدُّ لَوْ أَنَّ بَيْنَهَا وَبَيْنَهُ أَمَدًا بَعِيدًا وَيُحَذِّرُكُمُ اللَّهُ نَفْسَهُ وَاللَّهُ رَؤُوفٌ بِالْعِبَادِ
versets.tr: yawma tajidu kullu nafsin mā ʿamilat min khayrin muḥḍaran wa-mā ʿamilat min sūʾin tawaddu law anna baynahā wa-baynahu amadan baʿīdan wa-yuḥadhdhirukumu llāhu nafsahu wa-llāhu raʾūfun bi-l-ʿibād
versets.trad: On the Day when every soul will be confronted with the good it has done and the evil it has done, it will wish that a long distance lay between itself and the evil it has committed. God warns you of Himself; and God is compassionate toward His servants.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī grasps the twofold movement of the verse, which first frightens then consoles. “God warns you of Himself” is addressed to the knowers; “God is compassionate toward His servants” to the novices: to the one, rigour and constraint; to the other, alleviation and ease. And he says further: He extinguished them by “God warns you of Himself,” then revived them and gave them subsistence by “God is compassionate” — such is His custom: He gives hope to the heart in the very midst of what frightens it.

mots.racine: ع م ل / خ ي ر / ح ض ر
mots.sens: the good it has done, brought present
mots.def: the good deeds rendered present on the Day of reckoning.

mots.ar: تَوَدُّ لَوْ أَنَّ بَيْنَهَا وَبَيْنَهُ أَمَدًا بَعِيدًا
mots.tr: tawaddu law anna baynahā wa-baynahu amadan baʿīdan
mots.racine: و د د / أ م د
mots.sens: it would wish for a great distance
mots.def: the soul's wish to be far removed from the evil committed.

mots.racine: ر أ ف / ع ب د
mots.def: al-Raʾūf: God's tenderness for the novices (Qushayrī).

versets.ar: قُلْ إِن كُنتُمْ تُحِبُّونَ اللَّهَ فَاتَّبِعُونِي يُحْبِبْكُمُ اللَّهُ وَيَغْفِرْ لَكُمْ ذُنُوبَكُمْ وَاللَّهُ غَفُورٌ رَّحِيمٌ
versets.tr: qul in kuntum tuḥibbūna llāha fa-ttabiʿūnī yuḥbibkumu llāhu wa-yaghfir lakum dhunūbakum wa-llāhu ghafūrun raḥīm
versets.trad: Say: “If you love God, follow me, and God will love you and forgive you your sins. God is forgiving, merciful.”
versets.commentaire: This verse, the “verse of love,” is for Qushayrī the heart of the way. He distinguishes the two loves: “you love God” belongs to dispersion, “God loves you” to union; yours is mingled with motive, His is without any cause — it is the very reality of the joining. He then defines the servant's love: a subtle state he finds within himself, which carries him to embrace the divine command in contentment and not in repugnance, and makes him prefer God to every thing and every being. And he lays down the cardinal condition: the condition of love is that no portion of self be mingled with it; whoever is not wholly extinguished from his own portions has not even a gleam of love.

mots.racine: ح ب ب
mots.def: the servant's love for God, mingled with motive.

mots.racine: ت ب ع
mots.def: the following of the Prophet as proof of love.

mots.racine: ح ب ب
mots.def: God's love for the servant, without cause.

mots.racine: غ ف ر / ر ح م
mots.def: two Names of the divine mercy.

versets.ar: قُلْ أَطِيعُوا اللَّهَ وَالرَّسُولَ فَإِن تَوَلَّوْا فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ لَا يُحِبُّ الْكَافِرِينَ
versets.tr: qul aṭīʿū llāha wa-r-rasūla fa-in tawallaw fa-inna llāha lā yuḥibbu l-kāfirīn
versets.trad: Say: “Obey God and the Messenger. But if you turn away, know that God does not love the deniers.”
themes: obedience to God and the Messenger
love persisting for the sinful believer
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī notes a fine point of vocabulary full of hope. He commanded them obedience, then said: “if they turn away” — that is, if they fail in obedience by disobeying. Yet He does not then say “the disobedient”; He says “the deniers.” And the implicit sense of the discourse is that He loves the believers, even were they sinners.

mots.racine: ط و ع / ر س ل
mots.def: the joint command to obey God and His messenger.

mots.racine: و ل ي
mots.def: the failure in obedience through disobedience.

mots.racine: ح ب ب / ك ف ر
mots.def: “deniers” and not “sinners”: the faulty believer remains beloved (Qushayrī).

versets.ar: إِنَّ اللَّهَ اصْطَفَىٰ آدَمَ وَنُوحًا وَآلَ إِبْرَاهِيمَ وَآلَ عِمْرَانَ عَلَى الْعَالَمِينَ
versets.tr: inna llāha ṣṭafā ādama wa-nūḥan wa-āla ibrāhīma wa-āla ʿimrāna ʿalā l-ʿālamīn
versets.trad: Truly, God chose Adam, Noah, the family of Abraham, and the family of ʿImrān above all the worlds.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī founds election upon pure favour, never upon blood. Adam and his descendants share one and the same clay; election rests only on the choice that precedes it, not on lineage or kinship. The family is honoured because God chose it from all eternity, not because it would possess some merit by birth.

mots.racine: ص ف و
mots.def: the pre-eternal election, pure favour of God.

mots.racine: أ و ل
mots.sens: the family of Abraham and the family of ʿImrān

mots.racine: ع ل م
mots.def: preferred to all the creatures of their time.

versets.ar: ذُرِّيَّةً بَعْضُهَا مِن بَعْضٍ وَاللَّهُ سَمِيعٌ عَلِيمٌ
versets.tr: dhurriyyatan baʿḍuhā min baʿḍin wa-llāhu samīʿun ʿalīm
versets.trad: They proceed one from another. God is the All-Hearing, the All-Knowing.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī ties this progeny to the sole logic of election, prolonging the preceding verse: what unites this lineage is not flesh but the divine choice, and it is through that prior choice that grace flows “one from another.” God, who “hears all” and “knows all,” apportions His favour according to His knowledge alone, not according to the titles of blood.

mots.racine: ذ ر ر
mots.sens: a progeny proceeding one from another
mots.def: a lineage united by election more than by blood.

mots.racine: س م ع / ع ل م
mots.def: two Names: total hearing and knowledge.

versets.ar: إِذْ قَالَتِ امْرَأَتُ عِمْرَانَ رَبِّ إِنِّي نَذَرْتُ لَكَ مَا فِي بَطْنِي مُحَرَّرًا فَتَقَبَّلْ مِنِّي إِنَّكَ أَنتَ السَّمِيعُ الْعَلِيمُ
versets.tr: idh qālati mraʾatu ʿimrāna rabbi innī nadhartu laka mā fī baṭnī muḥarraran fa-taqabbal minnī innaka anta s-samīʿu l-ʿalīm
versets.trad: The wife of ʿImrān said: “My Lord! I have consecrated to You what is in my womb; accept it from me. You are, in truth, the All-Hearing, the All-Knowing.”
themes: the one set free for God
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī gives the word “consecrated” (muḥarrar) its full spiritual sense. The muḥarrar is the one who is in servitude to no creature; the Real has freed him, in His prior decree, from the slavery of every attachment, in all its aspects and all its states. The mother of ʿImrān vows to God not merely a child, but a whole being set free for Him.

mots.racine: ن ذ ر / ب ط ن
mots.sens: I have vowed to You what is in my womb
mots.def: the vow to consecrate the child to come.

mots.racine: ح ر ر
mots.def: freed from the service of every creature, devoted to God alone (Qushayrī).

mots.racine: ق ب ل
mots.def: the plea for acceptance of the offering.

versets.ar: فَلَمَّا وَضَعَتْهَا قَالَتْ رَبِّ إِنِّي وَضَعْتُهَا أُنثَىٰ وَاللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ بِمَا وَضَعَتْ وَلَيْسَ الذَّكَرُ كَالْأُنثَىٰ وَإِنِّي سَمَّيْتُهَا مَرْيَمَ وَإِنِّي أُعِيذُهَا بِكَ وَذُرِّيَّتَهَا مِنَ الشَّيْطَانِ الرَّجِيمِ
versets.tr: fa-lammā waḍaʿathā qālat rabbi innī waḍaʿtuhā unthā wa-llāhu aʿlamu bimā waḍaʿat wa-laysa dh-dhakaru ka-l-unthā wa-innī sammaytuhā maryama wa-innī uʿīdhuhā bika wa-dhurriyyatahā mina sh-shayṭāni r-rajīm
versets.trad: When she had given birth to her, she said: “My Lord! I have given birth to a female.” But God knew best what she had brought forth — the male is not like the female! “I have named her Mary, and I place her under Your protection, her and her offspring, against Satan the accursed.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī gathers up the mother's confusion and turns it into grace. When the mother of Mary had made her vow and given birth to a girl, she was perplexed, for a girl could not, she believed, become “consecrated”; but God said: “God knows best what she brought forth” — and, by my life, the male is not like the female in outward appearance, yet when the Real accepts her, every wonder rises from her. And of her taking refuge in God: she took refuge with God so that Satan should have no part in her story, in the slightest degree, that the design of hearts might be fully accomplished.

mots.racine: و ض ع / أ ن ث
mots.sens: she gave birth to her, a female
mots.def: the unexpected birth of a girl.

mots.racine: ذ ك ر / أ ن ث
mots.sens: the male is not like the female
mots.def: an outward distinction that the divine acceptance transcends.

mots.racine: س م و
mots.def: the name given to the child.

mots.racine: ع و ذ
mots.sens: I place her under Your protection
mots.def: taking refuge in God against Satan.

versets.ar: فَتَقَبَّلَهَا رَبُّهَا بِقَبُولٍ حَسَنٍ وَأَنبَتَهَا نَبَاتًا حَسَنًا وَكَفَّلَهَا زَكَرِيَّا كُلَّمَا دَخَلَ عَلَيْهَا زَكَرِيَّا الْمِحْرَابَ وَجَدَ عِندَهَا رِزْقًا قَالَ يَا مَرْيَمُ أَنَّىٰ لَكِ هَٰذَا قَالَتْ هُوَ مِنْ عِندِ اللَّهِ إِنَّ اللَّهَ يَرْزُقُ مَن يَشَاءُ بِغَيْرِ حِسَابٍ
versets.tr: fa-taqabbalahā rabbuhā bi-qabūlin ḥasanin wa-anbatahā nabātan ḥasanan wa-kaffalahā zakariyyā kullamā dakhala ʿalayhā zakariyyā l-miḥrāba wajada ʿindahā rizqan qāla yā maryamu annā laki hādhā qālat huwa min ʿindi llāhi inna llāha yarzuqu man yashāʾu bi-ghayri ḥisāb
versets.trad: Her Lord accepted her with a fair acceptance, made her grow with a fair growth, and entrusted her to Zachariah. Whenever Zachariah went to her in the sanctuary, he found provision beside her, and he would ask: “O Mary! Whence comes this to you?” She would answer: “It is from God! God provides for whom He wills without reckoning.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī unfolds the senses of the “fair acceptance.” He showered her beyond what her mother had hoped; He reserved her for His service and took charge of her as He takes charge of His friends. And the provision found in the sanctuary is a sign: among the marks of the fair acceptance, God did not hand over her whole affair to Zachariah — that the workers might know that God does not lay the charge of His friends upon another, and that whoever serves a friend of God lives by the very relief of that friend. As for “God provides for whom He wills without reckoning”: it is the very evidence of pure tawḥīd — His sustenance and His bounty proceed from His will alone, neither caused by works nor merited by acts of worship.

mots.racine: ق ب ل / ح س ن
mots.sens: her Lord accepted her with a fair acceptance
mots.def: the divine acceptance that reserves her for His service.

mots.racine: ن ب ت / ح س ن
mots.sens: He made her grow with a fair growth

mots.racine: ك ف ل
mots.def: the guardianship of Mary committed to the prophet Zachariah.

mots.racine: و ج د / ر ز ق
mots.def: the miraculous sustenance, sign of acceptance.

mots.racine: ر ز ق / ح س ب
mots.sens: He provides for whom He wills without reckoning
mots.def: the sustenance granted by pure will, without cause (Qushayrī).

versets.ar: هُنَالِكَ دَعَا زَكَرِيَّا رَبَّهُ قَالَ رَبِّ هَبْ لِي مِن لَّدُنكَ ذُرِّيَّةً طَيِّبَةً إِنَّكَ سَمِيعُ الدُّعَاءِ
versets.tr: hunālika daʿā zakariyyā rabbahu qāla rabbi hab lī min ladunka dhurriyyatan ṭayyibatan innaka samīʿu d-duʿāʾ
versets.trad: There and then Zachariah implored his Lord, saying: “My Lord! Grant me, from Yourself, a goodly offspring. You are, in truth, the One who hears prayer.”
themes: desire for the right of God
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī shows that the sight of grace draws grace. When he saw the honour God bestowed on Mary, his certitude grew upon his certitude, his hope upon his hope, and he asked for a son despite his great age — and that this prayer was answered was a breaking of the customary order. And on the motive of his request: he asked for a son to have a support in obedience and an heir to his prophethood, who would stand firm in the right of God; this is why he merited the answer — for the request, when it is for the right of the Real and not for the soul's portion, knows no refusal.

mots.racine: د ع و
mots.def: the supplication aroused by the sight of grace.

mots.ar: هَبْ لِي مِن لَّدُنكَ ذُرِّيَّةً طَيِّبَةً
mots.tr: hab lī min ladunka dhurriyyatan ṭayyiba
mots.racine: و ه ب / ذ ر ر / ط ي ب
mots.def: the request for a goodly heir, for the right of God.

mots.racine: س م ع / د ع و

versets.ar: فَنَادَتْهُ الْمَلَائِكَةُ وَهُوَ قَائِمٌ يُصَلِّي فِي الْمِحْرَابِ أَنَّ اللَّهَ يُبَشِّرُكَ بِيَحْيَىٰ مُصَدِّقًا بِكَلِمَةٍ مِّنَ اللَّهِ وَسَيِّدًا وَحَصُورًا وَنَبِيًّا مِّنَ الصَّالِحِينَ
versets.tr: fa-nādathu l-malāʾikatu wa-huwa qāʾimun yuṣallī fī l-miḥrābi anna llāha yubashshiruka bi-yaḥyā muṣaddiqan bi-kalimatin mina llāhi wa-sayyidan wa-ḥaṣūran wa-nabiyyan mina ṣ-ṣāliḥīn
versets.trad: As he stood praying in the sanctuary, the angels called to him: “God gives you the good tidings of [the birth of] John (Yaḥyā), who will confirm a Word from God, a lord, a chaste one, a prophet among the righteous.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī makes of the answer a lesson in perseverance. When he had prayed and held fast at the door, the answer came to him; therein is an indication: whoever has a need with kings must remain at the door until the hour of the response. He then unveils the names of John: he was named Yaḥyā — “he lives” — for the life of his heart through God; “lord” (sayyid): one who is in servitude to no creature, freed from the slavery of his passion; “chaste” (ḥaṣūr): preserved from the appetites, exempt from the bonds of human nature while yet remaining human.

mots.racine: ن د و / ل أ ك
mots.def: the angelic annunciation of the good tidings.

mots.racine: ب ش ر / ح ي ي
mots.def: Yaḥyā: “he lives,” through the life of the heart in God (Qushayrī).

mots.racine: ص د ق / ك ل م
mots.def: attesting to Jesus, “the Word” issuing from God.

mots.racine: س و د / ح ص ر
mots.sens: a lord and a chaste one
mots.def: sayyid: freed from all servitude; ḥaṣūr: preserved from the appetites.

mots.racine: ن ب أ / ص ل ح
mots.def: worthy to attain the rank of the righteous.

versets.ar: قَالَ رَبِّ أَنَّىٰ يَكُونُ لِي غُلَامٌ وَقَدْ بَلَغَنِيَ الْكِبَرُ وَامْرَأَتِي عَاقِرٌ قَالَ كَذَٰلِكَ اللَّهُ يَفْعَلُ مَا يَشَاءُ
versets.tr: qāla rabbi annā yakūnu lī ghulāmun wa-qad balaghaniya l-kibaru wa-mraʾatī ʿāqirun qāla kadhālika llāhu yafʿalu mā yashāʾ
versets.trad: Zachariah said: “My Lord! How shall I have a son? Old age has overtaken me, and my wife is barren.” It was answered: “So it shall be; God does what He wills.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī opens several senses to this “how?”. It is said that between his request and the answer a long time elapsed, whence his “how can this be?”; and one may understand: by what merit of my own would this answer come to me, save by Your favour? The reply “So it shall be” cuts short all calculation: God does what He wills — and it is from this very wife, despite age and barrenness, that the child will come, because the two of you endured together the solitude of isolation; thus the good tidings of the child shall be given to you both.

mots.racine: ك و ن / غ ل م
mots.sens: how shall I have a son
mots.def: astonishment before the promise beyond nature.

mots.racine: ب ل غ / ك ب ر
م ر أ / ع ق ر
mots.def: the barrenness of the wife, a double apparent impossibility.

mots.racine: ف ع ل / ش ي أ
mots.sens: so it shall be; God does what He wills
mots.def: the sovereign freedom of the divine acting.

versets.ar: قَالَ رَبِّ اجْعَل لِّي آيَةً ۖ قَالَ آيَتُكَ أَلَّا تُكَلِّمَ النَّاسَ ثَلَاثَةَ أَيَّامٍ إِلَّا رَمْزًا ۗ وَاذْكُر رَّبَّكَ كَثِيرًا وَسَبِّحْ بِالْعَشِيِّ وَالْإِبْكَارِ
versets.tr: qāla rabbi-jʿal lī āyatan qāla āyatuka allā tukallima n-nāsa thalāthata ayyāmin illā ramzan wa-dhkur rabbaka kathīran wa-sabbiḥ bi-l-ʿashiyyi wa-l-ibkāri
versets.trad: Zachariah said: "My Lord, grant me a sign!" He was answered: "Your sign shall be that for three days you will speak to men only by gestures. Remember your Lord abundantly; glorify Him at dusk and at dawn."
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī notes that Zachariah did not ask for the sign out of any doubt that his prayer would be granted, but in order to know the precise moment when his supplication was received. And the sign of his nearness was wondrous: his tongue withheld from creatures, yet loosed toward God in glorification. As though the Real were saying: "Do not imagine yourself barred from addressing Me, for I never forbid My intimates the confidence of inward intimacy." The remembrance is then made "with heart and tongue, at all times," and the glorification "in unceasing prayer."

mots.racine: أ ي ي
ر م ز
س ب ح / ع ش و / ب ك ر
mots.sens: glorify at evening and at dawn
mots.def: ʿashiyy: dusk; ibkār: the day's beginning.

versets.ar: وَإِذْ قَالَتِ الْمَلَائِكَةُ يَا مَرْيَمُ إِنَّ اللَّهَ اصْطَفَاكِ وَطَهَّرَكِ وَاصْطَفَاكِ عَلَىٰ نِسَاءِ الْعَالَمِينَ
versets.tr: wa-idh qālati l-malāʾikatu yā maryamu inna llāha-ṣṭafāki wa-ṭahharaki wa-ṣṭafāki ʿalā nisāʾi l-ʿālamīna
versets.trad: The angels said: "O Mary! In truth, God has chosen you, He has purified you; He has chosen you above all the women of the universe.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī unfolds the twofold choosing: the first election honored her by rank and by the loftiness of her state; the second distinguished her in that she bore Jesus without a father — no woman ever resembled her, nor shall resemble her until the Day. The purification is the beautiful preservation (ʿiṣma) that set her apart from every defilement. Chosen "above the women of the worlds in her time," she was singled out beyond her like and her equal.

mots.racine: ص ف و
mots.def: iṣṭafā: to elect, to choose with predilection.

mots.racine: ط ه ر
ن س و / ع ل م
mots.sens: above the women of the worlds

versets.ar: يَا مَرْيَمُ اقْنُتِي لِرَبِّكِ وَاسْجُدِي وَارْكَعِي مَعَ الرَّاكِعِينَ
versets.tr: yā maryamu-qnutī li-rabbiki wa-sjudī wa-rkaʿī maʿa r-rākiʿīna
versets.trad: O Mary! Be devoted to your Lord; prostrate yourself and bow with those who bow."
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī hears in it a summons to abide: hold fast to the carpet of worship, persevere in obedience, do not slacken in continual service. And he draws out the pearl: just as the Real singled you out by your rank, be the unique one of your age in His worship. Election calls for a response to match it: that the grace received become service offered.

mots.racine: ق ن ت
س ج د
ر ك ع

versets.ar: ذَٰلِكَ مِنْ أَنبَاءِ الْغَيْبِ نُوحِيهِ إِلَيْكَ ۚ وَمَا كُنتَ لَدَيْهِمْ إِذْ يُلْقُونَ أَقْلَامَهُمْ أَيُّهُمْ يَكْفُلُ مَرْيَمَ وَمَا كُنتَ لَدَيْهِمْ إِذْ يَخْتَصِمُونَ
versets.tr: dhālika min anbāʾi l-ghaybi nūḥīhi ilayka wa-mā kunta ladayhim idh yulqūna aqlāmahum ayyuhum yakfulu maryama wa-mā kunta ladayhim idh yakhtaṣimūna
versets.trad: These are tidings of the Unseen that We reveal to you [O Muhammad]. You were not among them when they cast their reeds to decide which of them should have charge of Mary. Nor were you among them when they fell into dispute [over this].
versets.commentaire: These accounts, says Qushayrī, reach you by a way higher than the witnessing of the eyes: We have made their meanings known to you by addressing the Word to you; Our speech that conveys them to you is nobler, more august, and more complete than had you been their eyewitness. Unveiling through the Word surpasses bodily presence at the events.

mots.racine: ن ب أ / غ ي ب
mots.def: nabaʾ: weighty tiding; ghayb: the unseen.

mots.racine: ل ق ي / ق ل م
ك ف ل

versets.ar: إِذْ قَالَتِ الْمَلَائِكَةُ يَا مَرْيَمُ إِنَّ اللَّهَ يُبَشِّرُكِ بِكَلِمَةٍ مِّنْهُ اسْمُهُ الْمَسِيحُ عِيسَى ابْنُ مَرْيَمَ وَجِيهًا فِي الدُّنْيَا وَالْآخِرَةِ وَمِنَ الْمُقَرَّبِينَ
versets.tr: idh qālati l-malāʾikatu yā maryamu inna llāha yubashshiruki bi-kalimatin minhu-smuhu l-masīḥu ʿīsā-bnu maryama wajīhan fī d-dunyā wa-l-ākhirati wa-mina l-muqarrabīna
versets.trad: The angels said: "O Mary! God gives you the glad tidings of a Word from Him. His name is the Messiah, Jesus, son of Mary, illustrious in this world and in the Hereafter. He is among those brought near to God.
versets.commentaire: The tidings, observes Qushayrī, concern no worldly portion nor future enjoyments, but the greatness of the sign and the fact that he would be a prophet of God confirmed by miracle. Mary is informed that whoever is seized by the sovereignty of Power receives, of the marvels of that Power, what none had ever known before. The Word arises beyond all causes, as a seal of the divine unheard-of.

mots.racine: ك ل م
م س ح
mots.sens: the Messiah, Jesus son of Mary

mots.racine: و ج ه
mots.def: wajīh: eminent, held in high regard.

mots.racine: ق ر ب

versets.ar: وَيُكَلِّمُ النَّاسَ فِي الْمَهْدِ وَكَهْلًا وَمِنَ الصَّالِحِينَ
versets.tr: wa-yukallimu n-nāsa fī l-mahdi wa-kahlan wa-mina ṣ-ṣāliḥīna
versets.trad: He shall speak to men from the cradle and in his maturity, and he shall be among the righteous."
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī relates that the glad tidings came by degrees: this son would live to speak to men as a child and then as a man of full age, and the cunning of his enemies would have no hold upon him. And he sees in it a strengthening of Mary's heart: if her own tongue was not to proclaim her innocence, God would make Jesus speak to attest her truthfulness and her nobility. The miracle outstrips the slander.

mots.racine: م ه د
ك ه ل
mots.sens: and a man of mature age
mots.def: kahl: an adult of ripe age.

mots.racine: ص ل ح

versets.ar: قَالَتْ رَبِّ أَنَّىٰ يَكُونُ لِي وَلَدٌ وَلَمْ يَمْسَسْنِي بَشَرٌ ۖ قَالَ كَذَٰلِكِ اللَّهُ يَخْلُقُ مَا يَشَاءُ ۚ إِذَا قَضَىٰ أَمْرًا فَإِنَّمَا يَقُولُ لَهُ كُن فَيَكُونُ
versets.tr: qālat rabbi annā yakūnu lī waladun wa-lam yamsasnī basharun qāla kadhāliki llāhu yakhluqu mā yashāʾu idhā qaḍā amran fa-innamā yaqūlu lahu kun fa-yakūnu
versets.trad: She said: "My Lord! How shall I have a son when no man has touched me?" He answered: "Thus shall it be. God creates what He wills; when He decrees a thing, He need only say to it: 'Be,' and it is."
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī answers by the plain evidence of prodigies already seen: just as you have seen arise in your own provision things that broke with custom, so shall We break with custom in creating a child without any mortal having touched you. When He "decrees a matter" — that is, wills to set His decree in motion — it costs Him nothing either to begin or to bring forth: the "Be" knows no obstacle.

mots.racine: و ل د
mots.sens: how shall I have a son

mots.racine: م س س / ب ش ر
mots.def: massa: to touch; bashar: a human being.

mots.racine: ك و ن

versets.ar: وَيُعَلِّمُهُ الْكِتَابَ وَالْحِكْمَةَ وَالتَّوْرَاةَ وَالْإِنجِيلَ
versets.tr: wa-yuʿallimuhu l-kitāba wa-l-ḥikmata wa-t-tawrāta wa-l-injīla
versets.trad: God will teach him the Book, Wisdom, the Torah, and the Gospel.
versets.commentaire: God Himself takes charge of the instruction of Jesus: not a knowledge acquired from men, but an infused science. Qushayrī ties this teaching to the manifest signs and resplendent proofs that Jesus would bear; the Book and Wisdom precede the miracle and ground it. He whom God teaches need not beg knowledge from creatures.

mots.racine: ك ت ب / ح ك م

versets.ar: وَرَسُولًا إِلَىٰ بَنِي إِسْرَائِيلَ أَنِّي قَدْ جِئْتُكُم بِآيَةٍ مِّن رَّبِّكُمْ ۖ أَنِّي أَخْلُقُ لَكُم مِّنَ الطِّينِ كَهَيْئَةِ الطَّيْرِ فَأَنفُخُ فِيهِ فَيَكُونُ طَيْرًا بِإِذْنِ اللَّهِ ۖ وَأُبْرِئُ الْأَكْمَهَ وَالْأَبْرَصَ وَأُحْيِي الْمَوْتَىٰ بِإِذْنِ اللَّهِ ۖ وَأُنَبِّئُكُم بِمَا تَأْكُلُونَ وَمَا تَدَّخِرُونَ فِي بُيُوتِكُمْ ۚ إِنَّ فِي ذَٰلِكَ لَآيَةً لَّكُمْ إِن كُنتُم مُّؤْمِنِينَ
versets.tr: wa-rasūlan ilā banī isrāʾīla annī qad jiʾtukum bi-āyatin min rabbikum annī akhluqu lakum mina ṭ-ṭīni ka-hayʾati ṭ-ṭayri fa-anfukhu fīhi fa-yakūnu ṭayran bi-idhni llāhi wa-ubriʾu l-akmaha wa-l-abraṣa wa-uḥyī l-mawtā bi-idhni llāhi wa-unabbiʾukum bi-mā taʾkulūna wa-mā taddakhirūna fī buyūtikum inna fī dhālika la-āyatan lakum in kuntum muʾminīna
versets.trad: He shall be sent to the children of Israel [and shall say to them]: "I have come to you with a sign from your Lord: for you I fashion out of clay the likeness of a bird, I breathe into it, and behold, it becomes a bird by God's leave. I heal the blind from birth and the leper, I raise the dead, by God's leave, and I tell you what you eat and what you store away in your houses. In all this there is surely a sign for you, if you are believers.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī enumerates his manifest signs and his resplendent, overwhelming proofs: bringing the dead back to life, healing the man born blind and the leper, disclosing what they concealed, among many other prodigies. But the inserted phrase "by God's leave," repeated, strips the prophet of every pretension: the marvels are not from him, they pass through him. He also confirms the earlier Laws while coming to abrogate certain of their provisions.

mots.racine: ه ي أ / ط ي ر
أ ذ ن
ك م ه / ب ر ص
mots.sens: the man born blind and the leper
mots.def: akmah: blind from birth; abraṣ: leper.

mots.racine: ح ي ي / م و ت
mots.def: aḥyā: to bring back to life.

versets.ar: وَمُصَدِّقًا لِّمَا بَيْنَ يَدَيَّ مِنَ التَّوْرَاةِ وَلِأُحِلَّ لَكُم بَعْضَ الَّذِي حُرِّمَ عَلَيْكُمْ ۚ وَجِئْتُكُم بِآيَةٍ مِّن رَّبِّكُمْ فَاتَّقُوا اللَّهَ وَأَطِيعُونِ
versets.tr: wa-muṣaddiqan li-mā bayna yadayya mina t-tawrāti wa-li-uḥilla lakum baʿḍa lladhī ḥurrima ʿalaykum wa-jiʾtukum bi-āyatin min rabbikum fa-ttaqū llāha wa-aṭīʿūni
versets.trad: [I have come] to confirm what was before me of the Torah and to make lawful for you some of what was forbidden to you. I have come to you with a sign from your Lord. So fear God and obey me!
versets.commentaire: Jesus, says Qushayrī, was a confirmer of the Laws that preceded him, and the bearer of a Law of his own that abrogated a part of the past while upholding another, as the Qurʾan details. His mission is neither rupture nor mere repetition: it fulfils. And the call is sealed upon the twofold command: "fear God," the foundation, "and obey me," the way.

mots.racine: ص د ق
mots.def: muṣaddiq: one who attests the truth.

mots.racine: ح ل ل
mots.sens: and to make lawful for you

mots.racine: و ق ي / ط و ع
mots.def: ittaqā: to fear with piety; aṭāʿa: to obey.

versets.ar: إِنَّ اللَّهَ رَبِّي وَرَبُّكُمْ فَاعْبُدُوهُ ۗ هَٰذَا صِرَاطٌ مُّسْتَقِيمٌ
versets.tr: inna llāha rabbī wa-rabbukum fa-ʿbudūhu hādhā ṣirāṭun mustaqīmun
versets.trad: God is, in truth, my Lord and your Lord. Worship Him: this is a straight path."
versets.commentaire: Jesus refers all things to the Source: not that he be worshipped, but that the worship go to the one Lord, his and theirs. This is the heart of his message and the anticipated refutation of those who would deify him. The "straight path" is that road without deviation where the sole object of worship and the sole object of contemplation coincide.

mots.racine: ر ب ب
ع ب د
ص ر ط / ق و م

versets.ar: فَلَمَّا أَحَسَّ عِيسَىٰ مِنْهُمُ الْكُفْرَ قَالَ مَنْ أَنصَارِي إِلَى اللَّهِ ۖ قَالَ الْحَوَارِيُّونَ نَحْنُ أَنصَارُ اللَّهِ آمَنَّا بِاللَّهِ وَاشْهَدْ بِأَنَّا مُسْلِمُونَ
versets.tr: fa-lammā aḥassa ʿīsā minhumu l-kufra qāla man anṣārī ilā llāhi qāla l-ḥawāriyyūna naḥnu anṣāru llāhi āmannā bi-llāhi wa-shhad bi-annā muslimūna
versets.trad: When Jesus perceived their unbelief, he said: "Who shall be my helpers in the way of God?" The disciples said: "We are the helpers of God; we believe in God; bear witness that we are submitted [to Him] (muslimūn)!
versets.commentaire: When Jesus had delivered the message and they divided — a few believing him, the most denying him — he knew, says Qushayrī, that prophethood never goes without trial and without the gripping hand of enemies: so he detached his heart from them and turned his whole purpose sincerely toward God. And there answered those upon whom the signs of divine solicitude had spread and who were elected by the marks of election: "We are the helpers of God."

mots.racine: ح س س / ك ف ر
ن ص ر
mots.sens: who are my helpers toward God

mots.racine: ح و ر
ش ه د / س ل م
mots.sens: bear witness that we are submitted

versets.ar: رَبَّنَا آمَنَّا بِمَا أَنزَلْتَ وَاتَّبَعْنَا الرَّسُولَ فَاكْتُبْنَا مَعَ الشَّاهِدِينَ
versets.tr: rabbanā āmannā bi-mā anzalta wa-ttabaʿnā r-rasūla fa-ktubnā maʿa sh-shāhidīna
versets.trad: Our Lord! We have believed in what You have revealed; we have followed the Messenger; inscribe us among the witnesses!"
themes: to be inscribed among the witnesses
versets.commentaire: The disciples seal their faith with a supplication: to believe in the revelation, to follow the Messenger, and to ask to be inscribed among those who attest the truth. Qushayrī notes the purity of their state: they reassure their prophet of the firmness of their belief, with no trouble altering what they are. True faith is known by this, that it wishes to be recorded in the register of the faithful, not in the regard of men.

mots.racine: ن ز ل
mots.def: anzala: to send down, to reveal.

mots.racine: ت ب ع / ر س ل
ك ت ب / ش ه د

versets.ar: وَمَكَرُوا وَمَكَرَ اللَّهُ ۖ وَاللَّهُ خَيْرُ الْمَاكِرِينَ
versets.tr: wa-makarū wa-makara llāhu wa-llāhu khayru l-mākirīna
versets.trad: They [the deniers] devised a scheme [against Jesus], and God too devised; and God is the best of devisers.
versets.commentaire: The others, says Qushayrī, persisted in discord, pressed their hostility to the extreme, laid snares for him and schemed; but God made them taste the recoil of their own scheme. They thought they had crucified and killed him: sheer ignorance, and confusion cast upon them. God — glory be to Him — raised Jesus, His prophet and His near one, while exclusion and curse struck his enemies. The "best of devisers" turns the adversary's plot back upon himself.

mots.racine: م ك ر
م ك ر
mots.def: the divine makr: the sovereign overturning of the plan.

mots.racine: خ ي ر / م ك ر

versets.ar: إِذْ قَالَ اللَّهُ يَا عِيسَىٰ إِنِّي مُتَوَفِّيكَ وَرَافِعُكَ إِلَيَّ وَمُطَهِّرُكَ مِنَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا وَجَاعِلُ الَّذِينَ اتَّبَعُوكَ فَوْقَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا إِلَىٰ يَوْمِ الْقِيَامَةِ ۖ ثُمَّ إِلَيَّ مَرْجِعُكُمْ فَأَحْكُمُ بَيْنَكُمْ فِيمَا كُنتُمْ فِيهِ تَخْتَلِفُونَ
versets.tr: idh qāla llāhu yā ʿīsā innī mutawaffīka wa-rāfiʿuka ilayya wa-muṭahhiruka mina lladhīna kafarū wa-jāʿilu lladhīna-ttabaʿūka fawqa lladhīna kafarū ilā yawmi l-qiyāmati thumma ilayya marjiʿukum fa-aḥkumu baynakum fī-mā kuntum fīhi takhtalifūna
versets.trad: God said: "O Jesus! I shall take you to Myself, raise you up to Me, purify you of those who disbelieve, and set those who have followed you above the deniers until the Day of Resurrection. Then to Me shall be your return; I shall judge between you concerning that wherein you differed.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī reads here an inward allusion of rare profundity: "I gather you to yourself, I withdraw you from yourself, I raise you above the attributes of humanity, I purify you wholly of your own will — until you are moved by Us, for Us, with nothing left for you to choose of your own". And he adds: He purified his heart of the regard for what is other than Him, of the contemplation of likenesses and traces, in every state. The raising of the body figures the raising up out of the self.

mots.racine: و ف ي
mots.def: tawaffā: to gather fully, to take back.

mots.racine: ر ف ع
ط ه ر
ر ج ع
mots.def: marjiʿ: the place and term of return.

versets.ar: فَأَمَّا الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا فَأُعَذِّبُهُمْ عَذَابًا شَدِيدًا فِي الدُّنْيَا وَالْآخِرَةِ وَمَا لَهُم مِّن نَّاصِرِينَ
versets.tr: fa-ammā lladhīna kafarū fa-uʿadhdhibuhum ʿadhāban shadīdan fī d-dunyā wa-l-ākhirati wa-mā lahum min nāṣirīna
versets.trad: As for the deniers, I shall chastise them with a severe chastisement in this world and in the life to come. They shall find no defenders.
versets.commentaire: The verse parts the destinies: for the deniers, Gehenna, says Qushayrī, with no helper at all. The absence of "defenders" expresses the essential isolation of one who has cut himself off from God: having denied the only recourse, he finds no support left, neither in this world nor in the next. The deprivation of aid is the obverse of the refusal of the Source.

mots.racine: ع ذ ب
ع ذ ب / ش د د
ن ص ر

versets.ar: وَأَمَّا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحَاتِ فَيُوَفِّيهِمْ أُجُورَهُمْ ۗ وَاللَّهُ لَا يُحِبُّ الظَّالِمِينَ
versets.tr: wa-ammā lladhīna āmanū wa-ʿamilū ṣ-ṣāliḥāti fa-yuwaffīhim ujūrahum wa-llāhu lā yuḥibbu ẓ-ẓālimīna
versets.trad: And as for those who have believed and done righteous deeds, God will pay them their reward in full. And God does not love the unjust.
versets.commentaire: To the chastisement of the one answers the full reward of the other: the believers are in bliss, says Qushayrī, as the deniers are in the Fire. The payment "in full" (yuwaffīhim) means that nothing of merit is withheld; and the divine refusal of injustice ("God does not love the unjust") guarantees that no sincere work shall be forgotten.

mots.racine: ع م ل / ص ل ح
و ف ي / أ ج ر
mots.sens: He pays them their wage in full
mots.def: waffā: to discharge in full; ajr: reward.

mots.racine: ظ ل م

versets.ar: ذَٰلِكَ نَتْلُوهُ عَلَيْكَ مِنَ الْآيَاتِ وَالذِّكْرِ الْحَكِيمِ
versets.tr: dhālika natlūhu ʿalayka mina l-āyāti wa-dh-dhikri l-ḥakīmi
versets.trad: This is part of the signs and of the wise Remembrance that We recite to you."
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī insists on the way of this knowledge: We recite it to you, O Muhammad, and We make its meanings known to you by what We reveal to you — not by the effort you would expend to attain them, nor by the learning of reports, nor by what you would deduce through reasoning. The "wise Remembrance" descends; it is not wrested from the labor of the intellect.

mots.racine: ت ل و
أ ي ي
ذ ك ر / ح ك م
mots.def: dhikr: remembrance; ḥakīm: imbued with wisdom.

versets.ar: إِنَّ مَثَلَ عِيسَىٰ عِندَ اللَّهِ كَمَثَلِ آدَمَ ۖ خَلَقَهُ مِن تُرَابٍ ثُمَّ قَالَ لَهُ كُن فَيَكُونُ
versets.tr: inna mathala ʿīsā ʿinda llāhi ka-mathali ādama khalaqahu min turābin thumma qāla lahu kun fa-yakūnu
versets.trad: Truly, the case of Jesus before God is as the case of Adam: God created him from dust, then said to him: "Be!" and he was.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī draws the two figures together: He singled them both out by the purity of spirit, without transmission through lineages: Adam was distinguished by the attribute of origination, and Jesus by the breathing of the spirit into him as an honor. Yet he keeps the measure: however great they both may be, the mark of contingency and of the creaturely station remains attached to them. Born without ordinary cause, Jesus is nonetheless a creature: the "Be" that brought him forth binds him to his condition.

mots.racine: م ث ل
م ث ل
خ ل ق / ت ر ب

versets.ar: الْحَقُّ مِن رَّبِّكَ فَلَا تَكُن مِّنَ الْمُمْتَرِينَ
versets.tr: al-ḥaqqu min rabbika fa-lā takun mina l-mumtarīna
versets.trad: The Truth comes from your Lord. Be not, then, of those who doubt.
versets.commentaire: "The Truth comes from your Lord, O Muhammad," comments Qushayrī: never doubt that He — glory be to Him — has no peer in the act of bringing into being, and that no creature has any power to establish anything of itself. And he refers all being to its source: existents drawn out of the void of non-being have in God their principle and toward Him their return. Doubt has no hold upon one who beholds the sole Maker.

mots.racine: ح ق ق
mots.def: al-ḥaqq: the Real, the certain reality.

mots.racine: ر ب ب
م ر ي
mots.def: mumtarī: sceptic, one who is agitated in doubt.

versets.ar: فَمَنْ حَاجَّكَ فِيهِ مِن بَعْدِ مَا جَاءَكَ مِنَ الْعِلْمِ فَقُلْ تَعَالَوْا نَدْعُ أَبْنَاءَنَا وَأَبْنَاءَكُمْ وَنِسَاءَنَا وَنِسَاءَكُمْ وَأَنفُسَنَا وَأَنفُسَكُمْ ثُمَّ نَبْتَهِلْ فَنَجْعَل لَّعْنَتَ اللَّهِ عَلَى الْكَاذِبِينَ
versets.tr: fa-man ḥājjaka fīhi min baʿdi mā jāʾaka mina l-ʿilmi fa-qul taʿālaw nadʿu abnāʾanā wa-abnāʾakum wa-nisāʾanā wa-nisāʾakum wa-anfusanā wa-anfusakum thumma nabtahil fa-najʿal laʿnata llāhi ʿalā l-kādhibīna
versets.trad: If anyone disputes with you concerning this matter after the knowledge that has come to you, say: "Come! Let us summon our sons and your sons, our women and your women, ourselves and yourselves; then let us make a mutual imprecation, calling down the curse of God upon the liars."
versets.commentaire: "After the truth of what is said to you has become clear, after the knowledge of Our Word has been realized in your heart," says Qushayrī, feel no embarrassment in inviting them to the imprecation: be assured that victory and triumph are yours, that We have taken you under Our tutelage and sheltered you in the bosom of Our nearness. He adds that had they consented to this ordeal, the valleys would have blazed over them with a burning fire, but God spared them for the sake of the believers He knew to be in their progeny.

mots.racine: ح ج ج
ب ه ل
mots.def: ibtahala: to implore ardently, to consign to the curse.

mots.racine: ل ع ن
ك ذ ب

versets.ar: إِنَّ هَٰذَا لَهُوَ الْقَصَصُ الْحَقُّ ۚ وَمَا مِنْ إِلَٰهٍ إِلَّا اللَّهُ ۚ وَإِنَّ اللَّهَ لَهُوَ الْعَزِيزُ الْحَكِيمُ
versets.tr: inna hādhā la-huwa l-qaṣaṣu l-ḥaqqu wa-mā min ilāhin illā llāhu wa-inna llāha la-huwa l-ʿazīzu l-ḥakīmu
versets.trad: This is indeed the true account; and there is no god but God (Allāh). And it is He, God, who is the All-Mighty, the All-Wise.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī raises the doxology to the metaphysical plane: no mote of doubt could ever impose itself upon the witnesses of the oneness; no created mind can pierce the secret of His decree; nothing known that existence encompasses, nothing imagined that representation fashions, could ever approach Him. The "true account" is true because it issues into the one Real alone: no divinity but Him, the All-Mighty, the All-Wise.

mots.racine: ق ص ص / ح ق ق
أ ل ه
ع ز ز / ح ك م
mots.def: ʿazīz: unapproachable and strong; ḥakīm: wise.

versets.ar: فَإِن تَوَلَّوْا فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ عَلِيمٌ بِالْمُفْسِدِينَ
versets.tr: fa-in tawallaw fa-inna llāha ʿalīmun bi-l-mufsidīna
versets.trad: If they turn away, then God knows well the sowers of corruption.
versets.commentaire: "If they turn away, O Muhammad," says Qushayrī, no ambiguity of any disputant can stand before the radiance of your lights. And he sketches the fate of the corrupters: either He seizes them at a stroke, or He shows forbearance until, their illusions firmly settled, He takes them unawares, with none to help them. The divine knowledge does not overlook the sower of corruption.

mots.racine: و ل ي
mots.def: tawallā: to turn away, to turn one's back.

mots.racine: ع ل م
ف س د
