versets.ar: قُلْ يَا أَهْلَ الْكِتَابِ تَعَالَوْا إِلَىٰ كَلِمَةٍ سَوَاءٍ بَيْنَنَا وَبَيْنَكُمْ أَلَّا نَعْبُدَ إِلَّا اللَّهَ وَلَا نُشْرِكَ بِهِ شَيْئًا وَلَا يَتَّخِذَ بَعْضُنَا بَعْضًا أَرْبَابًا مِّن دُونِ اللَّهِ ۚ فَإِن تَوَلَّوْا فَقُولُوا اشْهَدُوا بِأَنَّا مُسْلِمُونَ
versets.tr: qul yā ahla l-kitābi taʿālaw ilā kalimatin sawāʾin baynanā wa-baynakum allā naʿbuda illā llāha wa-lā nushrika bihi shayʾan wa-lā yattakhidha baʿḍunā baʿḍan arbāban min dūni llāhi fa-in tawallaw fa-qūlū-shhadū bi-annā muslimūna
versets.trad: Say: "O People of the Book! Come to a word common between us and you: that we worship none but God, that we associate nothing with Him, and that none of us take others as lords besides God." If they turn away, say to them: "Bear witness that we are submitted [muslimūn]!"
versets.commentaire: "The common word," says Qushayrī, "is the word of oneness and the recognition that the Real alone brings things into being." And he draws out the inward pearl: "to worship none but God" means: let your inmost secret contemplate no creature; just as He is your sole worshipped one, let Him be your sole goal and your sole regard — and herein lies the guarding against association. You are the first of the others-than-Him whom you must not contemplate. "That none take another for a lord" means: to abandon both praise and blame addressed to men.

mots.racine: ك ل م / س و ي
ع ب د
mots.sens: that we worship none but God

mots.racine: ش ر ك
mots.sens: that we associate nothing with Him

mots.racine: ر ب ب

versets.ar: يَا أَهْلَ الْكِتَابِ لِمَ تُحَاجُّونَ فِي إِبْرَاهِيمَ وَمَا أُنزِلَتِ التَّوْرَاةُ وَالْإِنجِيلُ إِلَّا مِن بَعْدِهِ ۚ أَفَلَا تَعْقِلُونَ
versets.tr: yā ahla l-kitābi lima tuḥājjūna fī ibrāhīma wa-mā unzilati t-tawrātu wa-l-injīlu illā min baʿdihi a-fa-lā taʿqilūna
versets.trad: O People of the Book! Why do you dispute concerning Abraham, when the Torah and the Gospel were not revealed until after him? Will you not reason?
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī sees here a divine jealousy for His intimate: He let down upon His friend — peace be upon him — the veil of desire and the curtain of jealousy, severing every bond between him and them after each had claimed him for their own. And he makes the absurdity plain: how could Abraham have followed a religion that came after him? This is "a self-contradiction of mere conjecture."

mots.racine: ح ج ج
ب ع د
ع ق ل

versets.ar: هَا أَنتُمْ هَٰؤُلَاءِ حَاجَجْتُمْ فِيمَا لَكُم بِهِ عِلْمٌ فَلِمَ تُحَاجُّونَ فِيمَا لَيْسَ لَكُم بِهِ عِلْمٌ ۚ وَاللَّهُ يَعْلَمُ وَأَنتُمْ لَا تَعْلَمُونَ
versets.tr: hā antum hāʾulāʾi ḥājajtum fī-mā lakum bihi ʿilmun fa-lima tuḥājjūna fī-mā laysa lakum bihi ʿilmun wa-llāhu yaʿlamu wa-antum lā taʿlamūna
versets.trad: Here you are, the very ones who have already disputed about things of which you had knowledge; why then dispute about things of which you have no knowledge? God knows, but you do not know.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī distinguishes the two disputes: as for what stood in your Book, you had "an explanation and could have a proof"; but as for that of which you have no indication and no way toward knowledge — how have you taken it upon yourselves to judge it and presumed to grasp it? The boldness of pronouncing without knowledge is denounced as an effrontery of the soul.

mots.sens: here you are, the very ones

mots.racine: ع ل م
ع ل م

versets.ar: مَا كَانَ إِبْرَاهِيمُ يَهُودِيًّا وَلَا نَصْرَانِيًّا وَلَٰكِن كَانَ حَنِيفًا مُّسْلِمًا وَمَا كَانَ مِنَ الْمُشْرِكِينَ
versets.tr: mā kāna ibrāhīmu yahūdiyyan wa-lā naṣrāniyyan wa-lākin kāna ḥanīfan musliman wa-mā kāna mina l-mushrikīna
versets.trad: Abraham was neither a Jew nor a Christian, but he was a pure monotheist (ḥanīf), submitted to God (muslim), and he was not among the associators.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī clarifies the root of ḥanīf: the upright one, he who holds firm in rectitude upon the truth, not the inclined one who would swerve. Then he draws the portrait of the intimate: Abraham was a ḥanīf, neither inclined away from the Real nor swerving from the Way, lingering at nothing in which the soul might have its share; he committed his wealth, his person, his son, and all that was his to the decree of God, awaiting His command.

mots.racine: ه و د
ن ص ر
ح ن ف / س ل م
mots.def: ḥanīf: upright monotheist; muslim: submitted to God.

mots.racine: ش ر ك
mots.def: mushrik: one who associates partners with God.

versets.ar: إِنَّ أَوْلَى النَّاسِ بِإِبْرَاهِيمَ لَلَّذِينَ اتَّبَعُوهُ وَهَٰذَا النَّبِيُّ وَالَّذِينَ آمَنُوا ۗ وَاللَّهُ وَلِيُّ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ
versets.tr: inna awlā n-nāsi bi-ibrāhīma la-lladhīna-ttabaʿūhu wa-hādhā n-nabiyyu wa-lladhīna āmanū wa-llāhu waliyyu l-muʾminīna
versets.trad: Truly, the men worthiest of Abraham are those who followed him, together with this prophet and those who believe; and God is the Protector of the believers.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī makes of the faithful of every age a single party: when passions and heresies divided and each faction fell into some error, the people of the Real remained, in every age and every time, upon the highest proof; they were a single party, the nearest of all to one another. Abraham is "the master of the Real"; his true posterity is that which shares his oneness. And "God is the protector of the believers," through aid, triumph, election, and nearness.

mots.racine: و ل ي
mots.def: awlā: the most entitled, the nearest.

mots.racine: ت ب ع
و ل ي / أ م ن

versets.ar: وَدَّت طَّائِفَةٌ مِّنْ أَهْلِ الْكِتَابِ لَوْ يُضِلُّونَكُمْ وَمَا يُضِلُّونَ إِلَّا أَنفُسَهُمْ وَمَا يَشْعُرُونَ
versets.tr: waddat ṭāʾifatun min ahli l-kitābi law yuḍillūnakum wa-mā yuḍillūna illā anfusahum wa-mā yashʿurūna
versets.trad: A party of the People of the Book would dearly love to lead you astray; but they lead none astray except themselves, and they do not perceive it.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī lays bare the psychology of the misleader: one whom seduction has seized, whom trial has touched, whom temptation has turned aside, would wish all men to share what has befallen him. Thus the People of the Book wish to swerve the believers from the Real, but God refuses anything but to perfect His light, and to make the recoil of their own act fall back upon them. The evil willed for another returns upon its author, unbeknown to him.

mots.racine: و د د / ط و ف
ض ل ل
mots.sens: that they might lead you astray

mots.racine: ش ع ر

versets.ar: يَا أَهْلَ الْكِتَابِ لِمَ تَكْفُرُونَ بِآيَاتِ اللَّهِ وَأَنتُمْ تَشْهَدُونَ
versets.tr: yā ahla l-kitābi lima takfurūna bi-āyāti llāhi wa-antum tashhadūna
versets.trad: O People of the Book! Why do you deny the signs of God while you yourselves bear witness to them?
themes: denial in the face of evidence
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī recalls that they used to attest, before his sending — peace be upon him — the truth of his prophethood. Hence his pressing question: what then drives you in your straying, to the point of denying what you knew? The gravest of denials is that which arises against acknowledged evidence.

mots.racine: ك ف ر
أ ي ي
ش ه د
mots.sens: while you bear witness to them

versets.ar: يَا أَهْلَ الْكِتَابِ لِمَ تَلْبِسُونَ الْحَقَّ بِالْبَاطِلِ وَتَكْتُمُونَ الْحَقَّ وَأَنتُمْ تَعْلَمُونَ
versets.tr: yā ahla l-kitābi lima talbisūna l-ḥaqqa bi-l-bāṭili wa-taktumūna l-ḥaqqa wa-antum taʿlamūna
versets.trad: O People of the Book! Why do you clothe the Truth with falsehood, and why do you knowingly conceal the Truth?
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī specifies the object of the concealment: you keep silent about the truth concerning Muhammad — peace be upon him — while knowing that he is the truthful prophet; and what is that but the verdict of abandonment (khidhlān) and the sentence of deprivation? He adds that some of them hide their hostility through hypocrisy, professing accord in public while nourishing their corrupt belief in secret. To disguise the true while knowing it is the worst of willed blindnesses.

mots.racine: ل ب س / ح ق ق / ب ط ل
mots.sens: you clothe the true with the false
mots.def: labasa: to confuse, to obscure; bāṭil: false.

mots.racine: ك ت م
ع ل م

versets.ar: وَقَالَت طَّائِفَةٌ مِّنْ أَهْلِ الْكِتَابِ آمِنُوا بِالَّذِي أُنزِلَ عَلَى الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَجْهَ النَّهَارِ وَاكْفُرُوا آخِرَهُ لَعَلَّهُمْ يَرْجِعُونَ
versets.tr: wa-qālat ṭāʾifatun min ahli l-kitābi āminū bi-lladhī unzila ʿalā lladhīna āmanū wajha n-nahāri wa-kfurū ākhirahu laʿallahum yarjiʿūna
versets.trad: A party of the People of the Book say among themselves: "At the beginning of the day, believe in what has been sent down to the believers, and at its end, deny it: perhaps thus they will turn back [to their former religion];
versets.commentaire: Here is the stratagem of confusion: to feign faith in the morning, to abjure it in the evening, so as to shake the believers. Qushayrī shows that God thwarts it: He made it known that their hypocrisy was unveiled to the believers and would profit them nothing — neither in this world, for God informed His prophet and the believers, nor in the next, for want of sincerity. The feint that thinks to manipulate faith shatters upon the divine unveiling.

mots.racine: أ م ن / ن ه ر
mots.def: wajh an-nahār: the face, the beginning of the day.

mots.racine: ك ف ر / أ خ ر
ر ج ع
mots.sens: that perhaps they will turn back

versets.ar: وَلَا تُؤْمِنُوا إِلَّا لِمَن تَبِعَ دِينَكُمْ قُلْ إِنَّ الْهُدَىٰ هُدَى اللَّهِ أَن يُؤْتَىٰ أَحَدٌ مِّثْلَ مَا أُوتِيتُمْ أَوْ يُحَاجُّوكُمْ عِندَ رَبِّكُمْ ۗ قُلْ إِنَّ الْفَضْلَ بِيَدِ اللَّهِ يُؤْتِيهِ مَن يَشَاءُ ۗ وَاللَّهُ وَاسِعٌ عَلِيمٌ
versets.tr: wa-lā tuʾminū illā li-man tabiʿa dīnakum qul inna l-hudā hudā llāhi an yuʾtā aḥadun mithla mā ūtītum aw yuḥājjūkum ʿinda rabbikum qul inna l-faḍla bi-yadi llāhi yuʾtīhi man yashāʾu wa-llāhu wāsiʿun ʿalīmun
versets.trad: and trust none but those who follow your religion!" Say [O Muhammad]: "Truly, the right guidance is the guidance of God." [They say further:] "In this way you will prevent anyone from receiving the like of what was given to you, or from disputing with you in the presence of your Lord." Say: "Grace is in the hand of God; He gives it to whom He wills." And God is bountiful, all-knowing.
themes: grace in the hand of God
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī gathers here a word framed as a warning to the believers: do not consort with adversaries, and do not deliver your secrets to strangers. Then he rises to the pearl: "Grace is in the hand of God" — it is He who singles out whom He wills by the lights of knowledge, and reserves for whom He wills abandonment and deprivation. Guidance and the gift are not bartered among men: they descend from a sovereign hand.

mots.racine: ه د ي
mots.sens: guidance is the guidance of God

mots.racine: ف ض ل
mots.sens: grace is in the hand of God

mots.racine: و س ع / ع ل م

versets.ar: يَخْتَصُّ بِرَحْمَتِهِ مَن يَشَاءُ ۗ وَاللَّهُ ذُو الْفَضْلِ الْعَظِيمِ
versets.tr: yakhtaṣṣu bi-raḥmatihi man yashāʾu wa-llāhu dhū l-faḍli l-ʿaẓīmi
versets.trad: He singles out for His mercy whom He wills. And God is the Master of immense grace.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī unfolds the diversity of the gifts: one people He has distinguished by the grace of noble character, another by the grace of provisions, another by the grace of worship, another by that of pure willing, others by the encounter of secrets — "were you to count the favors of God, you could not number them." And he draws the pearl: hearing "He reserves His mercy for whom He wills," they knew that the means do not guide of themselves: all is in the first initiative and the pure willing.

mots.racine: خ ص ص / ر ح م
mots.sens: He singles out for His mercy
mots.def: ikhtaṣṣa: to single out, to reserve.

mots.racine: ش ي أ
ف ض ل / ع ظ م

versets.ar: وَمِنْ أَهْلِ الْكِتَابِ مَنْ إِن تَأْمَنْهُ بِقِنطَارٍ يُؤَدِّهِ إِلَيْكَ وَمِنْهُم مَّنْ إِن تَأْمَنْهُ بِدِينَارٍ لَّا يُؤَدِّهِ إِلَيْكَ إِلَّا مَا دُمْتَ عَلَيْهِ قَائِمًا ۗ ذَٰلِكَ بِأَنَّهُمْ قَالُوا لَيْسَ عَلَيْنَا فِي الْأُمِّيِّينَ سَبِيلٌ وَيَقُولُونَ عَلَى اللَّهِ الْكَذِبَ وَهُمْ يَعْلَمُونَ
versets.tr: wa-min ahli l-kitābi man in taʾmanhu bi-qinṭārin yuʾaddihi ilayka wa-minhum man in taʾmanhu bi-dīnārin lā yuʾaddihi ilayka illā mā dumta ʿalayhi qāʾiman dhālika bi-annahum qālū laysa ʿalaynā fī l-ummiyyīna sabīlun wa-yaqūlūna ʿalā llāhi l-kadhiba wa-hum yaʿlamūna
versets.trad: Among the People of the Book is one to whom, if you entrust a sack of gold, he will render it back to you, and among them is one to whom, if you entrust a single dinar, he will not render it back unless you stand over him without relenting. That is because they say: "We are bound by no duty toward the unlettered!" Thus they utter lies against God, and they do so knowingly.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī observes that, despite their straying, they differ in their characters: all are faithless toward the trust of religion, yet some return to uprightness in dealings. Still, this honesty wins them no saving merit; it does no more than lighten their punishment. And he denounces their sophistry: "we are bound by no duty toward the unlettered" — as though the verdict belonged to them. But judgment is God's alone.

mots.racine: ق ن ط ر
mots.def: qinṭār: a great sum, a mass of gold.

mots.racine: أ د ي
mots.sens: he renders it back to you

mots.racine: أ م م / س ب ل
mots.sens: no claim against us regarding the unlettered
mots.def: ummī: one without Scripture; sabīl: way, claim.

versets.ar: بَلَىٰ مَنْ أَوْفَىٰ بِعَهْدِهِ وَاتَّقَىٰ فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ يُحِبُّ الْمُتَّقِينَ
versets.tr: balā man awfā bi-ʿahdihi wa-ttaqā fa-inna llāha yuḥibbu l-muttaqīna
versets.trad: Nay, but whoever keeps his covenant and fears God shall know that God loves those who fear Him.
versets.commentaire: To the foregoing sophistry the verse opposes the straight road: to keep one's covenant and to fear God. Qushayrī makes fidelity to the pact (wafāʾ) the mark of the true servant; the divine love for the God-fearing (muttaqūn) crowns those who do not betray the trust confided to them. Reverential fear, far from being a joyless servitude, is the gateway of love.

mots.def: balā: a particle of affirmative refutation.

mots.racine: و ف ي / ع ه د
ح ب ب / و ق ي
mots.def: muttaqī: one who fears God piously.

versets.ar: إِنَّ الَّذِينَ يَشْتَرُونَ بِعَهْدِ اللَّهِ وَأَيْمَانِهِمْ ثَمَنًا قَلِيلًا أُولَٰئِكَ لَا خَلَاقَ لَهُمْ فِي الْآخِرَةِ وَلَا يُكَلِّمُهُمُ اللَّهُ وَلَا يَنظُرُ إِلَيْهِمْ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ وَلَا يُزَكِّيهِمْ وَلَهُمْ عَذَابٌ أَلِيمٌ
versets.tr: inna lladhīna yashtarūna bi-ʿahdi llāhi wa-aymānihim thamanan qalīlan ulāʾika lā khalāqa lahum fī l-ākhirati wa-lā yukallimuhumu llāhu wa-lā yanẓuru ilayhim yawma l-qiyāmati wa-lā yuzakkīhim wa-lahum ʿadhābun alīmun
versets.trad: Truly, those who sell for a paltry price the covenant of God and their oaths shall have no portion in the life to come. On the Day of Resurrection God will not speak to them nor cast upon them a glance. He will not purify them, and they shall suffer a painful chastisement.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī probes the radical loss: those who preferred their whim to their Hereafter, who set their desire before the good pleasure of their Master — these have no portion in the other world; for having enjoyed the ephemeral they chose, they have lost both abodes. And he measures its utmost: deprived of the Real without even tasting a share, and yet not knowing what has struck them: God speaks not to them, regards them not, purifies them not. Silence and the absence of the divine glance are the greatest of chastisements.

mots.racine: ش ر ي / ع ه د
mots.sens: sell the covenant for a paltry price
mots.def: ishtarā: to barter; thaman qalīl: a trifling price.

mots.racine: خ ل ق
ك ل م
mots.sens: God will not speak to them

mots.racine: ن ظ ر
mots.sens: He will not look upon them

versets.ar: وَإِنَّ مِنْهُمْ لَفَرِيقًا يَلْوُونَ أَلْسِنَتَهُم بِالْكِتَابِ لِتَحْسَبُوهُ مِنَ الْكِتَابِ وَمَا هُوَ مِنَ الْكِتَابِ وَيَقُولُونَ هُوَ مِنْ عِندِ اللَّهِ وَمَا هُوَ مِنْ عِندِ اللَّهِ وَيَقُولُونَ عَلَى اللَّهِ الْكَذِبَ وَهُمْ يَعْلَمُونَ
versets.tr: wa-inna minhum la-farīqan yalwūna alsinatahum bi-l-kitābi li-taḥsabūhu mina l-kitābi wa-mā huwa mina l-kitābi wa-yaqūlūna huwa min ʿindi llāhi wa-mā huwa min ʿindi llāhi wa-yaqūlūna ʿalā llāhi l-kadhiba wa-hum yaʿlamūna
versets.trad: Among them are some who twist their tongues while reciting, so that you may suppose it to be from the Book, whereas it is not from the Book. They say it comes from God, whereas it does not come from God. They utter lies against God, and they do so knowingly.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī applies the verse, beyond its primary sense, to the impostors of the way: the pretenders who adorn their expressions and loose their tongues upon what their hearts have no tidings or realization of, deceiving the naive, the common folk, and the beginners, making them believe they possess what they utter. And he warns: the people of the Real see these secrets plainly; such men speak from ruined hearts and veiled secrets — we seek refuge in God from disgrace!

mots.racine: ل و ي / ل س ن
ح س ب / ك ت ب
mots.sens: that you may suppose it from the Book

mots.racine: ق و ل / ك ذ ب

versets.ar: مَا كَانَ لِبَشَرٍ أَن يُؤْتِيَهُ اللَّهُ الْكِتَابَ وَالْحُكْمَ وَالنُّبُوَّةَ ثُمَّ يَقُولَ لِلنَّاسِ كُونُوا عِبَادًا لِّي مِن دُونِ اللَّهِ وَلَٰكِن كُونُوا رَبَّانِيِّينَ بِمَا كُنتُمْ تُعَلِّمُونَ الْكِتَابَ وَبِمَا كُنتُمْ تَدْرُسُونَ
versets.tr: mā kāna li-basharin an yuʾtiyahu llāhu l-kitāba wa-l-ḥukma wa-n-nubuwwata thumma yaqūla li-n-nāsi kūnū ʿibādan lī min dūni llāhi wa-lākin kūnū rabbāniyyīna bi-mā kuntum tuʿallimūna l-kitāba wa-bi-mā kuntum tadrusūna
versets.trad: It is not for a man to whom God has given the Book, judgment, and prophethood then to say to people: "Be my worshippers, not those of God"; rather he should say: "Be men of the Lord (rabbāniyyūn), since you teach the Scripture and study it."
themes: the prophet calls to God alone
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī makes of this verse a summit: it is not in the attribute of one whom We have elected for prophethood and chosen for sanctity to call men to himself; the call of the messengers and the saints goes only ever to God. Then he defines the rabbānī by a cascade of pearls: the knowers in God, gentle in God, who subsist by their extinction to all but Him, whose personal portions are consumed, immersed in the realities of His existence to the point of no longer sensing themselves; they speak by God, hear by God, see by God — effaced in God from all but Him. And again: the rabbānī is he whom the vicissitudes of destiny do not change, whom no trial alters and no blessing corrupts.

mots.racine: ك ت ب / ح ك م / ن ب أ

mots.ar: كُونُوا عِبَادًا لِّي مِن دُونِ اللَّهِ
mots.tr: kūnū ʿibādan lī min dūni llāh
mots.racine: ع ب د
ر ب ب
mots.def: rabbānī: wholly devoted to the Lord (rabb), versed in divine science.

versets.ar: وَلَا يَأْمُرَكُمْ أَن تَتَّخِذُوا الْمَلَائِكَةَ وَالنَّبِيِّينَ أَرْبَابًا ۗ أَيَأْمُرُكُم بِالْكُفْرِ بَعْدَ إِذْ أَنتُم مُّسْلِمُونَ
versets.tr: wa-lā yaʾmurakum an tattakhidhū l-malāʾikata wa-n-nabiyyīna arbāban a-yaʾmurukum bi-l-kufri baʿda idh antum muslimūna
versets.trad: Nor does he command you to take the angels and the prophets as lords. Would he command you unbelief after you have submitted [to God]?
themes: neither angels nor prophets as lords
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī gives the inward sense: ascribe to neither angels nor prophets the least particle of efficacy in good or in ill; acknowledge the limit of the created condition and the proper right of the divine lordship. And he transposes the closing question to the register of contemplation: "Would he command you unbelief after your submission?" — that is: would he command you to affirm the creature after you have contemplated the Real, to return to the likenesses after the lights of oneness have shone within your secrets and the suns of singling-out have risen in your hearts?

mots.racine: أ م ر
م ل ك / ن ب أ / ر ب ب
mots.sens: the angels and prophets as lords

mots.racine: أ م ر / ك ف ر
س ل م

versets.ar: وَإِذْ أَخَذَ اللَّهُ مِيثَاقَ النَّبِيِّينَ لَمَا آتَيْتُكُم مِّن كِتَابٍ وَحِكْمَةٍ ثُمَّ جَاءَكُمْ رَسُولٌ مُّصَدِّقٌ لِّمَا مَعَكُمْ لَتُؤْمِنُنَّ بِهِ وَلَتَنصُرُنَّهُ ۚ قَالَ أَأَقْرَرْتُمْ وَأَخَذْتُمْ عَلَىٰ ذَٰلِكُمْ إِصْرِي ۖ قَالُوا أَقْرَرْنَا ۚ قَالَ فَاشْهَدُوا وَأَنَا مَعَكُم مِّنَ الشَّاهِدِينَ
versets.tr: wa-idh akhadha llāhu mīthāqa n-nabiyyīna lamā ātaytukum min kitābin wa-ḥikmatin thumma jāʾakum rasūlun muṣaddiqun limā maʿakum latuʾminunna bihi wa-latanṣurunnahu qāla a-aqrartum wa-akhadhtum ʿalā dhālikum iṣrī qālū aqrarnā qāla fa-shhadū wa-anā maʿakum mina sh-shāhidīn
versets.trad: When God took the covenant of the prophets, He said: "Here is what I bestow upon you of Scripture and Wisdom. Then shall come to you a messenger confirming that which you possess. You shall believe in him and you shall assist him." He said further: "Do you consent, and do you take upon yourselves to bear this charge?" They answered: "We consent!" God said: "Then bear witness, and I am with you among the witnesses."
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī sees here the summit of honour: God took the covenant of all the prophets in favour of Muḥammad, just as He had taken theirs concerning the acknowledgement of His lordship. By binding the name of the Messenger to His own Name, He established his rank as He established His own — thus he becomes the unique one among all creation in degree. Then He smoothed for mankind the path toward knowledge of His majesty through the signs made manifest upon him.

mots.racine: و ث ق / ن ب أ
أ م ن / ن ص ر
mots.sens: you shall believe in him and assist him
mots.def: an oath of allegiance to the future Messenger.

mots.racine: أ ص ر
mots.def: iṣr: a pact that binds and obliges.

versets.ar: فَمَن تَوَلَّىٰ بَعْدَ ذَٰلِكَ فَأُولَٰئِكَ هُمُ الْفَاسِقُونَ
versets.tr: fa-man tawallā baʿda dhālika fa-ulāʾika humu l-fāsiqūn
versets.trad: Those who, thereafter, turn away — they are the depraved.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī unveils the hidden sense: whoever strays from his way or deviates from his path after his proof has arisen and his miracle has shone forth — they are those whose rank has become corrupted, upon whom wrath now weighs for having denied, and who are cut off from the bond of divine solicitude.

mots.racine: و ل ي
mots.def: tawallā: to turn one's back, to turn away.

mots.racine: ف س ق
mots.def: fāsiq: one who departs from obedience.

versets.ar: أَفَغَيْرَ دِينِ اللَّهِ يَبْغُونَ وَلَهُ أَسْلَمَ مَن فِي السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ طَوْعًا وَكَرْهًا وَإِلَيْهِ يُرْجَعُونَ
versets.tr: a-fa-ghayra dīni llāhi yabghūna wa-lahu aslama man fī s-samāwāti wa-l-arḍi ṭawʿan wa-karhan wa-ilayhi yurjaʿūn
versets.trad: Do they desire a religion other than that of God, when all that is in the heavens and the earth submits to Him, willingly or by constraint, and to Him they shall be brought back?
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī depicts the straying of one who looks toward other than the Real: like a mirage that the thirsting man takes for water, and which, when he reaches it, he finds to be but dust. The illusions of the mind are deceiving defiles: whoever ventures into them descends into a barren valley. And "to Him submits whoever is in the heavens and the earth, willingly or by constraint" — for the decree of divinity is exercised over them by way of compulsion.

mots.racine: د ي ن / ب غ ي
mots.sens: do they desire a religion other than God's

mots.racine: ط و ع / ك ر ه

versets.ar: قُلْ آمَنَّا بِاللَّهِ وَمَا أُنزِلَ عَلَيْنَا وَمَا أُنزِلَ عَلَىٰ إِبْرَاهِيمَ وَإِسْمَاعِيلَ وَإِسْحَاقَ وَيَعْقُوبَ وَالْأَسْبَاطِ وَمَا أُوتِيَ مُوسَىٰ وَعِيسَىٰ وَالنَّبِيُّونَ مِن رَّبِّهِمْ لَا نُفَرِّقُ بَيْنَ أَحَدٍ مِّنْهُمْ وَنَحْنُ لَهُ مُسْلِمُونَ
versets.tr: qul āmannā billāhi wa-mā unzila ʿalaynā wa-mā unzila ʿalā ibrāhīma wa-ismāʿīla wa-isḥāqa wa-yaʿqūba wa-l-asbāṭi wa-mā ūtiya mūsā wa-ʿīsā wa-n-nabiyyūna min rabbihim lā nufarriqu bayna aḥadin minhum wa-naḥnu lahu muslimūn
versets.trad: Say: "We believe in God, in what has been revealed to us and in what was revealed to Abraham, Ishmael, Isaac, Jacob and the Tribes; in what was given to Moses, to Jesus and to the prophets from their Lord. We make no distinction in favour of any one of them, and to God we are submitted."
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī unfolds the depth of the "we": we believe through God and not through our own souls, nor through our power or our strength; we make no distinction among any of them save through God, not by our choice, our effort or our acquisition. And he adds, in the manner of Dhū l-Nūn: had He not made known to us who He is, we would never have known Him — otherwise, when could we ever have known?

mots.racine: أ م ن
ف ر ق
mots.sens: we make no distinction among any of them

mots.racine: س ل م
mots.sens: and to Him we are submitted

versets.ar: وَمَن يَبْتَغِ غَيْرَ الْإِسْلَامِ دِينًا فَلَن يُقْبَلَ مِنْهُ وَهُوَ فِي الْآخِرَةِ مِنَ الْخَاسِرِينَ
versets.tr: wa-man yabtaghi ghayra l-islāmi dīnan fa-lan yuqbala minhu wa-huwa fī l-ākhirati mina l-khāsirīn
versets.trad: Whoever seeks a religion other than Islam, it shall not be accepted from him, and in the life to come he shall be among the losers.
themes: Islam as the only accepted way
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī raises "islam" to the level of extinction: whoever follows a path other than effacement beneath the flowing of the divine decree slips into bottomless abysses of error. And: whoever turns to Him through any intermediary other than clinging to Him — his loss exceeds his gain; whoever is not extinguished in the vision of the All does not attain Him through whom is the All. Nothing is accepted apart from marching beneath the banner of the Elect, glorious in his rank.

mots.racine: ب غ ي / س ل م
mots.sens: seeks a religion other than Islam

mots.racine: خ س ر

versets.ar: كَيْفَ يَهْدِي اللَّهُ قَوْمًا كَفَرُوا بَعْدَ إِيمَانِهِمْ وَشَهِدُوا أَنَّ الرَّسُولَ حَقٌّ وَجَاءَهُمُ الْبَيِّنَاتُ وَاللَّهُ لَا يَهْدِي الْقَوْمَ الظَّالِمِينَ
versets.tr: kayfa yahdī llāhu qawman kafarū baʿda īmānihim wa-shahidū anna r-rasūla ḥaqqun wa-jāʾahumu l-bayyinātu wa-llāhu lā yahdī l-qawma ẓ-ẓālimīn
versets.trad: How should God guide a people who turned to unbelief after having believed, after having borne witness to the truthfulness of the Prophet, and after clear proofs had come to them? God does not guide the iniquitous.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī traces all things back to the prior decree: him whom His first judgement has distanced from the merit of union, when would He ever bring near to the carpet of service through his present act? Him whom the sentence of eternity has set aside, when would the sincerity of works bring near? And God prevails over His decree.

mots.racine: ه د ي
mots.sens: how should God guide a people

mots.racine: ب ي ن
mots.sens: and clear proofs came to them

versets.ar: أُولَٰئِكَ جَزَاؤُهُمْ أَنَّ عَلَيْهِمْ لَعْنَةَ اللَّهِ وَالْمَلَائِكَةِ وَالنَّاسِ أَجْمَعِينَ
versets.tr: ulāʾika jazāʾuhum anna ʿalayhim laʿnata llāhi wa-l-malāʾikati wa-n-nāsi ajmaʿīn
versets.trad: For them, their recompense shall be that upon them falls the curse of God, of the angels, and of all mankind.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: their final state is nothing but what was decreed for them at the very beginning of their affair; their beginning was the rejection of the portion, their middle the barring from service, and their end the arrival at estrangement and abasement. The curse is the casting away into distance, sealed by the first decree.

mots.racine: ج ز ي
ل ع ن
mots.sens: the curse of God, the angels, and mankind

versets.ar: خَالِدِينَ فِيهَا لَا يُخَفَّفُ عَنْهُمُ الْعَذَابُ وَلَا هُمْ يُنظَرُونَ
versets.tr: khālidīna fīhā lā yukhaffafu ʿanhumu l-ʿadhābu wa-lā hum yunẓarūn
versets.trad: Therein shall they abide forever. The chastisement shall not be lightened for them, nor shall they be granted any respite,
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: eternal in this abasement, the chastisement does not relax from them for a single instant, and the separation is not lightened from them for a single hour. The weight is not so much the flame as the persistence of exile from the Presence.

mots.racine: خ ل د
خ ف ف / ع ذ ب
mots.sens: the chastisement shall not be lightened for them

versets.ar: إِلَّا الَّذِينَ تَابُوا مِن بَعْدِ ذَٰلِكَ وَأَصْلَحُوا فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ غَفُورٌ رَّحِيمٌ
versets.tr: illā lladhīna tābū min baʿdi dhālika wa-aṣlaḥū fa-inna llāha ghafūrun raḥīm
versets.trad: save those who thereafter repent and amend their ways. For God is forgiving, merciful.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: these are they whom mercy has overtaken; they were not, in the decree of predestination, among the number of the rejected — even though creation might have reckoned them of that band. Repentance reveals the hidden election lying beneath the appearance of sin.

mots.racine: ت و ب
ص ل ح
mots.def: aṣlaḥa: to set right, to reform.

mots.racine: غ ف ر / ر ح م

versets.ar: إِنَّ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا بَعْدَ إِيمَانِهِمْ ثُمَّ ازْدَادُوا كُفْرًا لَّن تُقْبَلَ تَوْبَتُهُمْ وَأُولَٰئِكَ هُمُ الضَّالُّونَ
versets.tr: inna lladhīna kafarū baʿda īmānihim thumma zdādū kufran lan tuqbala tawbatuhum wa-ulāʾika humu ḍ-ḍāllūn
versets.trad: Truly, those who fall into unbelief after having had faith, and whose unbelief then only increases — their repentance shall not be accepted: these are the ones gone astray.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī applies the verse to the wayfarer who turns back: those who have returned to the states of the people of habit after travelling the path of aspiration, who have preferred this lower world and the obedience of caprice to the quest for the Real, then denied the people of the Way — their repentance is not received, for trust is no longer restored once treachery has manifested itself. And he warns: the apostate from this Way becomes its fiercest detractor, more than the stranger who never entered it.

mots.racine: ز ي د / ك ف ر
ق ب ل / ت و ب
mots.sens: their repentance shall not be accepted

mots.racine: ض ل ل
mots.def: ḍāll: one who has lost the way.

versets.ar: إِنَّ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا وَمَاتُوا وَهُمْ كُفَّارٌ فَلَن يُقْبَلَ مِنْ أَحَدِهِم مِّلْءُ الْأَرْضِ ذَهَبًا وَلَوِ افْتَدَىٰ بِهِ أُولَٰئِكَ لَهُمْ عَذَابٌ أَلِيمٌ وَمَا لَهُم مِّن نَّاصِرِينَ
versets.tr: inna lladhīna kafarū wa-mātū wa-hum kuffārun fa-lan yuqbala min aḥadihim milʾu l-arḍi dhahaban wa-lawi ftadā bihi ulāʾika lahum ʿadhābun alīmun wa-mā lahum min nāṣirīn
versets.trad: Truly, those who disbelieve and die as disbelievers — from none of them shall a ransom be accepted, even were he to offer all the gold of the earth. For them is a painful chastisement, and they shall have no helpers.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī draws a subtle warning for the wayfarer who dies after his rupture, even after a fair beginning: he shall not be gathered in the hereafter with the people of that station, and a thousand knowers might intercede for him in vain. Such is the fineness of the divine stratagem: his likeness is cast upon another, so that his kin among the knowers believe it to be him — and none then thinks that intercession for him is needed.

mots.racine: م و ت / ك ف ر
mots.def: death in the state of denial.

mots.racine: م ل أ / ذ ه ب
mots.sens: all the gold of the earth
mots.def: milʾ: a fill, that which fills.

mots.racine: ن ص ر

versets.ar: لَن تَنَالُوا الْبِرَّ حَتَّىٰ تُنفِقُوا مِمَّا تُحِبُّونَ وَمَا تُنفِقُوا مِن شَيْءٍ فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ بِهِ عَلِيمٌ
versets.tr: lan tanālū l-birra ḥattā tunfiqū mimmā tuḥibbūna wa-mā tunfiqū min shayʾin fa-inna llāha bihi ʿalīm
versets.trad: You shall not attain pure virtue until you give freely of that which you love. And whatever you spend thus, truly God knows it.
themes: virtue through giving what one loves
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī meditates upon the partitive "min": whoever desires virtue (al-birr), let him spend from a part of what he loves; but whoever desires the Beneficent (al-Bārr), let him spend all that he loves. And he sharpens it: whoever spends his beloved object of this world finds his desired object with the Real; whoever remains bound to the portions of his soul does not taste the nearness of his Lord. If you attain to giving only by surrendering your beloved, when will you attain the Beneficent by preferring your portions over Him?

mots.racine: ن ي ل / ب ر ر
ن ف ق / ح ب ب
mots.sens: you spend of that which you love

versets.ar: كُلُّ الطَّعَامِ كَانَ حِلًّا لِّبَنِي إِسْرَائِيلَ إِلَّا مَا حَرَّمَ إِسْرَائِيلُ عَلَىٰ نَفْسِهِ مِن قَبْلِ أَن تُنَزَّلَ التَّوْرَاةُ قُلْ فَأْتُوا بِالتَّوْرَاةِ فَاتْلُوهَا إِن كُنتُمْ صَادِقِينَ
versets.tr: kullu ṭ-ṭaʿāmi kāna ḥillan li-banī isrāʾīla illā mā ḥarrama isrāʾīlu ʿalā nafsihi min qabli an tunazzala t-tawrātu qul fa-ʾtū bi-t-tawrāti fa-tlūhā in kuntum ṣādiqīn
versets.trad: All food was lawful for the Children of Israel, save what Israel forbade unto himself before the Torah was revealed. Say: "Bring then the Torah and recite it, if you are truthful!"
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī establishes a principle: the origin of things is that one should not intervene upon them with prohibition or permission; that which carries no boundary is, on the part of the Real, amplitude and gentleness until a command supervenes upon it. Then he distinguishes the ways: upon the people of arrival God has widened the obligations, for the burden weighing upon their hearts is heavier; upon the people of the beginning the way is straitened, that they may take the most arduous, their hearts being yet free of the deeper meanings.

mots.racine: ح ل ل
mots.sens: lawful for the Children of Israel

mots.racine: ح ر م

versets.ar: فَمَنِ افْتَرَىٰ عَلَى اللَّهِ الْكَذِبَ مِن بَعْدِ ذَٰلِكَ فَأُولَٰئِكَ هُمُ الظَّالِمُونَ
versets.tr: fa-mani ftarā ʿalā llāhi l-kadhiba min baʿdi dhālika fa-ulāʾika humu ẓ-ẓālimūn
versets.trad: Those who, after that, forge a lie against God — they are the iniquitous.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī applies the verse to the people of pretensions and illusions: they yield to their own souls, then ascribe to God the whisperings of those souls, whereas God is exempt from them. And he concludes: rare is the servant who knows how to discern between true inspiration and the murmur of the ego.

mots.racine: ف ر ي / ك ذ ب
mots.sens: to forge a lie against God

mots.racine: ظ ل م

versets.ar: قُلْ صَدَقَ اللَّهُ فَاتَّبِعُوا مِلَّةَ إِبْرَاهِيمَ حَنِيفًا وَمَا كَانَ مِنَ الْمُشْرِكِينَ
versets.tr: qul ṣadaqa llāhu fa-ttabiʿū millata ibrāhīma ḥanīfan wa-mā kāna mina l-mushrikīn
versets.trad: Say: "God has spoken true. Follow then the religion of Abraham, a pure believer. He was not among those who associate."
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī defines the way of Abraham as total exodus: the going forth toward God entirely, the surrender to His decree until no remnant remains; for to affirm a single particle of autonomy in the reckoning of created things — that, in truth, is association. Purity (ḥanīfiyya) is to see nothing subsisting any longer before Him.

mots.racine: ص د ق
م ل ل / ح ن ف
mots.sens: the religion of Abraham, a pure believer

versets.ar: إِنَّ أَوَّلَ بَيْتٍ وُضِعَ لِلنَّاسِ لَلَّذِي بِبَكَّةَ مُبَارَكًا وَهُدًى لِّلْعَالَمِينَ
versets.tr: inna awwala baytin wuḍiʿa li-n-nāsi la-lladhī bi-bakkata mubārakan wa-hudan li-l-ʿālamīn
versets.trad: Truly, the first House established for mankind is that at Bakka: a blessed place and a guidance for the worlds.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī makes the House a mirror of the heart: the House is stone, the servant is clay; He has bound the clay to the stone. Then he unveils: the House is the place of circumambulation for bodies, but the Real is the object sought by hearts. And the celebrated saying: the Kaʿba is the house of the Real in stone, and the heart is the house of the Real in the secret; the subtle realities revolve around the hearts of the knowers as the pilgrims revolve around the House.

mots.racine: ب ي ت / و ض ع
mots.sens: the first House established for mankind

mots.racine: ب ك ك
mots.def: from bakk: the press of the crowd thronging there.

mots.racine: ب ر ك / ه د ي
mots.sens: blessed and a guidance for the worlds

versets.ar: فِيهِ آيَاتٌ بَيِّنَاتٌ مَّقَامُ إِبْرَاهِيمَ وَمَن دَخَلَهُ كَانَ آمِنًا وَلِلَّهِ عَلَى النَّاسِ حِجُّ الْبَيْتِ مَنِ اسْتَطَاعَ إِلَيْهِ سَبِيلًا وَمَن كَفَرَ فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ غَنِيٌّ عَنِ الْعَالَمِينَ
versets.tr: fīhi āyātun bayyinātun maqāmu ibrāhīma wa-man dakhalahu kāna āminan wa-lillāhi ʿalā n-nāsi ḥijju l-bayti mani staṭāʿa ilayhi sabīlan wa-man kafara fa-inna llāha ghaniyyun ʿani l-ʿālamīn
versets.trad: Therein are manifest signs, [among them] the station of Abraham. Whoever enters it is in safety. And it is a duty owed to God, for all those who have the means, to make the pilgrimage to the House. As for the one who disbelieves, God has no need of the worlds...
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī reads the "signs" with the inner eye: these signs are not grasped by the glances of the head, but by the visions of hearts; the station of Abraham, outwardly, is the imprint of his foot — by allusion, it is the place where the Friend stood by his aspirations. As for "whoever enters it is in safety": the station of Abraham is surrender (taslīm); whoever has surrendered his affairs to God has no choice left, and safety is the obverse of fear. And he distinguishes two pilgrimages: one that seeks the House by its steps, the other that seeks the Master of the House by its heart — what a distance between the two!

mots.racine: ق و م
mots.def: maqām: a place where one stands.

mots.racine: د خ ل / أ م ن
mots.sens: whoever enters it is in safety

mots.racine: ح ج ج / ط و ع
mots.sens: the pilgrimage for whoever has the means
mots.def: ḥajj: pilgrimage; istaṭāʿa: to have the capacity.

mots.racine: غ ن ي
mots.sens: rich, having no need of the worlds

versets.ar: قُلْ يَا أَهْلَ الْكِتَابِ لِمَ تَكْفُرُونَ بِآيَاتِ اللَّهِ وَاللَّهُ شَهِيدٌ عَلَىٰ مَا تَعْمَلُونَ
versets.tr: qul yā ahla l-kitābi lima takfurūna bi-āyāti llāhi wa-llāhu shahīdun ʿalā mā taʿmalūn
versets.trad: Say: "O People of the Book! Why do you reject the signs of God, when God is witness to what you do?"
themes: the summons to the People of the Book
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī holds together the call and the decree: this address establishes the argument against them; yet from the standpoint of reality and compulsion, it closes upon them every escape: they are summoned by the Law and the command, set aside by the judgement and the constraint.

mots.racine: أ ه ل / ك ت ب
mots.def: ahl al-kitāb: the holders of Scripture.

mots.racine: ش ه د
mots.def: shahīd: one who sees and attests.

versets.ar: قُلْ يَا أَهْلَ الْكِتَابِ لِمَ تَصُدُّونَ عَن سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ مَنْ آمَنَ تَبْغُونَهَا عِوَجًا وَأَنتُمْ شُهَدَاءُ وَمَا اللَّهُ بِغَافِلٍ عَمَّا تَعْمَلُونَ
versets.tr: qul yā ahla l-kitābi lima taṣuddūna ʿan sabīli llāhi man āmana tabghūnahā ʿiwajan wa-antum shuhadāʾu wa-ma llāhu bi-ghāfilin ʿammā taʿmalūn
versets.trad: Say: "O People of the Book! Why do you bar the believer from the path of God, seeking to make it crooked, while you yourselves are witnesses?" And God is not heedless of what you do.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī condenses it into a luminous question: how could one who is himself barred bar the road to another? Therein lies, indeed, a secret of lordship. None turns another aside save the one whom the decree has already turned away.

mots.racine: ص د د / س ب ل
mots.sens: you bar from the path of God

mots.racine: ب غ ي / ع و ج
mots.sens: you seek to make it crooked

versets.ar: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا إِن تُطِيعُوا فَرِيقًا مِّنَ الَّذِينَ أُوتُوا الْكِتَابَ يَرُدُّوكُم بَعْدَ إِيمَانِكُمْ كَافِرِينَ
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā lladhīna āmanū in tuṭīʿū farīqan mina lladhīna ūtū l-kitāba yaruddūkum baʿda īmānikum kāfirīn
versets.trad: O you who believe! If you obey a group of those who have received the Scriptures, they will turn you into disbelievers after you have believed.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī makes "desolation" (waḥsha) a contagion: desolation does not remain attached to its hosts alone; it overflows upon whoever prowls about them. And he warns: whoever obeys the enemy of God, through the ill omen of keeping company with adversaries, is hurled by him into his abyss.

mots.racine: ط و ع / ف ر ق
mots.def: aṭāʿa: to obey; farīq: a faction.

mots.racine: ر د د / ك ف ر
mots.sens: they turn you into disbelievers after your faith
mots.def: radda: to make return, to turn back.

versets.ar: وَكَيْفَ تَكْفُرُونَ وَأَنتُمْ تُتْلَىٰ عَلَيْكُمْ آيَاتُ اللَّهِ وَفِيكُمْ رَسُولُهُ وَمَن يَعْتَصِم بِاللَّهِ فَقَدْ هُدِيَ إِلَىٰ صِرَاطٍ مُّسْتَقِيمٍ
versets.tr: wa-kayfa takfurūna wa-antum tutlā ʿalaykum āyātu llāhi wa-fīkum rasūluhu wa-man yaʿtaṣim billāhi fa-qad hudiya ilā ṣirāṭin mustaqīm
versets.trad: How could you disbelieve while the verses of God are recited to you and His Messenger is among you? Whoever holds fast to God is guided to a straight path.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: it is not fitting that unbelief should cast its shadow over one in whose heart the suns of gnosis have risen; for when day comes from one side, night withdraws from the other. And on holding fast: none holds fast to God save one who has received from God the safeguarding; the first guidance founds your final adherence — it is not your adherence that produces the guidance. He reports the saying: "I seek refuge in You from You."

mots.racine: ت ل و
mots.sens: the verses of God are recited to you

mots.racine: ع ص م
mots.def: iʿtaṣama: to cling, to be preserved through.

mots.racine: ص ر ط / ق و م
mots.def: ṣirāṭ mustaqīm: a way without crookedness.

versets.ar: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا اتَّقُوا اللَّهَ حَقَّ تُقَاتِهِ وَلَا تَمُوتُنَّ إِلَّا وَأَنتُم مُّسْلِمُونَ
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā lladhīna āmanu ttaqū llāha ḥaqqa tuqātihi wa-lā tamūtunna illā wa-antum muslimūn
versets.trad: O you who believe! Fear God as He ought to be feared. And do not die save as those submitted to Him (muslimūn).
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī unfolds the degrees of true fear: to avoid first the misstep, then heedlessness, then to purify oneself of every inclination, then of every blemish; and when you guard yourself even from contemplating your own fear after having been adorned with it, then you have feared God as He ought to be feared. And: true fear is the rejection of disobedience, the abolition of forgetfulness, the keeping of covenants, the vision of the divinity, the stripping away of the judgements of humanity, and extinction beneath the flowing of the decree.

mots.racine: و ق ي
mots.sens: fear God as He ought to be feared

mots.racine: م و ت / س ل م
mots.sens: do not die save as those submitted

versets.ar: وَاعْتَصِمُوا بِحَبْلِ اللَّهِ جَمِيعًا وَلَا تَفَرَّقُوا وَاذْكُرُوا نِعْمَتَ اللَّهِ عَلَيْكُمْ إِذْ كُنتُمْ أَعْدَاءً فَأَلَّفَ بَيْنَ قُلُوبِكُمْ فَأَصْبَحْتُم بِنِعْمَتِهِ إِخْوَانًا وَكُنتُمْ عَلَىٰ شَفَا حُفْرَةٍ مِّنَ النَّارِ فَأَنقَذَكُم مِّنْهَا كَذَٰلِكَ يُبَيِّنُ اللَّهُ لَكُمْ آيَاتِهِ لَعَلَّكُمْ تَهْتَدُونَ
versets.tr: wa-ʿtaṣimū bi-ḥabli llāhi jamīʿan wa-lā tafarraqū wa-dhkurū niʿmata llāhi ʿalaykum idh kuntum aʿdāʾan fa-allafa bayna qulūbikum fa-aṣbaḥtum bi-niʿmatihi ikhwānan wa-kuntum ʿalā shafā ḥufratin mina n-nāri fa-anqadhakum minhā kadhālika yubayyinu llāhu lakum āyātihi laʿallakum tahtadūn
versets.trad: And hold fast, all together, to the rope of God, and do not divide; remember the favours with which God has blessed you: you were enemies and God reconciled your hearts; by His grace you became brethren. You were on the brink of an abyss of fire and He delivered you from it. Thus does God make His signs clear to you; that perhaps you may be rightly guided...
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: to hold fast to His rope is to keep to the traces of the glorious intermediary, through realization and through adherence to the Book and the Sunna. He meditates on union: He united your hearts by delivering you from the captivity of created things; your aims became one — and a thousand men bent toward a single object are, in reality, one. Division (tafriqa) is the harshest of chastisements, the twin sister of association; and the deliverance "from the brink of the abyss" is accomplished by the light of contentment and extinction beneath the decree.

mots.racine: ع ص م / ح ب ل
mots.sens: hold fast to the rope of God
mots.def: ḥabl: rope, bond; here the Book and the Sunna.

mots.racine: أ ل ف
mots.def: allafa: to reconcile, to bring close.

mots.racine: ش ف و / ح ف ر
mots.sens: on the brink of an abyss of fire
mots.def: shafā: the edge; ḥufra: a pit.

versets.ar: وَلْتَكُن مِّنكُمْ أُمَّةٌ يَدْعُونَ إِلَى الْخَيْرِ وَيَأْمُرُونَ بِالْمَعْرُوفِ وَيَنْهَوْنَ عَنِ الْمُنكَرِ وَأُولَٰئِكَ هُمُ الْمُفْلِحُونَ
versets.tr: wa-l-takun minkum ummatun yadʿūna ilā l-khayri wa-yaʾmurūna bi-l-maʿrūfi wa-yanhawna ʿani l-munkari wa-ulāʾika humu l-mufliḥūn
versets.trad: Let there arise among you a Community whose members call to the good, enjoin what is right and forbid what is wrong. These are the felicitous!
themes: the community that calls to the good
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī designates an elite: men who have risen through God for God, whom no blame of any blamer turns aside, whom no indulgence tears away from God; they have devoted their whole being to the indications of His command, shortened their breaths and consumed their lives in winning His good pleasure. They laboured for God, counselled the religion for God, called His creation toward God — their commerce prospered, and their market suffered no loss.

mots.racine: أ م م / د ع و
mots.sens: a community that calls to the good

mots.racine: أ م ر / ع ر ف / ن ك ر
mots.sens: enjoin the right, forbid the wrong
mots.def: maʿrūf: the recognized good; munkar: the reproved.

mots.racine: ف ل ح

versets.ar: وَلَا تَكُونُوا كَالَّذِينَ تَفَرَّقُوا وَاخْتَلَفُوا مِن بَعْدِ مَا جَاءَهُمُ الْبَيِّنَاتُ وَأُولَٰئِكَ لَهُمْ عَذَابٌ عَظِيمٌ
versets.tr: wa-lā takūnū ka-lladhīna tafarraqū wa-khtalafū min baʿdi mā jāʾahumu l-bayyinātu wa-ulāʾika lahum ʿadhābun ʿaẓīm
versets.trad: Do not be like those who divided and disputed among themselves after the clear proofs had come to them. For them is a mighty chastisement.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: these are a people upon whom appeared at the beginning the inscriptions of the quest, then whom He branded at the end with the searing seal of separation; they passed the night in the camp of the beloved, and awoke at morning among the company of strangers. Discord after the evidence is the fall outside the circle of intimacy.

mots.racine: ف ر ق / خ ل ف
mots.sens: like those who divided and disputed
mots.def: tafarraqa: to split; ikhtalafa: to diverge.

mots.racine: ع ذ ب / ع ظ م

versets.ar: يَوْمَ تَبْيَضُّ وُجُوهٌ وَتَسْوَدُّ وُجُوهٌ فَأَمَّا الَّذِينَ اسْوَدَّتْ وُجُوهُهُمْ أَكَفَرْتُم بَعْدَ إِيمَانِكُمْ فَذُوقُوا الْعَذَابَ بِمَا كُنتُمْ تَكْفُرُونَ
versets.tr: yawma tabyaḍḍu wujūhun wa-taswaddu wujūhun fa-ammā lladhīna swaddat wujūhuhum a-kafartum baʿda īmānikum fa-dhūqū l-ʿadhāba bimā kuntum takfurūn
versets.trad: [There shall come] the Day when some faces will be whitened and others blackened. To those whose faces are blackened it will be said: "Did you disbelieve after having believed? Then taste the chastisement for your unbelief!"
themes: the faces on the Day of Judgement
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī reads whiteness and blackness as states of the heart already here below: the people of vain pretensions have a blackened face, the people of true meanings a whitened face; the men of unveilings will tomorrow see their faces radiant with light, and the people of the veil will see theirs blackened by occultation. And: whoever whitens his heart today will tomorrow see his face whitened.

mots.racine: ب ي ض / س و د
mots.def: ibyaḍḍa: to become white; iswadda: to blacken.

mots.racine: ذ و ق / ع ذ ب

versets.ar: وَأَمَّا الَّذِينَ ابْيَضَّتْ وُجُوهُهُمْ فَفِي رَحْمَةِ اللَّهِ هُمْ فِيهَا خَالِدُونَ
versets.tr: wa-ammā lladhīna byaḍḍat wujūhuhum fa-fī raḥmati llāhi hum fīhā khālidūn
versets.trad: But those whose faces are whitened shall dwell in the mercy of God, abiding therein forever.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: whoever turned away from creation in the moment of his trials saw his face whitened by the spirit of trusting surrender (tafwīḍ); whoever suspended his heart upon other-than-God in his need saw his face blackened by the dust of craving. Thus the first are in familiarity and respite, and the second in trial and lamentation.

mots.racine: ب ي ض
mots.def: a sign of the light of faith.

mots.ar: فَفِي رَحْمَةِ اللَّهِ هُمْ فِيهَا خَالِدُونَ
mots.tr: fa-fī raḥmati llāhi hum fīhā khālidūn
mots.racine: ر ح م / خ ل د
mots.sens: in the mercy of God, abiding forever

versets.ar: تِلْكَ آيَاتُ اللَّهِ نَتْلُوهَا عَلَيْكَ بِالْحَقِّ وَمَا اللَّهُ يُرِيدُ ظُلْمًا لِّلْعَالَمِينَ
versets.tr: tilka āyātu llāhi natlūhā ʿalayka bi-l-ḥaqqi wa-ma llāhu yurīdu ẓulman li-l-ʿālamīn
versets.trad: Such are the signs of God. We recite them to you in all truth. And God desires no injustice for the worlds.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī hears in it a tenderness of intimacy: boon-companion of Our discourse with you, at every instant, in little and in much — this is the discourse of love that endures. And on the absence of injustice: how could injustice be conceivable in His decision or His act, when all creation is His creation and the judgement passed upon it is His judgement?

mots.racine: ت ل و / ح ق ق
mots.sens: We recite them to you in truth

mots.racine: ر و د / ظ ل م

versets.ar: وَلِلَّهِ مَا فِي السَّمَاوَاتِ وَمَا فِي الْأَرْضِ وَإِلَى اللَّهِ تُرْجَعُ الْأُمُورُ
versets.tr: wa-lillāhi mā fī s-samāwāti wa-mā fī l-arḍi wa-ilā llāhi turjaʿu l-umūr
versets.trad: To God belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth. And to God all matters are brought back.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī condenses it: to God belongs whatever is in the heavens and on the earth — in ownership; and to God all matters return — in judgement. Possession and return are two faces of a single lordship: nothing escapes Him, nothing remains outside His hand.

mots.ar: وَلِلَّهِ مَا فِي السَّمَاوَاتِ وَمَا فِي الْأَرْضِ
mots.tr: wa-lillāhi mā fī s-samāwāti wa-mā fī l-arḍ
mots.racine: س م و / أ ر ض
mots.sens: to God belongs what is in the heavens and on the earth

mots.racine: ر ج ع / أ م ر
mots.sens: to God all matters are brought back

versets.ar: كُنتُمْ خَيْرَ أُمَّةٍ أُخْرِجَتْ لِلنَّاسِ تَأْمُرُونَ بِالْمَعْرُوفِ وَتَنْهَوْنَ عَنِ الْمُنكَرِ وَتُؤْمِنُونَ بِاللَّهِ وَلَوْ آمَنَ أَهْلُ الْكِتَابِ لَكَانَ خَيْرًا لَّهُم مِّنْهُمُ الْمُؤْمِنُونَ وَأَكْثَرُهُمُ الْفَاسِقُونَ
versets.tr: kuntum khayra ummatin ukhrijat li-n-nāsi taʾmurūna bi-l-maʿrūfi wa-tanhawna ʿani l-munkari wa-tuʾminūna billāhi wa-law āmana ahlu l-kitābi la-kāna khayran lahum minhumu l-muʾminūna wa-aktharuhumu l-fāsiqūn
versets.trad: You are the best Community brought forth for mankind: you enjoin what is right, you forbid what is wrong, and you believe in God. Had the People of the Book believed, it would have been better for them. Among them are believers, but most of them are depraved.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī founds the excellence not upon the number of acts but upon election: this community is the best because its Prophet is the noblest of the prophets; its superiority lies not in the abundance of its prayers and acts of worship, but in the surplus of its turning (iqbāl) toward God and in His particular election of it. And he unveils: the right is the service of the Real and the preference given to His due; the wrong is the company of the ego and the choosing of the soul's portion. The right is what brings you near to Him, the wrong what veils you from Him.

mots.racine: خ ي ر / أ م م / خ ر ج
mots.sens: the best community brought forth for mankind

mots.racine: أ م ن
ك ث ر / ف س ق
mots.sens: and most of them are depraved

versets.ar: لَن يَضُرُّوكُمْ إِلَّا أَذًى وَإِن يُقَاتِلُوكُمْ يُوَلُّوكُمُ الْأَدْبَارَ ثُمَّ لَا يُنصَرُونَ
versets.tr: lan yaḍurrūkum illā adhan wa-in yuqātilūkum yuwallūkumu l-adbāra thumma lā yunṣarūn
versets.trad: They will do you no harm beyond some annoyance. Should they fight you, they will turn their backs upon you; and thereafter they shall not be helped.
themes: the protection of the friends of God
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the Real grants enemies power over His friends only to the measure in which He wills to make sincere their flight toward Him; and when their flight is true, He honours their resting-place with Him. As for the adversaries: if they vaunt themselves against the friends according to their illusion, the affair turns back upon them in abasement and humiliation.

mots.racine: ض ر ر / أ ذ ي
mots.sens: they will harm you only with annoyance

mots.racine: و ل ي / د ب ر
mots.sens: they will turn their backs upon you
mots.def: wallā l-dubur: to take to flight.

versets.ar: ضُرِبَتْ عَلَيْهِمُ الذِّلَّةُ أَيْنَ مَا ثُقِفُوا إِلَّا بِحَبْلٍ مِّنَ اللَّهِ وَحَبْلٍ مِّنَ النَّاسِ وَبَاءُوا بِغَضَبٍ مِّنَ اللَّهِ وَضُرِبَتْ عَلَيْهِمُ الْمَسْكَنَةُ ذَٰلِكَ بِأَنَّهُمْ كَانُوا يَكْفُرُونَ بِآيَاتِ اللَّهِ وَيَقْتُلُونَ الْأَنبِيَاءَ بِغَيْرِ حَقٍّ ذَٰلِكَ بِمَا عَصَوا وَّكَانُوا يَعْتَدُونَ
versets.tr: ḍuribat ʿalayhimu dh-dhillatu ayna mā thuqifū illā bi-ḥablin mina llāhi wa-ḥablin mina n-nāsi wa-bāʾū bi-ghaḍabin mina llāhi wa-ḍuribat ʿalayhimu l-maskanatu dhālika bi-annahum kānū yakfurūna bi-āyāti llāhi wa-yaqtulūna l-anbiyāʾa bi-ghayri ḥaqqin dhālika bimā ʿaṣaw wa-kānū yaʿtadūn
versets.trad: Abasement has been stamped upon them wherever they are found, save those held by a rope from God and a rope from men. They incurred the wrath of God, and wretchedness was stamped upon them — this because they denied the signs of God and slew the prophets without right, and because they disobeyed and transgressed.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī makes this debasement a legible sign: the mark of abandonment cannot be hidden, the imprint of estrangement cannot be concealed, the token of rupture cannot be veiled; behold them in the smallness of rejection and the humiliation of refusal — the men of vision take a lesson from it, while their fellows among the apostates let themselves be deluded by it.

mots.racine: ض ر ب / ذ ل ل
ب و أ / غ ض ب
mots.sens: they incurred the wrath of God

mots.racine: ق ت ل / ن ب أ
mots.sens: and slay the prophets without right

versets.ar: لَيْسُوا سَوَاءً مِّنْ أَهْلِ الْكِتَابِ أُمَّةٌ قَائِمَةٌ يَتْلُونَ آيَاتِ اللَّهِ آنَاءَ اللَّيْلِ وَهُمْ يَسْجُدُونَ
versets.tr: laysū sawāʾan min ahli l-kitābi ummatun qāʾimatun yatlūna āyāti llāhi ānāʾa l-layli wa-hum yasjudūn
versets.trad: They are not all alike: among the People of the Book there is an upright community whose members recite the verses of God through the watches of the night and prostrate themselves.
themes: the upright community that keeps vigil
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī marks the irreducible gulf between states: as He has set light and darkness in an opposition of contraries, He has fixed the incompatibility between the states of the friends and those of the enemies. When then could clarity and obscurity be equal, certainty and suspicion, union and rupture, the one who abides upon the carpet and the one who turns away from the door? Like night and day, they never meet.

mots.racine: س و ي
أ م م / ق و م
س ج د
mots.sens: through the watches of the night, prostrating

versets.ar: يُؤْمِنُونَ بِاللَّهِ وَالْيَوْمِ الْآخِرِ وَيَأْمُرُونَ بِالْمَعْرُوفِ وَيَنْهَوْنَ عَنِ الْمُنكَرِ وَيُسَارِعُونَ فِي الْخَيْرَاتِ وَأُولَٰئِكَ مِنَ الصَّالِحِينَ
versets.tr: yuʾminūna billāhi wa-l-yawmi l-ākhiri wa-yaʾmurūna bi-l-maʿrūfi wa-yanhawna ʿani l-munkari wa-yusāriʿūna fī l-khayrāti wa-ulāʾika mina ṣ-ṣāliḥīn
versets.trad: They believe in God and the Last Day, they enjoin what is right, they forbid what is wrong, and they vie with one another in good works. These are among the righteous.
versets.commentaire: In continuation of the preceding verse, Qushayrī ranks these among the men of the high plateau and of intimacy, those who hasten toward the good because their hearts can no longer bear to wait far from the Real. The rushing to good works is the sign of a soul outpaced by its thirst for the Presence.

mots.racine: أ م ن / أ خ ر
mots.sens: they believe in God and the Last Day
mots.def: al-yawm al-ākhir: the life to come.

mots.racine: س ر ع / خ ي ر
mots.sens: they vie with one another in good works

mots.racine: ص ل ح

versets.ar: وَمَا يَفْعَلُوا مِنْ خَيْرٍ فَلَن يُكْفَرُوهُ وَاللَّهُ عَلِيمٌ بِالْمُتَّقِينَ
versets.tr: wa-mā yafʿalū min khayrin fa-lan yukfarūhu wa-llāhu ʿalīmun bi-l-muttaqīn
versets.trad: Whatever good they do shall not be denied them, for God knows perfectly those who fear Him.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī makes this verse a promise of absolute generosity: no aspirant shall be disappointed at His door, no merchant shall suffer loss with Him, no companion shall know desolation in His company, no seeker shall be humiliated before Him. Whoever turns toward Him never returns empty-handed.

mots.racine: ف ع ل / خ ي ر
ك ف ر
mots.sens: it shall not be denied them

mots.racine: ع ل م / و ق ي
mots.sens: knowing of those who fear Him

versets.ar: إِنَّ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا لَن تُغْنِيَ عَنْهُمْ أَمْوَالُهُمْ وَلَا أَوْلَادُهُم مِّنَ اللَّهِ شَيْئًا وَأُولَٰئِكَ أَصْحَابُ النَّارِ هُمْ فِيهَا خَالِدُونَ
versets.tr: inna lladhīna kafarū lan tughniya ʿanhum amwāluhum wa-lā awlāduhum mina llāhi shayʾan wa-ulāʾika aṣḥābu n-nāri hum fīhā khālidūn
versets.trad: Truly, as for the disbelievers, neither their wealth nor their children shall avail them anything against God. These shall be the inhabitants of the Fire, abiding therein forever.
themes: the vanity of wealth and children
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī seals their loss on both sides: neither in the present moment do they have a substitute, nor in what is to come a replacement; in their present they have lost, and in their future await rupture and exile, trial and loss, chastisement and denial. Wealth and children, where the ego seeks refuge, hold back nothing when the decree comes.

mots.ar: لَن تُغْنِيَ عَنْهُمْ أَمْوَالُهُمْ وَلَا أَوْلَادُهُم
mots.tr: lan tughniya ʿanhum amwāluhum wa-lā awlāduhum
mots.racine: غ ن ي / م و ل / و ل د
mots.sens: neither wealth nor children shall avail them

mots.racine: ص ح ب / ن و ر / خ ل د
mots.sens: inhabitants of the Fire, abiding therein forever
mots.def: aṣḥāb al-nār: the people of the Fire.

versets.ar: مَثَلُ مَا يُنفِقُونَ فِي هَٰذِهِ الْحَيَاةِ الدُّنْيَا كَمَثَلِ رِيحٍ فِيهَا صِرٌّ أَصَابَتْ حَرْثَ قَوْمٍ ظَلَمُوا أَنفُسَهُمْ فَأَهْلَكَتْهُ وَمَا ظَلَمَهُمُ اللَّهُ وَلَٰكِنْ أَنفُسَهُمْ يَظْلِمُونَ
versets.tr: mathalu mā yunfiqūna fī hādhihi l-ḥayāti d-dunyā ka-mathali rīḥin fīhā ṣirrun aṣābat ḥartha qawmin ẓalamū anfusahum fa-ahlakathu wa-mā ẓalamahumu llāhu wa-lākin anfusahum yaẓlimūn
versets.trad: What men spend in this lower life is like a wind bearing bitter frost that smites the harvest of a people who have wronged themselves, and destroys it. God did them no wrong, but it is they who wronged themselves.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: from all they lavished upon other than God they gathered nothing but regrets that follow one upon another, and from their illusions they obtained nothing but trials that link one to the next — such is the recompense of one who has turned away and turned his back. Spending without the face turned toward God is like a crop that the icy wind mows down.

mots.racine: م ث ل / ر و ح / ص ر ر
mots.sens: like a wind bearing bitter frost

mots.racine: ص و ب / ح ر ث / ظ ل م
mots.sens: smites the harvest of a people who wronged themselves

mots.racine: ظ ل م / ن ف س
mots.sens: but it is themselves they wrong

versets.ar: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا لَا تَتَّخِذُوا بِطَانَةً مِّن دُونِكُمْ لَا يَأْلُونَكُمْ خَبَالًا وَدُّوا مَا عَنِتُّمْ قَدْ بَدَتِ الْبَغْضَاءُ مِنْ أَفْوَاهِهِمْ وَمَا تُخْفِي صُدُورُهُمْ أَكْبَرُ قَدْ بَيَّنَّا لَكُمُ الْآيَاتِ إِن كُنتُمْ تَعْقِلُونَ
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā lladhīna āmanū lā tattakhidhū biṭānatan min dūnikum lā yaʾlūnakum khabālan waddū mā ʿanittum qad badati l-baghḍāʾu min afwāhihim wa-mā tukhfī ṣudūruhum akbaru qad bayyannā lakumu l-āyāti in kuntum taʿqilūn
versets.trad: O you who believe! Do not take as confidants those who are not of you; they will spare no effort to corrupt you, rejoicing in your hardships. Hatred has already shown itself upon their mouths, but what their breasts conceal is fouler still. We have made the signs clear to you; if only you would understand!
themes: not taking the enemy as confidant
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: to lean upon the adversary, after the trials have become clear, is to aid him against oneself by means that the enemy's own cunning could not attain. Thus the Real counsels the believers to guard themselves, to manifest the rupture with every other, and to keep pure sincerity toward Him in heart and in secret. And: the enmity of these people toward the Messenger is original, not passing — how could it be otherwise? He is the locus of orientation, they the locus of turning away; and when then would night and day ever meet?

mots.racine: أ خ ذ / ب ط ن
mots.sens: do not take confidants from outside your own
mots.def: biṭāna: an intimate, a keeper of the secret.

mots.racine: ب د و / ب غ ض / ف و ه
mots.sens: hatred has appeared upon their mouths

mots.racine: خ ف ي / ص د ر / ك ب ر
mots.sens: what their breasts conceal is worse

versets.ar: هَا أَنتُمْ أُولَاءِ تُحِبُّونَهُمْ وَلَا يُحِبُّونَكُمْ وَتُؤْمِنُونَ بِالْكِتَابِ كُلِّهِ وَإِذَا لَقُوكُمْ قَالُوا آمَنَّا وَإِذَا خَلَوْا عَضُّوا عَلَيْكُمُ الْأَنَامِلَ مِنَ الْغَيْظِ قُلْ مُوتُوا بِغَيْظِكُمْ إِنَّ اللَّهَ عَلِيمٌ بِذَاتِ الصُّدُورِ
versets.tr: hā antum ulāʾi tuḥibbūnahum wa-lā yuḥibbūnakum wa-tuʾminūna bi-l-kitābi kullihi wa-idhā laqūkum qālū āmannā wa-idhā khalaw ʿaḍḍū ʿalaykumu l-anāmila mina l-ghayẓi qul mūtū bi-ghayẓikum inna llāha ʿalīmun bi-dhāti ṣ-ṣudūr
versets.trad: Here is your situation: these people, you love them but they do not love you, and you believe in the whole of the Book. When they meet you they say: "We believe!", but when they withdraw they bite their fingertips in rage against you. Say: "Die in your rage! God knows what the breasts contain!"
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī sets the limpidity of the believers against the blackness of their hearts: you, through your nobility, your hearts are purified of disturbances, so that compassion overtakes you toward them; and they, through their hardness and treachery, lay for you every snare they can. So, O Muḥammad, empty your heart of them. And on "die in your rage": leave them alone to endure the fire of their spite, and rest your heart from what befalls them; for God is worthier of His servants, conveying to whom He wills what He wills.

mots.racine: ح ب ب
mots.sens: you love them but they do not love you

mots.racine: ع ض ض / ن م ل / غ ي ظ
mots.sens: they bite their fingertips in rage

mots.racine: م و ت / غ ي ظ

versets.ar: إِن تَمْسَسْكُمْ حَسَنَةٌ تَسُؤْهُمْ وَإِن تُصِبْكُمْ سَيِّئَةٌ يَفْرَحُوا بِهَا وَإِن تَصْبِرُوا وَتَتَّقُوا لَا يَضُرُّكُمْ كَيْدُهُمْ شَيْئًا إِنَّ اللَّهَ بِمَا يَعْمَلُونَ مُحِيطٌ
versets.tr: in tamsaskum ḥasanatun tasuʾhum wa-in tuṣibkum sayyiʾatun yafraḥū bihā wa-in taṣbirū wa-tattaqū lā yaḍurrukum kayduhum shayʾan inna llāha bimā yaʿmalūna muḥīṭ
versets.trad: If good fortune befalls you, it grieves them, and if misfortune strikes you, they rejoice at it. But if you are patient and fear God, their cunning will not harm you at all; for God encompasses with His knowledge all that they do.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī applies the verse to the deserters of the Way: those who have turned aside from the path of aspiration, returned to the states of the people of habit, to whom it is displeasing that an aspirant should attain; and when they see the faltering of a wayfarer, they take their ease in it. Then the luminous promise: God, through His grace and His favour, perfects His light upon the people of His solicitude, and leaves the unjust who deviate from His way in the chastisement of their estrangement, with no care for what awaits them. Patience and the fear of God render the enemy's cunning vain.

mots.racine: م س س / ح س ن / س و أ
mots.sens: if good touches you, it grieves them
mots.def: massa: to touch; sāʾa: to grieve.

mots.racine: ص ب ر / و ق ي
mots.sens: if you are patient and fear God
mots.def: ṣabara: to endure; ittaqā: to be on guard.

mots.racine: ح و ط / ع م ل
mots.def: muḥīṭ: one who encompasses with His knowledge.

versets.ar: وَإِذْ غَدَوْتَ مِنْ أَهْلِكَ تُبَوِّئُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ مَقَاعِدَ لِلْقِتَالِ ۗ وَاللَّهُ سَمِيعٌ عَلِيمٌ
versets.tr: wa-idh ghadawta min ahlika tubawwiʾu l-muʾminīna maqāʿida li-l-qitāli wa-llāhu samīʿun ʿalīmun
versets.trad: And so it was when, at daybreak, you departed from your household to assign the believers their stations for the battle - God is the All-Hearing, the All-Knowing,
versets.commentaire: This verse recalls the morning of Uḥud, when the Prophet stationed the believers in their places of battle. Qushayrī sees in it less a strategy than a decree: the Prophet acts upon command, yet the true pivot of every matter remains God's decree and His foreordainment. The lesson lies not in the effort expended, but in the manner in which God sets His decrees in motion and exercises His choosing. Man disposes himself; God disposes all.

mots.racine: غ د و
mots.def: ghadā: to depart at the break of day.

mots.racine: ب و أ
mots.def: bawwaʾa: to install, to give a place.

mots.racine: ق ع د / ق ت ل

versets.ar: إِذْ هَمَّت طَّائِفَتَانِ مِنكُمْ أَن تَفْشَلَا وَاللَّهُ وَلِيُّهُمَا ۗ وَعَلَى اللَّهِ فَلْيَتَوَكَّلِ الْمُؤْمِنُونَ
versets.tr: idh hammat ṭāʾifatāni minkum an tafshalā wa-llāhu waliyyuhumā wa-ʿalā llāhi fa-l-yatawakkali l-muʾminūna
versets.trad: and when two of your companies were on the verge of losing heart, though God was the Protector of them both. So in God let the believers place their trust!
versets.commentaire: Two clans nearly yielded to discouragement, yet God was their Protector. Here Qushayrī unveils the secret of the Whole: creatures appear, upon the forestage of choice, as though the matter belonged to them — their affirming and their denying, their acting and their abstaining. In truth, however, they move only by the handling of the divine Hand and the turning of His power. Hence the call: let the believer entrust everything to the One who alone holds the hearts.

mots.racine: ه م م / ط و ف
mots.sens: two clans were on the verge
mots.def: hamma: to be on the point of.

mots.racine: ف ش ل
mots.def: fashila: to weaken, to lose courage.

mots.racine: و ل ي

versets.ar: وَلَقَدْ نَصَرَكُمُ اللَّهُ بِبَدْرٍ وَأَنتُمْ أَذِلَّةٌ ۖ فَاتَّقُوا اللَّهَ لَعَلَّكُمْ تَشْكُرُونَ
versets.tr: wa-laqad naṣarakumu llāhu bi-badrin wa-antum adhillatun fa-ttaqū llāha laʿallakum tashkurūna
versets.trad: God did indeed succour you at Badr, when you were in a position of weakness. So be mindful of God! Perhaps you will be thankful...
versets.commentaire: The memory of Badr, where succour came despite the believers' weakness, is here summoned. Qushayrī teaches that to recall a favour already granted is to open the door to the pressing plea for the like of it in time to come. To evoke past grace is to incline the heart toward the grace yet to come; and mindfulness of God is the threshold of gratitude.

mots.racine: ن ص ر
ب د ر
mots.def: site of the first great victory.

mots.racine: ذ ل ل

versets.ar: إِذْ تَقُولُ لِلْمُؤْمِنِينَ أَلَن يَكْفِيَكُمْ أَن يُمِدَّكُمْ رَبُّكُم بِثَلَاثَةِ آلَافٍ مِّنَ الْمَلَائِكَةِ مُنزَلِينَ
versets.tr: idh taqūlu li-l-muʾminīna a-lan yakfiyakum an yumiddakum rabbukum bi-thalāthati ālāfin mina l-malāʾikati munzalīna
versets.trad: When you said to the believers: "Is it not enough for you that your Lord should reinforce you by sending down three thousand of His angels to your aid?"
versets.commentaire: The Prophet announces the reinforcement of three thousand angels. Qushayrī distinguishes two registers of soothing: the calm that God bestows upon the heart of the Chosen One comes without intermediary, directly from God; whereas the steadying of the believers' hearts passes through the agency of the Messenger. And he adds with subtlety: had there not remained in them some trace of weakness, He would not, in the discourse of succour, have brought them back to the descent of the angels — for the matter in its entirety is in the hands of the King alone.

mots.racine: ك ف ي
mots.sens: is it not enough for you

mots.racine: م د د
ث ل ث / م ل ك

versets.ar: بَلَىٰ ۚ إِن تَصْبِرُوا وَتَتَّقُوا وَيَأْتُوكُم مِّن فَوْرِهِمْ هَٰذَا يُمْدِدْكُمْ رَبُّكُم بِخَمْسَةِ آلَافٍ مِّنَ الْمَلَائِكَةِ مُسَوِّمِينَ
versets.tr: balā in taṣbirū wa-tattaqū wa-yaʾtūkum min fawrihim hādhā yumdidkum rabbukum bi-khamsati ālāfin mina l-malāʾikati musawwimīna
versets.trad: Yes indeed! If you are patient and mindful of God, and if your enemies fall upon you in a sudden assault, your Lord will reinforce you with five thousand of His angels, clearly marked.
versets.commentaire: The succour grows with fidelity: to patience and mindfulness is joined a reinforcement of five thousand angels. Qushayrī makes of this gradation a law of the path: whoever increases in readiness sees his aid increase; whoever diminishes sees his aid diminish. The measure of heavenly support matches exactly the measure of the heart's perseverance.

mots.def: particle of affirmation after a negation.

mots.racine: ص ب ر / و ق ي
mots.sens: if you are patient and mindful
mots.def: ṣabara: to be patient; ittaqā: to be mindful of God.

mots.racine: ف و ر
س و م
mots.def: sawwama: to mark with a sign.

versets.ar: وَمَا جَعَلَهُ اللَّهُ إِلَّا بُشْرَىٰ لَكُمْ وَلِتَطْمَئِنَّ قُلُوبُكُم بِهِ ۗ وَمَا النَّصْرُ إِلَّا مِنْ عِندِ اللَّهِ الْعَزِيزِ الْحَكِيمِ
versets.tr: wa-mā jaʿalahu llāhu illā bushrā lakum wa-li-taṭmaʾinna qulūbukum bihi wa-mā n-naṣru illā min ʿindi llāhi l-ʿazīzi l-ḥakīmi
versets.trad: God made this only as glad tidings for you, that by it your hearts might be set at rest - and victory comes from none but God, the Almighty, the All-Wise,
versets.commentaire: The reinforcement was but glad tidings to set the hearts at rest; true victory issues from none but God. Qushayrī sees here a constant law with the friends of God: when their resolves weaken, when their determination dwindles, or when their hearts draw near a halting-place of torpor, He shows them graces and manner of favours that fortify the causes of their knowledge and confirm the realities of their certitude. Then He cuts clean: "victory comes from none but God", severing the hearts from all other than Him.

mots.racine: ب ش ر
ط م ن
mots.sens: that your hearts may be at rest

mots.racine: ع ز ز / ح ك م

versets.ar: لِيَقْطَعَ طَرَفًا مِّنَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا أَوْ يَكْبِتَهُمْ فَيَنقَلِبُوا خَائِبِينَ
versets.tr: li-yaqṭaʿa ṭarafan mina lladhīna kafarū aw yakbitahum fa-yanqalibū khāʾibīna
versets.trad: that He might cut off a portion of those who disbelieved, or subdue them, so that they should withdraw in defeat.
versets.commentaire: The aim of the succour is to cut away a portion of the disbelievers or to abase them unto rout. Qushayrī reads in it the protective justice shown to the friends of God: God never delivers His intimates to the malicious gloating of their enemy; should the believer suffer a reverse, his adversary will inevitably be cast by God into trial and chastisement.

mots.racine: ق ط ع / ط ر ف
mots.def: qaṭaʿa: to sever, to cut away.

mots.racine: ك ب ت
mots.sens: to lower them, to subdue them

mots.racine: ق ل ب / خ ي ب

versets.ar: لَيْسَ لَكَ مِنَ الْأَمْرِ شَيْءٌ أَوْ يَتُوبَ عَلَيْهِمْ أَوْ يُعَذِّبَهُمْ فَإِنَّهُمْ ظَالِمُونَ
versets.tr: laysa laka mina l-amri shayʾun aw yatūba ʿalayhim aw yuʿadhdhibahum fa-innahum ẓālimūna
versets.trad: This matter is no concern of yours. It may be that God will turn to them in mercy, or that He will chastise them, for they are wrongdoers.
themes: the stripping bare of the Prophet
versets.commentaire: "You have no part in the matter": Qushayrī makes of this verse a summit of dispossession. Divinity belongs to the One who holds command and prohibition; having no equal in lordship, the Prophet likewise has no part in the command. And he specifies: God stripped him of all else, of every particle of self-interest and every claim, by announcing to him that he has nothing of the matter. If the master of the first and the last holds none of it, who then, of lesser rank, could lay claim to the least part of it?

mots.racine: أ م ر
mots.sens: you have nothing of the matter

mots.racine: ت و ب
ظ ل م

versets.ar: وَلِلَّهِ مَا فِي السَّمَاوَاتِ وَمَا فِي الْأَرْضِ ۚ يَغْفِرُ لِمَن يَشَاءُ وَيُعَذِّبُ مَن يَشَاءُ ۚ وَاللَّهُ غَفُورٌ رَّحِيمٌ
versets.tr: wa-li-llāhi mā fī s-samāwāti wa-mā fī l-arḍi yaghfiru li-man yashāʾu wa-yuʿadhdhibu man yashāʾu wa-llāhu ghafūrun raḥīmun
versets.trad: To God belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth. He forgives whom He wills, and He chastises whom He wills. God is forgiving, merciful.
themes: forgiveness and chastisement by His will
versets.commentaire: All that is in the heavens and on the earth belongs to God; He forgives and chastises according to His will. Qushayrī unfolds the consequence of the preceding verse: if the sovereignty is His sovereignty, the command His command, the judgement His judgement — then whom He wills He chastises, whom He wills He draws near, whom He wills He guides, whom He wills He leads astray. Nothing escapes His decree, and His mercy enfolds His command.

mots.racine: س م و
mots.sens: to God belongs what is in the heavens

mots.racine: غ ف ر / ش ي أ
غ ف ر / ر ح م

versets.ar: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا لَا تَأْكُلُوا الرِّبَا أَضْعَافًا مُّضَاعَفَةً ۖ وَاتَّقُوا اللَّهَ لَعَلَّكُمْ تُفْلِحُونَ
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā lladhīna āmanū lā taʾkulū r-ribā aḍʿāfan muḍāʿafatan wa-ttaqū llāha laʿallakum tufliḥūna
versets.trad: O you who believe! Do not feed upon usury, doubling and redoubling [the sum lent]. Be mindful of God! Perhaps you will prosper...
versets.commentaire: Usury is forbidden: to lend one and demand two. Qushayrī draws from this a subtle indication: God asks of His servant the loan of a single one, which He will repay him sevenfold and beyond, unto infinity; the allusion is that munificence does not befit the creature — it is the attribute of the Real. Infinite largesse belongs to God alone: the man who multiplies what is owed him usurps a trait that is not his.

mots.racine: أ ك ل / ر ب و
ض ع ف
ف ل ح
mots.def: aflaḥa: to thrive, to be saved.

versets.ar: وَاتَّقُوا النَّارَ الَّتِي أُعِدَّتْ لِلْكَافِرِينَ
versets.tr: wa-ttaqū n-nāra llatī uʿiddat li-l-kāfirīna
versets.trad: And guard yourselves against the Fire that has been prepared for the disbelievers.
themes: the implicit promise to the believer
versets.commentaire: "Guard yourselves against the Fire prepared for the disbelievers." From the very wording Qushayrī draws a consolation: the allusion of the discourse is that the believer shall not be chastised therein; and even were he chastised for a time, he shall not abide there eternally. The Fire is said to be destined for the deniers: which is to say that it is not the final abode of those who have believed.

mots.racine: و ق ي / ن و ر
ع د د / ك ف ر

versets.ar: وَأَطِيعُوا اللَّهَ وَالرَّسُولَ لَعَلَّكُمْ تُرْحَمُونَ
versets.tr: wa-aṭīʿū llāha wa-r-rasūla laʿallakum turḥamūna
versets.trad: Obey God and the Messenger; perhaps mercy will be shown to you...
themes: obedience to God and the Messenger
versets.commentaire: Obedience to the Messenger is joined to obedience to God. Qushayrī discerns in this a twofold design: He coupled obedience to the Messenger with obedience to Himself in order to honour his rank, and to lighten the burden of the community by bringing it back to the companionship of a man arisen from its own midst — for like finds greater repose in the presence of like. The gentleness of God draws near by passing through a messenger of our own kind.

mots.racine: ط و ع
ر س ل
ر ح م
mots.sens: you will be taken into mercy

versets.ar: وَسَارِعُوا إِلَىٰ مَغْفِرَةٍ مِّن رَّبِّكُمْ وَجَنَّةٍ عَرْضُهَا السَّمَاوَاتُ وَالْأَرْضُ أُعِدَّتْ لِلْمُتَّقِينَ
versets.tr: wa-sāriʿū ilā maghfiratin min rabbikum wa-jannatin ʿarḍuhā s-samāwātu wa-l-arḍu uʿiddat li-l-muttaqīna
versets.trad: Hasten toward forgiveness from your Lord and toward a Garden as vast as the heavens and the earth, prepared for those mindful of God,
themes: the degrees of the race toward God
versets.commentaire: "Hasten toward forgiveness." As hearts took fright at a demand seemingly too harsh, the Prophet set them at ease: "remorse is repentance." Qushayrī apportions men within this race: the worshippers hasten by their steps in deeds; the knowers hasten by their aspirations in drawings-near; the sinners hasten by their remorse, drinking the cups of regret. And he concludes: whoever hastens by his steps finds his reward; whoever hastens by his aspiration finds his nearness; whoever hastens by his remorse finds His mercy.

mots.racine: س ر ع
غ ف ر / ر ب ب
ج ن ن / ع ر ض
mots.sens: a Garden as vast as the heavens

versets.ar: الَّذِينَ يُنفِقُونَ فِي السَّرَّاءِ وَالضَّرَّاءِ وَالْكَاظِمِينَ الْغَيْظَ وَالْعَافِينَ عَنِ النَّاسِ ۗ وَاللَّهُ يُحِبُّ الْمُحْسِنِينَ
versets.tr: alladhīna yunfiqūna fī s-sarrāʾi wa-ḍ-ḍarrāʾi wa-l-kāẓimīna l-ghayẓa wa-l-ʿāfīna ʿani n-nāsi wa-llāhu yuḥibbu l-muḥsinīna
versets.trad: those who give in charity, in ease as in hardship, who restrain their anger and pardon their fellow men. God loves those who do good,
versets.commentaire: A portrait of the godly: they spend in ease and in hardship, swallow their anger, and pardon. Qushayrī widens the spending beyond goods: they withhold nothing from God and prefer Him above all things — spending their bodies in obedience, their wealth in the spreading of benefits, their hearts in the quest, their spirits in the purity of love, and their innermost secrets in contemplation at every instant. And he defines excellence by the famous hadith: iḥsān is to worship God as though you saw Him; toward men, it is to relinquish all one's due without holding it against them.

mots.racine: ن ف ق / س ر ر / ض ر ر
mots.sens: they spend in ease and hardship
mots.def: anfaqa: to spend; sarrāʾ: ease; ḍarrāʾ: adversity.

mots.racine: ك ظ م / غ ي ظ
mots.def: kaẓama: to swallow down; ghayẓ: rage.

mots.racine: ع ف و
mots.def: ʿafā: to pardon, to grant grace.

mots.racine: ح س ن
mots.def: muḥsin: one who acts with excellence.

versets.ar: وَالَّذِينَ إِذَا فَعَلُوا فَاحِشَةً أَوْ ظَلَمُوا أَنفُسَهُمْ ذَكَرُوا اللَّهَ فَاسْتَغْفَرُوا لِذُنُوبِهِمْ وَمَن يَغْفِرُ الذُّنُوبَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ وَلَمْ يُصِرُّوا عَلَىٰ مَا فَعَلُوا وَهُمْ يَعْلَمُونَ
versets.tr: wa-lladhīna idhā faʿalū fāḥishatan aw ẓalamū anfusahum dhakarū llāha fa-staghfarū li-dhunūbihim wa-man yaghfiru dh-dhunūba illā llāhu wa-lam yuṣirrū ʿalā mā faʿalū wa-hum yaʿlamūna
versets.trad: those who, having committed an indecency or wronged themselves, remember God and seek forgiveness for their sins - and who forgives sins but God? - and who do not persist knowingly in what they have done.
versets.commentaire: Those who, after the fault, remember God and implore His forgiveness without persisting. Qushayrī sets the fault in proportion to one's rank: the gravity of each one's offence is measured by his state and his station; the mere passing of a transgression through the spirit of the great equals, in them, the act committed by others.
