More deeply still: they wronged themselves by lingering to gaze upon their own states; then they implored forgiveness, disowning their movements and their stillnesses, knowing there is no way to Him save through Him. The true return is to emerge from the darkness of the self.

mots.racine: ف ح ش
ظ ل م / ن ف س
mots.def: ẓalama nafsahu: to do oneself wrong.

mots.racine: غ ف ر / ذ ن ب
mots.sens: they sought forgiveness for their sins

mots.racine: ص ر ر

versets.ar: أُولَٰئِكَ جَزَاؤُهُم مَّغْفِرَةٌ مِّن رَّبِّهِمْ وَجَنَّاتٌ تَجْرِي مِن تَحْتِهَا الْأَنْهَارُ خَالِدِينَ فِيهَا ۚ وَنِعْمَ أَجْرُ الْعَامِلِينَ
versets.tr: ulāʾika jazāʾuhum maghfiratun min rabbihim wa-jannātun tajrī min taḥtihā l-anhāru khālidīna fīhā wa-niʿma ajru l-ʿāmilīna
versets.trad: These shall have as their reward forgiveness from their Lord and Gardens beneath which rivers flow; therein they shall abide forever. How excellent is the reward of those who do good works!
versets.commentaire: Their reward is forgiveness and eternal gardens. Qushayrī gives forgiveness an inward sense: "forgiveness from their Lord" through their return to the contemplation of Lordship, and according to the good apportioned to them in the pre-eternal sharing. As for the gardens with their rivers, some are deferred, in the Firdaws, and some are present, in the joy of permitted graces and the fullness of intimacy. The reward begins here below, in the heart of the servant restored to his Lord.

mots.racine: ج ز ي / غ ف ر
ج ن ن / ج ر ي / ن ه ر
ن ع م / أ ج ر / ع م ل
mots.sens: how excellent the reward of the workers

versets.ar: قَدْ خَلَتْ مِن قَبْلِكُمْ سُنَنٌ فَسِيرُوا فِي الْأَرْضِ فَانظُرُوا كَيْفَ كَانَ عَاقِبَةُ الْمُكَذِّبِينَ
versets.tr: qad khalat min qablikum sunanun fa-sīrū fī l-arḍi fa-nẓurū kayfa kāna ʿāqibatu l-mukadhdhibīna
versets.trad: Many a way of life passed away before you; so journey through the land and see how those who cried lies came to their end!
versets.commentaire: Divine customs have passed away before you: journey through the land and ponder the end of the deniers. Qushayrī invites the drawing of a lesson: consider what befell those who came before; see how We dealt with whoever was faithful to Us, and how We took vengeance upon whoever was hostile to Us. The history of peoples is a mirror held up to the attentive heart.

mots.racine: خ ل و / س ن ن
mots.sens: ways of life have passed before you
mots.def: sunan: plural of sunna, ways, established laws.

mots.racine: س ي ر / أ ر ض
ع ق ب / ك ذ ب
mots.def: ʿāqiba: outcome; mukadhdhib: one who cries lies.

versets.ar: هَٰذَا بَيَانٌ لِّلنَّاسِ وَهُدًى وَمَوْعِظَةٌ لِّلْمُتَّقِينَ
versets.tr: hādhā bayānun li-n-nāsi wa-hudan wa-mawʿiẓatun li-l-muttaqīna
versets.trad: This is a clear declaration for mankind, a guidance and an admonition for those mindful of God.
versets.commentaire: The Qurʾān is a clear exposition for mankind, guidance and admonition for the godly. Qushayrī distinguishes three receptions of this clarity: it is an exposition for some through the proofs of reason, for others through the unveilings of the hearts, and for yet others through the manifestation of the Real within the innermost secrets. One and the same word reaches each according to the measure of his depth.

mots.racine: ب ي ن
ه د ي
و ع ظ / و ق ي

versets.ar: وَلَا تَهِنُوا وَلَا تَحْزَنُوا وَأَنتُمُ الْأَعْلَوْنَ إِن كُنتُم مُّؤْمِنِينَ
versets.tr: wa-lā tahinū wa-lā taḥzanū wa-antumu l-aʿlawna in kuntum muʾminīna
versets.trad: Do not lose heart, do not grieve, for you shall have the upper hand, if you are believers.
versets.commentaire: "Do not falter, do not grieve, you are the highest." Qushayrī reads in this an inward nobility: when you hold fast by God and lean upon God, it does not befit you to fear any but God; do not falter nor grow faint, for the succour comes from God, the Victor is God, and all that is other than Him weighs not an atom. And he concludes: it befits the believer that no fear of any other than God should overshadow him.

mots.racine: و ه ن
ح ز ن
ع ل و

versets.ar: إِن يَمْسَسْكُمْ قَرْحٌ فَقَدْ مَسَّ الْقَوْمَ قَرْحٌ مِّثْلُهُ ۚ وَتِلْكَ الْأَيَّامُ نُدَاوِلُهَا بَيْنَ النَّاسِ وَلِيَعْلَمَ اللَّهُ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَيَتَّخِذَ مِنكُمْ شُهَدَاءَ ۗ وَاللَّهُ لَا يُحِبُّ الظَّالِمِينَ
versets.tr: in yamsaskum qarḥun fa-qad massa l-qawma qarḥun mithluhu wa-tilka l-ayyāmu nudāwiluhā bayna n-nāsi wa-li-yaʿlama llāhu lladhīna āmanū wa-yattakhidha minkum shuhadāʾa wa-llāhu lā yuḥibbu ẓ-ẓālimīna
versets.trad: If a wound has touched you, a like wound has touched the others as well. Such are the days We cause to alternate among mankind, so that God may know those who believe and may take from among you witnesses [martyrs] - for God loves not the wrongdoers,
versets.commentaire: Every wound borne by the believers has its like among the enemy; the days alternate among mankind. Qushayrī both consoles and warns: if a trial befalls you for Our sake, those who went before you met with a like trial; whoever is patient prevails, whoever frets at his burden is lost. The days are turns, the states are alternations, and nothing escapes the Real. The very alternation is a divine pedagogy.

mots.racine: م س س / ق ر ح
د و ل
mots.sens: We cause them to alternate among the people
mots.def: dāwala: to make pass round in turns.

mots.racine: أ خ ذ / ش ه د
mots.sens: that He take from among you witnesses

versets.ar: وَلِيُمَحِّصَ اللَّهُ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَيَمْحَقَ الْكَافِرِينَ
versets.tr: wa-li-yumaḥḥiṣa llāhu lladhīna āmanū wa-yamḥaqa l-kāfirīna
versets.trad: and so that God may purify those who believe and wipe out the disbelievers.
versets.commentaire: The trial purifies the believers and effaces the disbelievers. Qushayrī spins out the image of the goldsmith: the trials of the unseen are a smelting of the servant; through the diversity of his states, God delivers him from his impurities until He renders him like pure gold, without dross — thus is he clarified of his defects and made pure for God. As for the deniers, they are effaced in the valleys of dispersion, like the scum that goes away useless.

mots.racine: م ح ص
أ م ن
م ح ق / ك ف ر
mots.sens: and that He efface the deniers
mots.def: maḥaqa: to annihilate, to wipe out.

versets.ar: أَمْ حَسِبْتُمْ أَن تَدْخُلُوا الْجَنَّةَ وَلَمَّا يَعْلَمِ اللَّهُ الَّذِينَ جَاهَدُوا مِنكُمْ وَيَعْلَمَ الصَّابِرِينَ
versets.tr: am ḥasibtum an tadkhulū l-jannata wa-lammā yaʿlami llāhu lladhīna jāhadū minkum wa-yaʿlama ṣ-ṣābirīna
versets.trad: Did you suppose that you would enter Paradise before God had come to know those of you who strove and come to know those who are patient?
versets.commentaire: None enters the Garden without the striving and patience being put to the test. Qushayrī warns the over-hasty desire: whoever imagines he can attain a sublime station without enduring the trials, his illusions hurl him into the abyss of perdition. And he teaches the just measure of effort: whoever knows the worth of what he seeks finds it easy to expend all his striving upon it. The greatness of the goal justifies the greatness of the toil.

mots.racine: ح س ب
ج ه د
ع ل م / ص ب ر
mots.sens: and that He know the patient

versets.ar: وَلَقَدْ كُنتُمْ تَمَنَّوْنَ الْمَوْتَ مِن قَبْلِ أَن تَلْقَوْهُ فَقَدْ رَأَيْتُمُوهُ وَأَنتُمْ تَنظُرُونَ
versets.tr: wa-laqad kuntum tamannawna l-mawta min qabli an talqawhu fa-qad raʾaytumūhu wa-antum tanẓurūna
versets.trad: Truly, you used to long for death before you encountered it; and now you have seen it with your own eyes.
versets.commentaire: You used to long for death before you saw it; you have seen it with your own eyes. Qushayrī makes of this verse the trial that distinguishes the sincere wish from the easy impulse, citing a verse: when the tears stream down the cheeks, then is told apart the one who truly weeps from the one who feigns to weep. Aspiration is measured only at the hour of the encounter.

mots.racine: م ن ي / م و ت
ل ق ي
ن ظ ر

versets.ar: وَمَا مُحَمَّدٌ إِلَّا رَسُولٌ قَدْ خَلَتْ مِن قَبْلِهِ الرُّسُلُ ۚ أَفَإِن مَّاتَ أَوْ قُتِلَ انقَلَبْتُمْ عَلَىٰ أَعْقَابِكُمْ ۚ وَمَن يَنقَلِبْ عَلَىٰ عَقِبَيْهِ فَلَن يَضُرَّ اللَّهَ شَيْئًا ۗ وَسَيَجْزِي اللَّهُ الشَّاكِرِينَ
versets.tr: wa-mā muḥammadun illā rasūlun qad khalat min qablihi r-rusulu a-fa-in māta aw qutila nqalabtum ʿalā aʿqābikum wa-man yanqalib ʿalā ʿaqibayhi fa-lan yaḍurra llāha shayʾan wa-sa-yajzī llāhu sh-shākirīna
versets.trad: Muḥammad is but a messenger; messengers have passed away before him. If he were to die or be slain, would you turn back upon your heels? Whoever turns back upon his heels does no harm to God in the least; and God will reward those who are thankful.
versets.commentaire: Muḥammad is but a messenger, like the messengers before him: his death must not make the believers retreat. Qushayrī evokes the day of his passing: the inward sights were struck with faintness, save that of the Veracious one; God upheld him with the strength of serenity, and he proclaimed: "Whoever worshipped Muḥammad, let him know that Muḥammad has died". His word, like the sun that effaces the brightness of the stars, brought all hearts back under the authority of the truth.

mots.racine: ح م د / ر س ل
م و ت / ق ت ل
mots.sens: if he were to die or be slain
mots.def: māta: to die; qutila: to be slain.

mots.racine: ق ل ب / ع ق ب
mots.sens: would you turn back upon your heels

mots.racine: ج ز ي / ش ك ر

versets.ar: وَمَا كَانَ لِنَفْسٍ أَن تَمُوتَ إِلَّا بِإِذْنِ اللَّهِ كِتَابًا مُّؤَجَّلًا ۗ وَمَن يُرِدْ ثَوَابَ الدُّنْيَا نُؤْتِهِ مِنْهَا وَمَن يُرِدْ ثَوَابَ الْآخِرَةِ نُؤْتِهِ مِنْهَا ۚ وَسَنَجْزِي الشَّاكِرِينَ
versets.tr: wa-mā kāna li-nafsin an tamūta illā bi-idhni llāhi kitāban muʾajjalan wa-man yurid thawāba d-dunyā nuʾtihi minhā wa-man yurid thawāba l-ākhirati nuʾtihi minhā wa-sa-najzī sh-shākirīna
versets.trad: No soul can die save by the leave of God, at a term prescribed. To whoever desires the reward of this world, We shall grant him a share of it; and to whoever desires the reward of the life to come, We shall grant him a share of it. And We shall reward those who are thankful.
versets.commentaire: No soul dies save at the prescribed term; each receives according to what he aims at. Qushayrī comments: the breaths are numbered, with neither surplus nor diminution. And he ranks the ends: for whoever desires the reward of the hereafter: its beginning is forgiveness, then the gardens, then the supreme good-pleasure. As for the thankful one, the reward of gratitude is gratitude itself — God thanking whoever thanks Him.

mots.racine: أ ذ ن
ك ت ب / أ ج ل
mots.sens: a decree at an appointed term

mots.racine: ث و ب / د ن و
mots.sens: the reward of this lower world

mots.racine: ث و ب / أ خ ر

versets.ar: وَكَأَيِّن مِّن نَّبِيٍّ قَاتَلَ مَعَهُ رِبِّيُّونَ كَثِيرٌ فَمَا وَهَنُوا لِمَا أَصَابَهُمْ فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ وَمَا ضَعُفُوا وَمَا اسْتَكَانُوا ۗ وَاللَّهُ يُحِبُّ الصَّابِرِينَ
versets.tr: wa-ka-ayyin min nabiyyin qātala maʿahu ribbiyyūna kathīrun fa-mā wahanū li-mā aṣābahum fī sabīli llāhi wa-mā ḍaʿufū wa-mā stakānū wa-llāhu yuḥibbu ṣ-ṣābirīna
versets.trad: How many a prophet has fought, accompanied by numerous devoted followers; they were not cast down by what befell them in the cause of God. They did not weaken, nor did they yield. God loves those who are patient.
themes: the divine love for the patient
versets.commentaire: So many prophets fought with their numerous companions, neither faltering nor yielding. Qushayrī sings their praise: those who walked the path of fidelity, who fulfilled the right of purity, who never drew back from the way, and who exacted from themselves its realization — they found the Real's love as the inheritance of their patience, and the Real became their heir at the end of their affair. They swerved neither from the striving nor from the covenant, and departed this world still faithful to the pledge.

mots.racine: ن ب أ
ر ب ب / ك ث ر
mots.def: ribbiyyūn: men dedicated to the Lord.

mots.racine: و ه ن
ك و ن / س ك ن
mots.def: istakāna: to abase oneself, to yield.

versets.ar: وَمَا كَانَ قَوْلَهُمْ إِلَّا أَن قَالُوا رَبَّنَا اغْفِرْ لَنَا ذُنُوبَنَا وَإِسْرَافَنَا فِي أَمْرِنَا وَثَبِّتْ أَقْدَامَنَا وَانصُرْنَا عَلَى الْقَوْمِ الْكَافِرِينَ
versets.tr: wa-mā kāna qawlahum illā an qālū rabbanā ghfir lanā dhunūbanā wa-isrāfanā fī amrinā wa-thabbit aqdāmanā wa-nṣurnā ʿalā l-qawmi l-kāfirīna
versets.trad: Their only words were these: "Our Lord! Forgive us our sins and our excesses, make firm our steps, and grant us victory over the disbelievers!"
versets.commentaire: Their sole utterance was a supplication for forgiveness and steadfastness. Qushayrī admires their reticence: realized in the realities of meaning, they became mute to every claim, then spoke with the tongue of seeking forgiveness, standing at the station of sacred shame. And he cites this verse: he flees from faults and then dreads them, as though his very good deeds were faults. The higher the rank, the keener the reverential awe.

mots.racine: ر ب ب / غ ف ر / ذ ن ب
س ر ف / أ م ر
mots.sens: and our excesses in our affair

mots.racine: ث ب ت / ق د م
mots.def: thabbata: to make firm; qadam: foot.

versets.ar: فَآتَاهُمُ اللَّهُ ثَوَابَ الدُّنْيَا وَحُسْنَ ثَوَابِ الْآخِرَةِ ۗ وَاللَّهُ يُحِبُّ الْمُحْسِنِينَ
versets.tr: fa-ātāhumu llāhu thawāba d-dunyā wa-ḥusna thawābi l-ākhirati wa-llāhu yuḥibbu l-muḥsinīna
versets.trad: So God granted them the reward of this world, together with the fair reward of the Hereafter. God loves those who do good.
versets.commentaire: God granted them the reward of this world and the fair reward of the Hereafter. Qushayrī grades the inward fruits: the least is contentment, then good-pleasure, then life with Him, then the intimacy of sitting before Him, then the fullness of the joy of His encounter, then the freedom of the secret in His presence. And he notes that the final reward is called "fair" for its duration, its fullness, and its fruits, which nothing thwarting can disturb.

mots.racine: أ ت ي
ث و ب / د ن و
ح س ن / ث و ب / أ خ ر
mots.sens: and the beauty of the reward to come

versets.ar: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا إِن تُطِيعُوا الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا يَرُدُّوكُمْ عَلَىٰ أَعْقَابِكُمْ فَتَنقَلِبُوا خَاسِرِينَ
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā lladhīna āmanū in tuṭīʿū lladhīna kafarū yaruddūkum ʿalā aʿqābikum fa-tanqalibū khāsirīna
versets.trad: O you who believe! If you obey those who disbelieve, they will turn you back upon your heels and you will return as losers.
themes: the danger of obeying the deniers
versets.commentaire: To obey the disbelievers makes you retreat and return as losers. Qushayrī gives the verse an inward reach: if you follow the adversaries, they will draw you toward their states and hurl you into their darknesses. To heed the opponents of the path is a fall out of the light of faith.

mots.racine: ط و ع / ك ف ر
ر د د / ع ق ب
mots.sens: they will turn you back upon your heels

mots.racine: ق ل ب / خ س ر

versets.ar: بَلِ اللَّهُ مَوْلَاكُمْ ۖ وَهُوَ خَيْرُ النَّاصِرِينَ
versets.tr: bali llāhu mawlākum wa-huwa khayru n-nāṣirīna
versets.trad: Nay! It is God who is your Protector, and He is the best of helpers.
versets.commentaire: God is your Master, the best of helpers. Qushayrī explains why His succour surpasses every other: He helps you against your own selves, to spare you your own evil; every other than Him aggravates your trial, for in helping you he sets your souls against you. Still more: every helper who is besought demands of your precious goods, then helps you or not; but He, when you implore Him, gives you every grace and never consents to leave you unaided.

mots.racine: و ل ي
خ ي ر / ن ص ر
mots.sens: He is the best of helpers

versets.ar: سَنُلْقِي فِي قُلُوبِ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا الرُّعْبَ بِمَا أَشْرَكُوا بِاللَّهِ مَا لَمْ يُنَزِّلْ بِهِ سُلْطَانًا ۖ وَمَأْوَاهُمُ النَّارُ ۚ وَبِئْسَ مَثْوَى الظَّالِمِينَ
versets.tr: sa-nulqī fī qulūbi lladhīna kafarū r-ruʿba bi-mā ashrakū bi-llāhi mā lam yunazzil bihi sulṭānan wa-maʾwāhumu n-nāru wa-biʾsa mathwā ẓ-ẓālimīna
versets.trad: We shall cast terror into the hearts of the disbelievers for having associated with God that for which He has sent down no authority. Their dwelling shall be the Fire. How wretched is the abode of the wrongdoers!
themes: the terror cast upon the deniers
the majesty of the friends of God
versets.commentaire: God casts terror into the hearts of the disbelievers. Qushayrī recalls the prophetic saying "I have been succoured by terror," then extends it to the friends of God: likewise He establishes this custom with His intimates, casting His reverential awe into the hearts; scarcely is there a man of truth without there emanating from him, upon the upholders of falsehood and illusion, a majestic gravity and a dominion within the hearts.

mots.racine: ل ق ي / ق ل ب
mots.sens: We shall cast into the hearts

mots.racine: ر ع ب
ش ر ك
mots.sens: for what they associated with God
mots.def: ashraka: to associate, to set up idols.

mots.racine: ب أ س / ث و ي / ظ ل م
mots.sens: how wretched the abode of the wrongdoers

versets.ar: وَلَقَدْ صَدَقَكُمُ اللَّهُ وَعْدَهُ إِذْ تَحُسُّونَهُم بِإِذْنِهِ ۖ حَتَّىٰ إِذَا فَشِلْتُمْ وَتَنَازَعْتُمْ فِي الْأَمْرِ وَعَصَيْتُم مِّن بَعْدِ مَا أَرَاكُم مَّا تُحِبُّونَ ۚ مِنكُم مَّن يُرِيدُ الدُّنْيَا وَمِنكُم مَّن يُرِيدُ الْآخِرَةَ ۚ ثُمَّ صَرَفَكُمْ عَنْهُمْ لِيَبْتَلِيَكُمْ ۖ وَلَقَدْ عَفَا عَنكُمْ ۗ وَاللَّهُ ذُو فَضْلٍ عَلَى الْمُؤْمِنِينَ
versets.tr: wa-laqad ṣadaqakumu llāhu waʿdahu idh taḥussūnahum bi-idhnihi ḥattā idhā fashiltum wa-tanāzaʿtum fī l-amri wa-ʿaṣaytum min baʿdi mā arākum mā tuḥibbūna minkum man yurīdu d-dunyā wa-minkum man yurīdu l-ākhirata thumma ṣarafakum ʿanhum li-yabtaliyakum wa-laqad ʿafā ʿankum wa-llāhu dhū faḍlin ʿalā l-muʾminīna
versets.trad: God did indeed fulfil His promise to you when, by His leave, you were destroying your enemies, until the moment you faltered, disputed over the command given, and disobeyed after He had shown you what you longed for. Among you are some who desire this lower world and some who desire the Hereafter. Then He turned you away from your enemies in order to test you. Yet He had already pardoned you. God is the holder of bounty toward the believers.
themes: the promise kept, then the trial
a man's worth lies in his desire
versets.commentaire: God kept His promise until the believers faltered and disobeyed at Uḥud. From "among you are some who desire this world, and some who desire the Hereafter" Qushayrī draws a sentence: the worth of each lies in his desire; whoever aspires only to this lower world has a worth as base and lowly as it; whoever aspires to the Hereafter has a noble rank; and whoever bears an aspiration turned wholly toward the Lord, that one is the master of his age. And he adds: whoever purifies himself of his own will arrives at Him.

mots.racine: ص د ق / و ع د
mots.sens: God kept His promise to you
mots.def: ṣadaqa: to be truthful; waʿd: promise.

mots.racine: ح س س / أ ذ ن
mots.sens: when you were destroying them by His leave

mots.racine: ر و د / د ن و
mots.sens: among you is one who desires this world

mots.racine: ع ف و
mots.def: ʿafā: to grant grace, to pardon.

versets.ar: إِذْ تُصْعِدُونَ وَلَا تَلْوُونَ عَلَىٰ أَحَدٍ وَالرَّسُولُ يَدْعُوكُمْ فِي أُخْرَاكُمْ فَأَثَابَكُمْ غَمًّا بِغَمٍّ لِّكَيْلَا تَحْزَنُوا عَلَىٰ مَا فَاتَكُمْ وَلَا مَا أَصَابَكُمْ ۗ وَاللَّهُ خَبِيرٌ بِمَا تَعْمَلُونَ
versets.tr: idh tuṣʿidūna wa-lā talwūna ʿalā aḥadin wa-r-rasūlu yadʿūkum fī ukhrākum fa-athābakum ghamman bi-ghammin li-kaylā taḥzanū ʿalā mā fātakum wa-lā mā aṣābakum wa-llāhu khabīrun bi-mā taʿmalūna
versets.trad: When you were climbing the slope, turning back for no one, while the Messenger was calling you from your rear, God repaid you with grief upon grief, so that you might not sorrow over what had escaped you [the spoils], nor over what had befallen you [the reverse]. God is fully aware of all that you do.
versets.commentaire: You were climbing in flight while the Messenger called you back. Qushayrī sees in this the allegory of one who passes through a lapse: the calls of the Real rise up from his soul and from every horizon — as though the stones of the streets and the bricks of the walls cried out to him: do not do this, O servant of God! — yet he persists in his straying. And the luminous sequel: when his affliction has been prolonged, the Real overtakes him by the beauty of His gentleness, delivers him from the narrowness of his captivity, and bears him into the vastness of His forgiveness and His grace.

mots.racine: ص ع د
mots.sens: when you were climbing in flight
mots.def: aṣʿada: to ascend, to draw away.

mots.racine: ل و ي
ث و ب / غ م م
mots.sens: He repaid you grief upon grief

versets.ar: ثُمَّ أَنزَلَ عَلَيْكُم مِّن بَعْدِ الْغَمِّ أَمَنَةً نُّعَاسًا يَغْشَىٰ طَائِفَةً مِّنكُمْ ۖ وَطَائِفَةٌ قَدْ أَهَمَّتْهُمْ أَنفُسُهُمْ يَظُنُّونَ بِاللَّهِ غَيْرَ الْحَقِّ ظَنَّ الْجَاهِلِيَّةِ ۖ يَقُولُونَ هَل لَّنَا مِنَ الْأَمْرِ مِن شَيْءٍ ۗ قُلْ إِنَّ الْأَمْرَ كُلَّهُ لِلَّهِ ۗ يُخْفُونَ فِي أَنفُسِهِم مَّا لَا يُبْدُونَ لَكَ ۖ يَقُولُونَ لَوْ كَانَ لَنَا مِنَ الْأَمْرِ شَيْءٌ مَّا قُتِلْنَا هَاهُنَا ۗ قُل لَّوْ كُنتُمْ فِي بُيُوتِكُمْ لَبَرَزَ الَّذِينَ كُتِبَ عَلَيْهِمُ الْقَتْلُ إِلَىٰ مَضَاجِعِهِمْ ۖ وَلِيَبْتَلِيَ اللَّهُ مَا فِي صُدُورِكُمْ وَلِيُمَحِّصَ مَا فِي قُلُوبِكُمْ ۗ وَاللَّهُ عَلِيمٌ بِذَاتِ الصُّدُورِ
versets.tr: thumma anzala ʿalaykum min baʿdi l-ghammi amanatan nuʿāsan yaghshā ṭāʾifatan minkum wa-ṭāʾifatun qad ahammathum anfusuhum yaẓunnūna bi-llāhi ghayra l-ḥaqqi ẓanna l-jāhiliyyati yaqūlūna hal lanā mina l-amri min shayʾin qul inna l-amra kullahu li-llāhi yukhfūna fī anfusihim mā lā yubdūna laka yaqūlūna law kāna lanā mina l-amri shayʾun mā qutilnā hāhunā qul law kuntum fī buyūtikum la-baraza lladhīna kutiba ʿalayhimu l-qatlu ilā maḍājiʿihim wa-li-yabtaliya llāhu mā fī ṣudūrikum wa-li-yumaḥḥiṣa mā fī qulūbikum wa-llāhu ʿalīmun bi-dhāti ṣ-ṣudūri
versets.trad: Then, after the grief, He sent down upon you a sense of security, a drowsiness that enveloped a party of you, while another party was anxious only for itself, harbouring of God a false thought, a thought born of ignorance. They were saying: "Have we any say in this affair?" Say: "The affair belongs entirely to God." They conceal within themselves what they do not disclose to you. They say: "Had we any say in this affair, we would not have been slain here." Say: "Even had you remained in your houses, those for whom death was decreed would have gone forth to their resting-places; this so that God might test what is in your breasts and purify what is in your hearts. For God knows perfectly the contents of the breasts."
themes: the affair belongs entirely to God
versets.commentaire: After the grief, God sent down security as a drowsiness upon some, while others remained captive to their souls. Qushayrī sets two groups against each other: those who, after their lapse, come to relinquish their souls, to wash their hands of them, to raise their hearts above them — living by God for God, with no covetous glance; and those who remain in the desolation of their self. On "the affair belongs entirely to God" he comments: whoever knows that the Author is God strips himself of his choice and his states as the hair is loosed from the dough, and entrusts his affairs to God absolutely; the sign of it is that he rests from the toil of his own management and lives in the vastness of contemplating His decree.

mots.racine: أ م ن / ن ع س
ظ ن ن / ح ق ق
mots.sens: thinking of God other than the truth

mots.racine: ظ ن ن / ج ه ل
أ م ر / ك ل ل
mots.sens: the affair belongs entirely to God

mots.racine: ع ل م / ص د ر
mots.sens: knowing the contents of the breasts

versets.ar: إِنَّ الَّذِينَ تَوَلَّوْا مِنكُمْ يَوْمَ الْتَقَى الْجَمْعَانِ إِنَّمَا اسْتَزَلَّهُمُ الشَّيْطَانُ بِبَعْضِ مَا كَسَبُوا ۖ وَلَقَدْ عَفَا اللَّهُ عَنْهُمْ ۗ إِنَّ اللَّهَ غَفُورٌ حَلِيمٌ
versets.tr: inna lladhīna tawallaw minkum yawma ltaqā l-jamʿāni innamā stazallahumu sh-shayṭānu bi-baʿḍi mā kasabū wa-laqad ʿafā llāhu ʿanhum inna llāha ghafūrun ḥalīmun
versets.trad: Those of you who turned away on the day the two armies met were made to stumble by Satan only because of some of the deeds they had done. Yet God has pardoned them. Truly God is forgiving, forbearing.
versets.commentaire: Those who turned their backs were made to stumble by the devil on account of some of their deeds, and God pardoned them. Qushayrī describes the inward mechanism of the fall: this verse alludes to the states of those whose will has weakened, whose resolves have faltered, whom passion has led and whom languor has overcome. The counsel of the sincere, the call of desire, and the whisperings of the devils presented themselves to them; they inclined toward absence, preferred passion to piety, and remained deprived of Him without even enjoying what they had preferred to Him.

mots.racine: و ل ي
ل ق ي / ج م ع
mots.sens: the day the two hosts met

mots.racine: ز ل ل / ش ط ن
mots.def: istazalla: to make slip; shayṭān: devil.

mots.racine: غ ف ر / ح ل م

versets.ar: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا لَا تَكُونُوا كَالَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا وَقَالُوا لِإِخْوَانِهِمْ إِذَا ضَرَبُوا فِي الْأَرْضِ أَوْ كَانُوا غُزًّى لَّوْ كَانُوا عِندَنَا مَا مَاتُوا وَمَا قُتِلُوا لِيَجْعَلَ اللَّهُ ذَٰلِكَ حَسْرَةً فِي قُلُوبِهِمْ ۗ وَاللَّهُ يُحْيِي وَيُمِيتُ ۗ وَاللَّهُ بِمَا تَعْمَلُونَ بَصِيرٌ
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā lladhīna āmanū lā takūnū ka-lladhīna kafarū wa-qālū li-ikhwānihim idhā ḍarabū fī l-arḍi aw kānū ghuzzan law kānū ʿindanā mā mātū wa-mā qutilū li-yajʿala llāhu dhālika ḥasratan fī qulūbihim wa-llāhu yuḥyī wa-yumītu wa-llāhu bi-mā taʿmalūna baṣīrun
versets.trad: O you who believe! Be not like those who disbelieve and say of their brethren who have gone forth to travel the land or who have gone to war: "Had they stayed with us, they would not have died, nor been slain." God made this a source of regret in their hearts. God gives life and gives death, and He sees perfectly all that you do.
themes: God, Master of life and death
versets.commentaire: Be not like the disbelievers who vainly lament the fate of their brethren. Qushayrī lays bare this sickness of the heart: he who grows accustomed to bewailing his vanished past, or to brooding over his future, the least of his punishments is the narrowing of the heart amid scattered cares, and the effacing of his heart's spiritual life through his heedlessness, through his "if only" and his "perhaps". And he concludes: the fruit of thought turned toward "if" and "perhaps" is desolation, regret, narrowness of heart, and dispersion.

mots.racine: ك و ن / ك ف ر
ض ر ب / أ ر ض
ح س ر / ق ل ب
ح ي ي / م و ت
mots.sens: God gives life and gives death
mots.def: aḥyā: to give life; amāta: to give death.

versets.ar: وَلَئِن قُتِلْتُمْ فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ أَوْ مُتُّمْ لَمَغْفِرَةٌ مِّنَ اللَّهِ وَرَحْمَةٌ خَيْرٌ مِّمَّا يَجْمَعُونَ
versets.tr: wa-laʾin qutiltum fī sabīli llāhi aw muttum la-maghfiratun mina llāhi wa-raḥmatun khayrun mimmā yajmaʿūna
versets.trad: And if you are slain in the cause of God or die, a forgiveness and a mercy from God are truly better than all that they amass.
themes: the gift of self upon the path
versets.commentaire: To die or be slain in the cause of God is better than all one amasses. Qushayrī raises the sentence to its mystical height: to give one's soul in God is better than to live with other than God, and to return to God is better, for whoever knows God, than to subsist with other than Him. And he warns: all that the servant prefers to God holds no blessing — be it this lower world, if you will, or the Hereafter itself.

mots.racine: ق ت ل / س ب ل
mots.sens: if you are slain in the path of God
mots.def: sabīl Allāh: the way of God.

mots.racine: غ ف ر / ر ح م
mots.sens: a forgiveness from God and a mercy

mots.racine: خ ي ر / ج م ع

versets.ar: وَلَئِن مُّتُّمْ أَوْ قُتِلْتُمْ لَإِلَى اللَّهِ تُحْشَرُونَ
versets.tr: wa-laʾin muttum aw qutiltum la-ilā llāhi tuḥsharūna
versets.trad: Whether you die or are slain, it is to God, beyond all doubt, that you shall be gathered.
versets.commentaire: Whether dying or slain, it is unto God that one is gathered. Qushayrī transmutes the dreaded end into gladness: when the destination is God, the journey toward God becomes sweet; a journey toward Him, after which we shall lay down our mounts, is sweeter than honey, despite all its trials. The destination sanctifies the road.

mots.racine: م و ت / ق ت ل
mots.sens: if you die or are slain

mots.racine: ح ش ر
mots.sens: it is unto God that you shall be gathered

versets.ar: فَبِمَا رَحْمَةٍ مِّنَ اللَّهِ لِنتَ لَهُمْ ۖ وَلَوْ كُنتَ فَظًّا غَلِيظَ الْقَلْبِ لَانفَضُّوا مِنْ حَوْلِكَ ۖ فَاعْفُ عَنْهُمْ وَاسْتَغْفِرْ لَهُمْ وَشَاوِرْهُمْ فِي الْأَمْرِ ۖ فَإِذَا عَزَمْتَ فَتَوَكَّلْ عَلَى اللَّهِ ۚ إِنَّ اللَّهَ يُحِبُّ الْمُتَوَكِّلِينَ
versets.tr: fa-bimā raḥmatin mina llāhi linta lahum wa-law kunta faẓẓan ghalīẓa l-qalbi la-nfaḍḍū min ḥawlika fa-ʿfu ʿanhum wa-staghfir lahum wa-shāwirhum fī l-amri fa-idhā ʿazamta fa-tawakkal ʿalā llāhi inna llāha yuḥibbu l-mutawakkilīna
versets.trad: By a mercy from God you were gentle toward them; had you been harsh and hard of heart, they would have scattered from around you. So pardon them, ask forgiveness for them, and consult them in the conduct of affairs. And once you have resolved, place your trust in God. God loves those who place their trust in Him.
versets.commentaire: By a divine mercy the Prophet was gentle toward the believers. Qushayrī makes of this gentleness a gift: had you poured out for them the pure wine of Oneness, unmixed with what leaves them a share, they would have scattered far from you, wandering distraught, unable to hold for an instant. On "pardon them, ask forgiveness for them, consult them," he unveils a delicacy: the one pardoned remains in the confusion of shame and does not see himself worthy of honour; by consulting them, you efface their brokenness and set their hearts at ease. And true trust? The contemplation of the decree, and the repose of hearts from the toil of management.

mots.ar: فَبِمَا رَحْمَةٍ مِّنَ اللَّهِ لِنتَ لَهُمْ
mots.tr: fa-bimā raḥmatin mina llāhi linta lahum
mots.racine: ر ح م / ل ي ن
mots.sens: by a mercy from God you were gentle

mots.racine: ف ظ ظ / غ ل ظ / ق ل ب
ش و ر / أ م ر
و ك ل

versets.ar: إِن يَنصُرْكُمُ اللَّهُ فَلَا غَالِبَ لَكُمْ ۖ وَإِن يَخْذُلْكُمْ فَمَن ذَا الَّذِي يَنصُرُكُم مِّن بَعْدِهِ ۗ وَعَلَى اللَّهِ فَلْيَتَوَكَّلِ الْمُؤْمِنُونَ
versets.tr: in yanṣurkumu llāhu fa-lā ghāliba lakum wa-in yakhdhulkum fa-man dhā lladhī yanṣurukum min baʿdihi wa-ʿalā llāhi fa-l-yatawakkali l-muʾminūna
versets.trad: If God succours you, none can overcome you. And if He forsakes you, who then, after Him, could succour you? So in God let the believers place their trust!
versets.commentaire: If God succours, none shall conquer; if He forsakes, none shall succour. Qushayrī interiorizes the struggle: succour is exercised against the enemy, and your fiercest enemy is your soul, the one that dwells between your flanks. The true succour is to undo the summonses of its covetousness by the ramparts of His mercy, until the armies of the passions are scattered beneath the onslaught of graces, and sovereignty remains to God alone, pure of the suspicions of the self. On forsaking (khidhlān) he says: it is to be left alone with one's disobediences; whomever He forsakes, He looses the rein upon him and abandons him to his own evil choosing.

mots.racine: ن ص ر
غ ل ب
خ ذ ل
و ك ل / أ م ن
mots.sens: let the believers place their trust in Him

versets.ar: وَمَا كَانَ لِنَبِيٍّ أَن يَغُلَّ ۚ وَمَن يَغْلُلْ يَأْتِ بِمَا غَلَّ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ ۚ ثُمَّ تُوَفَّىٰ كُلُّ نَفْسٍ مَّا كَسَبَتْ وَهُمْ لَا يُظْلَمُونَ
versets.tr: wa-mā kāna li-nabiyyin an yaghulla wa-man yaghlul yaʾti bi-mā ghalla yawma l-qiyāmati thumma tuwaffā kullu nafsin mā kasabat wa-hum lā yuẓlamūna
versets.trad: It is not for a prophet to act with treachery. Whoever defrauds shall come bearing his fraud on the Day of Resurrection. Then every soul shall be paid in full for what it has earned, and none shall be wronged.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī sanctifies the rank of the prophets above every stain of betrayal. The one whom God charges with bearing His message to His servants delivers it to those entitled to it as a sacred duty, without favouring a kinsman in defiance of the divine order, and without withholding out of rancour the portion that is anyone's due. And it is also said: it does not belong to any prophet to deposit the divine secrets in hearts unworthy of them; rather he situates each soul according to its rank, as the tradition reports: "we were commanded to assign people the place that is properly theirs."

mots.racine: غ ل ل
mots.def: ghalla: to commit fraud, to take secretly.

mots.racine: و ف ي
mots.sens: every soul shall be paid in full

versets.ar: أَفَمَنِ اتَّبَعَ رِضْوَانَ اللَّهِ كَمَن بَاءَ بِسَخَطٍ مِّنَ اللَّهِ وَمَأْوَاهُ جَهَنَّمُ ۚ وَبِئْسَ الْمَصِيرُ
versets.tr: a-fa-mani ttabaʿa riḍwāna llāhi ka-man bāʾa bi-sakhaṭin mina llāhi wa-maʾwāhu jahannamu wa-biʾsa l-maṣīru
versets.trad: Is one who seeks the good pleasure of God like one who has incurred the wrath of God, whose abode shall be Hell? What a wretched destination!
versets.commentaire: They are not equal, says Qushayrī: the one whom God has been pleased with from all eternity, and the one against whom He has been angered, whom He has abandoned to his own states, consigning him to his own works, heedless of contemplating His favours. To follow the divine good pleasure is to part from that which He turns one away from and to embrace what He commands: whoever strips himself of the forbidden and arms himself with firmness in the embrace of the commanded has followed His good pleasure and merited the Gardens.

mots.racine: ر ض و
ب و أ / س خ ط
mots.def: bāʾa: to return laden with; sakhaṭ: anger.

versets.ar: هُمْ دَرَجَاتٌ عِندَ اللَّهِ ۗ وَاللَّهُ بَصِيرٌ بِمَا يَعْمَلُونَ
versets.tr: hum darajātun ʿinda llāhi wa-llāhu baṣīrun bi-mā yaʿmalūna
versets.trad: They occupy differing degrees in the sight of God, and God sees perfectly all that they do.
themes: the degrees in the sight of God
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: they are the people of degrees within the decree of God: such is the felicitous one brought near, such is the wretched one cast far. Ranks are not measured by appearances but by inward truth, and the gaze of God penetrates whatever the works conceal.

mots.racine: د ر ج
ب ص ر

versets.ar: لَقَدْ مَنَّ اللَّهُ عَلَى الْمُؤْمِنِينَ إِذْ بَعَثَ فِيهِمْ رَسُولًا مِّنْ أَنفُسِهِمْ يَتْلُو عَلَيْهِمْ آيَاتِهِ وَيُزَكِّيهِمْ وَيُعَلِّمُهُمُ الْكِتَابَ وَالْحِكْمَةَ وَإِن كَانُوا مِن قَبْلُ لَفِي ضَلَالٍ مُّبِينٍ
versets.tr: la-qad manna llāhu ʿalā l-muʾminīna idh baʿatha fīhim rasūlan min anfusihim yatlū ʿalayhim āyātihi wa-yuzakkīhim wa-yuʿallimuhumu l-kitāba wa-l-ḥikmata wa-in kānū min qablu la-fī ḍalālin mubīnin
versets.trad: God has indeed bestowed a favour upon the believers when He sent among them a messenger from among themselves, who recites to them His verses, who purifies them, and who teaches them the Book and the Wisdom, whereas before they were in manifest error.
versets.commentaire: God lavished His favour upon the believers, says Qushayrī, by sending to them one such as the Chosen Prophet, lord of all creatures, making known to them their religion and rendering radiant for them its proofs. Where the enemies denied and swelled with pride, the believers adorned themselves with grace through election, and welcomed the command with hearing and obedience, from the very depths of their capacity: they were made happy in this world and the next, and merited from God honour and nearness.

mots.racine: م ن ن
ز ك و
mots.def: tazkiya: to purify, to make grow in purity.

versets.ar: أَوَلَمَّا أَصَابَتْكُم مُّصِيبَةٌ قَدْ أَصَبْتُم مِّثْلَيْهَا قُلْتُمْ أَنَّىٰ هَٰذَا ۖ قُلْ هُوَ مِنْ عِندِ أَنفُسِكُمْ ۗ إِنَّ اللَّهَ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ
versets.tr: a-wa-lammā aṣābatkum muṣībatun qad aṣabtum mithlayhā qultum annā hādhā qul huwa min ʿindi anfusikum inna llāha ʿalā kulli shayʾin qadīrun
versets.trad: When a reverse befell you, although you yourselves had already inflicted upon your enemies twice as much, you said: "Whence comes this?" Say: "It comes from your own selves!" Truly God has power over all things.
themes: the reverse that comes from oneself
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī observes the habit of created beings: to forget their own faults and disobedience, and to turn back toward God blaming Him for the trials and losses that befall them. Yet whoever has given himself over to sin has every reason never to forget that the chastisement must descend: the evil that strikes proceeds from what the soul has sown.

mots.racine: ص و ب
ن ف س
mots.def: the cause lies in the soul itself.

versets.ar: وَمَا أَصَابَكُمْ يَوْمَ الْتَقَى الْجَمْعَانِ فَبِإِذْنِ اللَّهِ وَلِيَعْلَمَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ
versets.tr: wa-mā aṣābakum yawma ltaqā l-jamʿāni fa-bi-idhni llāhi wa-li-yaʿlama l-muʾminīna
versets.trad: And the reverse that befell you on the day the two hosts met came about by the permission of God, that He might know the believers,
versets.commentaire: God lightens for the believers and the people of clear-sightedness, says Qushayrī, the immense trial endured on the day of Uḥud, by telling them that all of it came to pass by His permission; and the trial that befalls by the permission of God is, for the one who loves, sweeter than honey and more desirable than any felicity.

mots.racine: ل ق ي / ج م ع
أ ذ ن

versets.ar: وَلِيَعْلَمَ الَّذِينَ نَافَقُوا ۚ وَقِيلَ لَهُمْ تَعَالَوْا قَاتِلُوا فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ أَوِ ادْفَعُوا ۖ قَالُوا لَوْ نَعْلَمُ قِتَالًا لَّاتَّبَعْنَاكُمْ ۗ هُمْ لِلْكُفْرِ يَوْمَئِذٍ أَقْرَبُ مِنْهُمْ لِلْإِيمَانِ ۚ يَقُولُونَ بِأَفْوَاهِهِم مَّا لَيْسَ فِي قُلُوبِهِمْ ۗ وَاللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ بِمَا يَكْتُمُونَ
versets.tr: wa-li-yaʿlama lladhīna nāfaqū wa-qīla lahum taʿālaw qātilū fī sabīli llāhi awi-dfaʿū qālū law naʿlamu qitālan la-ttabaʿnākum hum li-l-kufri yawmaʾidhin aqrabu minhum li-l-īmāni yaqūlūna bi-afwāhihim mā laysa fī qulūbihim wa-llāhu aʿlamu bi-mā yaktumūna
versets.trad: and that He might know the hypocrites. It was said to them: "Come, fight in the way of God, or at least defend yourselves!" They answered: "Had we known how to fight, we would surely have followed you." That day they were nearer to disbelief than to faith. They were saying with their mouths what was not in their hearts. But God knew perfectly what they were concealing.
themes: the mouth that belies the heart
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī exposes those whose companionship held no sincerity: how they sought pretexts and feigned sloth. They were saying with their mouths what was not in their hearts; so, inevitably, they were served honey mingled with colocynth: they schemed, but God schemes without their knowing, and God is the best of strategists. The divorce of tongue and heart is the very mark of the hypocrite.

mots.racine: ن ف ق
mots.def: nifāq: to conceal disbelief beneath an outward show of faith.

mots.racine: ف و ه / ق ل ب
mots.sens: with their mouths what is not in their hearts
mots.def: the lie of the lips betrayed by the heart.

versets.ar: الَّذِينَ قَالُوا لِإِخْوَانِهِمْ وَقَعَدُوا لَوْ أَطَاعُونَا مَا قُتِلُوا ۗ قُلْ فَادْرَءُوا عَنْ أَنفُسِكُمُ الْمَوْتَ إِن كُنتُمْ صَادِقِينَ
versets.tr: alladhīna qālū li-ikhwānihim wa-qaʿadū law aṭāʿūnā mā qutilū qul fa-draʾū ʿan anfusikumu l-mawta in kuntum ṣādiqīna
versets.trad: Those who, remaining at home, said of their brethren: "Had they obeyed us, they would not have been slain." Say: "Then ward off death from yourselves, if you are truthful!"
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: these are they who leaned upon what their soul made fair to them, preferring passion, then dared to object against the One who disposes the decrees, claiming that holding back from battle would have preserved their safety. How blameworthy are such conjectures, how turned away from the contemplation of the Real are such hearts! "Then ward off death from yourselves": repel it, if you can; and when could you ever do so? Alas, never.

mots.racine: ق ع د
mots.def: qaʿada: to abstain, to refrain from going to battle.

mots.racine: د ر أ
mots.def: daraʾa: to push away, to avert.

versets.ar: وَلَا تَحْسَبَنَّ الَّذِينَ قُتِلُوا فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ أَمْوَاتًا ۚ بَلْ أَحْيَاءٌ عِندَ رَبِّهِمْ يُرْزَقُونَ
versets.tr: wa-lā taḥsabanna lladhīna qutilū fī sabīli llāhi amwātan bal aḥyāʾun ʿinda rabbihim yurzaqūna
versets.trad: Never think that those slain in the way of God are dead. No! they are alive, provided for in the presence of their Lord.
themes: life in the presence of the Lord
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: life in the remembrance of the Real, after the souls have been consumed in His good pleasure, is more perfect than subsistence by the bounty of creatures with the veil cast over the Real. And it is said: the one whose heir is the Living who never ceases to live is not dead, even though he be slain; bodies were created for death, yet a man's death in God is, beyond all doubt, the most noble of ends.

mots.racine: ق ت ل / س ب ل
mots.sens: slain in the way of God

mots.racine: ح ي ي / ر ز ق
mots.def: aḥyāʾ: living ones; yurzaqūn: nourished in God's presence.

versets.ar: فَرِحِينَ بِمَا آتَاهُمُ اللَّهُ مِن فَضْلِهِ وَيَسْتَبْشِرُونَ بِالَّذِينَ لَمْ يَلْحَقُوا بِهِم مِّنْ خَلْفِهِمْ أَلَّا خَوْفٌ عَلَيْهِمْ وَلَا هُمْ يَحْزَنُونَ
versets.tr: fariḥīna bi-mā ātāhumu llāhu min faḍlihi wa-yastabshirūna bi-lladhīna lam yalḥaqū bihim min khalfihim allā khawfun ʿalayhim wa-lā hum yaḥzanūna
versets.trad: They are joyful at the grace God has bestowed upon them, and they rejoice for those who, left behind, will rejoin them and will know neither fear nor grief.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the one who knows that his beloved ones await him, abiding in delicacy and felicity, savours no life without making ready to rejoin them, to be reunited with them and to alight at their side. The joy of the elect is turned toward the brethren who will follow them upon the path.

mots.racine: ف ر ح
ب ش ر
mots.def: istibshār: to welcome a happy announcement.

versets.ar: يَسْتَبْشِرُونَ بِنِعْمَةٍ مِّنَ اللَّهِ وَفَضْلٍ وَأَنَّ اللَّهَ لَا يُضِيعُ أَجْرَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ
versets.tr: yastabshirūna bi-niʿmatin mina llāhi wa-faḍlin wa-anna llāha lā yuḍīʿu ajra l-muʾminīna
versets.trad: They rejoice at a favour and a grace from God, and at the fact that God lets not the reward of the believers be lost.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the cause of their rejoicing, that which makes it necessary, is a grace from God and a favour from Him; had it not been for His grace and His bounty toward them, when would they ever have rejoiced? Their gladness is not so much for the favour as for this: that they are His servants and that He is their Master — without His favour upon them, never would this state have come to them.

mots.racine: ن ع م / ف ض ل
ض ي ع / أ ج ر
mots.sens: lets not the reward be lost
mots.def: aḍāʿa: to cause to be lost, to neglect.

versets.ar: الَّذِينَ اسْتَجَابُوا لِلَّهِ وَالرَّسُولِ مِن بَعْدِ مَا أَصَابَهُمُ الْقَرْحُ ۚ لِلَّذِينَ أَحْسَنُوا مِنْهُمْ وَاتَّقَوْا أَجْرٌ عَظِيمٌ
versets.tr: alladhīna stajābū li-llāhi wa-r-rasūli min baʿdi mā aṣābahumu l-qarḥu li-lladhīna aḥsanū minhum wa-ttaqaw ajrun ʿaẓīmun
versets.trad: Those who responded to God and to the Messenger after the wounds they had received: for those among them who acted with valour and feared God, a magnificent reward is reserved.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī distinguishes free response from mere obedience: to respond is to consent willingly, not under constraint; they responded to God not bearing the burden of any pain, but through the heart's impulse, the love of the inmost being, the choice of the spirit, and the sweetness found in bearing the decree. As for excellence, he cites the saying on iḥsān: "to worship God as though you saw Him" — this is contemplation and reverential awe — "for if you do not see Him, yet He sees you" — this is vigilance in striving. The magnificent reward is deferred for the beginners, anticipated for those of the end.

mots.racine: ج و ب
mots.def: istijāba: to respond freely to the call.

mots.racine: ق ر ح
mots.def: qarḥ: a wound received in battle.

mots.racine: ح س ن
mots.def: iḥsān: to act with excellence before God.

versets.ar: الَّذِينَ قَالَ لَهُمُ النَّاسُ إِنَّ النَّاسَ قَدْ جَمَعُوا لَكُمْ فَاخْشَوْهُمْ فَزَادَهُمْ إِيمَانًا وَقَالُوا حَسْبُنَا اللَّهُ وَنِعْمَ الْوَكِيلُ
versets.tr: alladhīna qāla lahumu n-nāsu inna n-nāsa qad jamaʿū lakum fa-khshawhum fa-zādahum īmānan wa-qālū ḥasbunā llāhu wa-niʿma l-wakīlu
versets.trad: Those to whom men said: "The people have gathered against you, so fear them!" and whose faith only increased thereby. They answered: "God suffices us! What an excellent Guardian!"
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: nothing of the states of this world clouded their outward life without dawns of unveiling at once opening within their inmost depths; thus their certitude grew by certitude. And he adds: one of the signs of certitude is that the hearts stand alone with God when every hope placed in creatures for succour and support is cut off.

mots.racine: ز ي د / أ م ن
ح س ب / و ك ل
mots.sens: God suffices us, what an excellent Guarantor
mots.def: ḥasb: that which suffices; wakīl: the guarantor in whom one places one's trust.

versets.ar: فَانقَلَبُوا بِنِعْمَةٍ مِّنَ اللَّهِ وَفَضْلٍ لَّمْ يَمْسَسْهُمْ سُوءٌ وَاتَّبَعُوا رِضْوَانَ اللَّهِ ۗ وَاللَّهُ ذُو فَضْلٍ عَظِيمٍ
versets.tr: fa-nqalabū bi-niʿmatin mina llāhi wa-faḍlin lam yamsashum sūʾun wa-ttabaʿū riḍwāna llāhi wa-llāhu dhū faḍlin ʿaẓīmin
versets.trad: They returned with a favour and a grace from God. No harm had touched them. They had sought the good pleasure of God, and God possesses a boundless grace.
versets.commentaire: Such is the way of the Real, says Qushayrī, with the one who is sincere in his recourse to Him: He prepares for him an abode in the shade of His sufficiency, so that no trial may touch him, no pain may reach him, no weariness may cast its shadow over him.

mots.racine: ق ل ب
mots.def: inqalaba: to come back, to turn back.

mots.racine: م س س / س و أ

versets.ar: إِنَّمَا ذَٰلِكُمُ الشَّيْطَانُ يُخَوِّفُ أَوْلِيَاءَهُ فَلَا تَخَافُوهُمْ وَخَافُونِ إِن كُنتُم مُّؤْمِنِينَ
versets.tr: innamā dhālikumu sh-shayṭānu yukhawwifu awliyāʾahu fa-lā takhāfūhum wa-khāfūni in kuntum muʾminīna
versets.trad: Thus it is with Satan: he frightens his henchmen. As for you, do not fear them, but fear Me, if you are believers!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī sees in this assault of Satan's whisperings upon the hearts of God's friends the truth of their flight toward Him: like the child frightened by something that terrifies the little ones — seized with fear, he finds refuge only with his mother; and when he comes to her, she gathers him to herself, presses him to her breast and lays her cheek against his. So it is with the servant: sincere in his imploring of God and his turning back to Him away from rebellion, He shelters him beneath the wing of His nearness and grasps him with the beauty of His loving-kindness.

mots.racine: خ و ف / و ل ي
mots.def: khawwafa: to instil fear; awliyāʾ: his partisans.

mots.racine: خ و ف
mots.def: the only legitimate fear is the fear of God.

versets.ar: وَلَا يَحْزُنكَ الَّذِينَ يُسَارِعُونَ فِي الْكُفْرِ ۚ إِنَّهُمْ لَن يَضُرُّوا اللَّهَ شَيْئًا ۗ يُرِيدُ اللَّهُ أَلَّا يَجْعَلَ لَهُمْ حَظًّا فِي الْآخِرَةِ ۖ وَلَهُمْ عَذَابٌ عَظِيمٌ
versets.tr: wa-lā yaḥzunka lladhīna yusāriʿūna fī l-kufri innahum lan yaḍurrū llāha shayʾan yurīdu llāhu allā yajʿala lahum ḥaẓẓan fī l-ākhirati wa-lahum ʿadhābun ʿaẓīmun
versets.trad: Let not those who vie with one another in rushing toward disbelief grieve you. They can in no way harm God. God wills not to grant them any portion in the life to come, and a terrible chastisement awaits them.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: God strengthened the heart of His Prophet by the renewal and confirmation of the covenant: that no enemy would rejoice at his misfortune, and that no harm would reach him from their part. The eagerness of others toward denial can in no way diminish the truth.

mots.racine: س ر ع / ك ف ر
ح ظ ظ
mots.def: ḥaẓẓ: an allotted lot, a share.

versets.ar: إِنَّ الَّذِينَ اشْتَرَوُا الْكُفْرَ بِالْإِيمَانِ لَن يَضُرُّوا اللَّهَ شَيْئًا وَلَهُمْ عَذَابٌ أَلِيمٌ
versets.tr: inna lladhīna shtarawu l-kufra bi-l-īmāni lan yaḍurrū llāha shayʾan wa-lahum ʿadhābun alīmun
versets.trad: Those who barter faith for disbelief harm God in no way, but a painful chastisement awaits them.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: if they cause harm, they harm none but themselves; if they persist, they persist only in their own perdition. To exchange faith for denial does not reach God: it is the soul of the foolish trader that ruins itself.

mots.racine: ش ر ي / ك ف ر / أ م ن
mots.sens: they bought disbelief at the price of faith
mots.def: ishtarā: to barter, to exchange at a loss.

mots.racine: ع ذ ب / أ ل م

versets.ar: وَلَا يَحْسَبَنَّ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا أَنَّمَا نُمْلِي لَهُمْ خَيْرٌ لِّأَنفُسِهِمْ ۚ إِنَّمَا نُمْلِي لَهُمْ لِيَزْدَادُوا إِثْمًا ۚ وَلَهُمْ عَذَابٌ مُّهِينٌ
versets.tr: wa-lā yaḥsabanna lladhīna kafarū annamā numlī lahum khayrun li-anfusihim innamā numlī lahum li-yazdādū ithman wa-lahum ʿadhābun muhīnun
versets.trad: Let not the disbelievers think that the respite We grant them is good for them. We grant them this respite only that they may increase in sin. A humiliating chastisement awaits them.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: of the perfection of the stratagem that envelops them and the extremity of their chastisement is that We punish them without their perceiving it, drawing them step by step from where they know not; We grant them a respite, and they deem it a grace, never reckoning it a retribution. When at last the hidden secrets of the decree are disclosed, they will know they were in perdition. That which becomes a cause of rebellion and an occasion of heedlessness cannot be counted among the blessings.

mots.racine: م ل و
mots.def: amlā: to allow a reprieve, to prolong.

mots.racine: ز ي د / أ ث م
mots.sens: that they may increase in sin

versets.ar: مَّا كَانَ اللَّهُ لِيَذَرَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ عَلَىٰ مَا أَنتُمْ عَلَيْهِ حَتَّىٰ يَمِيزَ الْخَبِيثَ مِنَ الطَّيِّبِ ۗ وَمَا كَانَ اللَّهُ لِيُطْلِعَكُمْ عَلَى الْغَيْبِ وَلَٰكِنَّ اللَّهَ يَجْتَبِي مِن رُّسُلِهِ مَن يَشَاءُ ۖ فَآمِنُوا بِاللَّهِ وَرُسُلِهِ ۚ وَإِن تُؤْمِنُوا وَتَتَّقُوا فَلَكُمْ أَجْرٌ عَظِيمٌ
versets.tr: mā kāna llāhu li-yadhara l-muʾminīna ʿalā mā antum ʿalayhi ḥattā yamīza l-khabītha mina ṭ-ṭayyibi wa-mā kāna llāhu li-yuṭliʿakum ʿalā l-ghaybi wa-lākinna llāha yajtabī min rusulihi man yashāʾu fa-āminū bi-llāhi wa-rusulihi wa-in tuʾminū wa-tattaqū fa-lakum ajrun ʿaẓīmun
versets.trad: God will not leave the believers in the state in which you now are, save for the time needed to distinguish the corrupt from the wholesome. Nor is God to acquaint you with the Unseen (al-ghayb); rather He chooses among His messengers whom He wills. So believe in God and in His messengers. If you believe and fear God, an immense reward is reserved for you.
themes: the sifting of the pure and the impure
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: today He has gathered them by their bodies and outward forms, but He has separated them in their realities and inner meanings: this one of pure nature, that one of corrupted clay. Though they mingle, before the penetrating gaze of the elect they remain distinct. And concerning the hidden: the secrets of the Unseen are not made manifest to those defiled by the impurities of the human condition; the Real reserves to Himself the knowledge of all that is, exalted or lowly, and elects for the knowledge of certain of His secrets only those of His prophets whom He wills.

mots.racine: م ي ز / خ ب ث / ط ي ب
mots.sens: to distinguish the impure from the pure

mots.racine: ج ب ي / ر س ل

versets.ar: وَلَا يَحْسَبَنَّ الَّذِينَ يَبْخَلُونَ بِمَا آتَاهُمُ اللَّهُ مِن فَضْلِهِ هُوَ خَيْرًا لَّهُم ۖ بَلْ هُوَ شَرٌّ لَّهُمْ ۖ سَيُطَوَّقُونَ مَا بَخِلُوا بِهِ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ ۗ وَلِلَّهِ مِيرَاثُ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ ۗ وَاللَّهُ بِمَا تَعْمَلُونَ خَبِيرٌ
versets.tr: wa-lā yaḥsabanna lladhīna yabkhalūna bi-mā ātāhumu llāhu min faḍlihi huwa khayran lahum bal huwa sharrun lahum sa-yuṭawwaqūna mā bakhilū bihi yawma l-qiyāmati wa-li-llāhi mīrāthu s-samāwāti wa-l-arḍi wa-llāhu bi-mā taʿmalūna khabīrun
versets.trad: Let not those who are miserly with what God has given them of His bounty think it good for them; rather it is an evil for them. On the Day of Resurrection they shall have hung about their necks that with which they were miserly. To God belongs the inheritance of the heavens and the earth, and God is fully aware of what you do.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: whoever prefers a thing to God receives no blessing from it; he will not enjoy it lastingly here below, nor will he, in the hereafter, ward off the chastisement it draws upon him. Then he lifts the meaning higher: avarice, upon the tongue of the learned, is to withhold what is due; but according to the allusion, it is to keep for oneself the smallest particle — be it an atom of good or a single breath of one's own states.

mots.racine: ب خ ل
mots.def: bukhl: to withhold what one ought to give.

mots.racine: ط و ق
mots.sens: they shall be encircled (about the neck)
mots.def: ṭawwaqa: to place a collar, to clasp around.

versets.ar: لَّقَدْ سَمِعَ اللَّهُ قَوْلَ الَّذِينَ قَالُوا إِنَّ اللَّهَ فَقِيرٌ وَنَحْنُ أَغْنِيَاءُ ۘ سَنَكْتُبُ مَا قَالُوا وَقَتْلَهُمُ الْأَنبِيَاءَ بِغَيْرِ حَقٍّ وَنَقُولُ ذُوقُوا عَذَابَ الْحَرِيقِ
versets.tr: la-qad samiʿa llāhu qawla lladhīna qālū inna llāha faqīrun wa-naḥnu aghniyāʾu sa-naktubu mā qālū wa-qatlahumu l-anbiyāʾa bi-ghayri ḥaqqin wa-naqūlu dhūqū ʿadhāba l-ḥarīqi
versets.trad: God has indeed heard the speech of those who said: "God is poor, and we are rich!" We shall record what they said, and their slaying of the prophets without right; and We shall say: "Taste the chastisement of the Blaze
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: this utterance, were it spoken among creatures, would be a grievance; and to complain of one's enemies to one's friends is the custom of the beloved. And he adds: God knew that among the believers some would slander — the ugliness of their word; so He disclosed an ugliness greater still, that the ugliness of the believers' word might appear lesser beside the ugliness of the deniers' word, who dared to ascribe to Him what ill befits His majesty.

mots.racine: ف ق ر
ك ت ب / ق و ل
mots.sens: We shall record what they said

versets.ar: ذَٰلِكَ بِمَا قَدَّمَتْ أَيْدِيكُمْ وَأَنَّ اللَّهَ لَيْسَ بِظَلَّامٍ لِّلْعَبِيدِ
versets.tr: dhālika bi-mā qaddamat aydīkum wa-anna llāha laysa bi-ẓallāmin li-l-ʿabīdi
versets.trad: as the recompense for what your hands have wrought." God is not unjust toward His servants.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: among creatures this would be almost an apology; as though the Real were saying: "My servant, this chastisement you meet today is because the fault is yours; had you not committed it, I would not have punished you." There is no iniquity in God: the evil is the harvest of man's own hand.

mots.racine: ق د م / ي د ي
mots.def: qaddama: to send before oneself (one's works).

mots.racine: ظ ل م

versets.ar: الَّذِينَ قَالُوا إِنَّ اللَّهَ عَهِدَ إِلَيْنَا أَلَّا نُؤْمِنَ لِرَسُولٍ حَتَّىٰ يَأْتِيَنَا بِقُرْبَانٍ تَأْكُلُهُ النَّارُ ۗ قُلْ قَدْ جَاءَكُمْ رُسُلٌ مِّن قَبْلِي بِالْبَيِّنَاتِ وَبِالَّذِي قُلْتُمْ فَلِمَ قَتَلْتُمُوهُمْ إِن كُنتُمْ صَادِقِينَ
versets.tr: alladhīna qālū inna llāha ʿahida ilaynā allā nuʾmina li-rasūlin ḥattā yaʾtiyanā bi-qurbānin taʾkuluhu n-nāru qul qad jāʾakum rusulun min qablī bi-l-bayyināti wa-bi-lladhī qultum fa-lima qataltumūhum in kuntum ṣādiqīna
versets.trad: There are those who say: "God has charged us not to believe in any messenger until he brings us an offering that the fire consumes." Say: "Messengers have already come to you before me with clear proofs, and with that of which you speak. Why then did you slay them, if you are truthful?"
themes: the pretext for refusing to believe
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: they invented false pretexts against God for forsaking faith. Say to them: prophets brought you what you demanded, and you did not believe; were I to grant it to you, you would believe no more. For the one whom the prior decrees have cast aside — though the sun should speak to him in clear tongue, or the mountains prostrate before him beneath his very gaze — knowledge would not penetrate his heart: he would only add doubt upon doubt.

mots.racine: ق ر ب / أ ك ل / ن و ر
mots.sens: an offering that the fire consumes

mots.racine: ق ت ل
mots.sens: why then did you slay them?

versets.ar: فَإِن كَذَّبُوكَ فَقَدْ كُذِّبَ رُسُلٌ مِّن قَبْلِكَ جَاءُوا بِالْبَيِّنَاتِ وَالزُّبُرِ وَالْكِتَابِ الْمُنِيرِ
versets.tr: fa-in kadhdhabūka fa-qad kudhdhiba rusulun min qablika jāʾū bi-l-bayyināti wa-z-zuburi wa-l-kitābi l-munīri
versets.trad: If they charge you with falsehood, so too were messengers before you charged with falsehood, who came with clear proofs, with the Scriptures, and with the luminous Book.
themes: denial, the custom of the deniers
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī, briefly: to charge the messengers with falsehood is the custom of the deniers; thus their forebears walked, and their successors conformed in turn. The rejection of the Prophet is but one link in the long chain of denial.

mots.racine: ك ذ ب
mots.def: kadhdhaba: to deny, to call a liar.

mots.racine: ز ب ر / ك ت ب / ن و ر
mots.sens: the Scriptures and the luminous Book
mots.def: zubur: revealed pages; munīr: that which gives light.

versets.ar: كُلُّ نَفْسٍ ذَائِقَةُ الْمَوْتِ ۗ وَإِنَّمَا تُوَفَّوْنَ أُجُورَكُمْ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ ۖ فَمَن زُحْزِحَ عَنِ النَّارِ وَأُدْخِلَ الْجَنَّةَ فَقَدْ فَازَ ۗ وَمَا الْحَيَاةُ الدُّنْيَا إِلَّا مَتَاعُ الْغُرُورِ
versets.tr: kullu nafsin dhāʾiqatu l-mawti wa-innamā tuwaffawna ujūrakum yawma l-qiyāmati fa-man zuḥziḥa ʿani n-nāri wa-udkhila l-jannata fa-qad fāza wa-mā l-ḥayātu d-dunyā illā matāʿu l-ghurūri
versets.trad: Every soul shall taste death. And only on the Day of Resurrection shall you be paid your rewards in full. Whoever is drawn away from the Fire and admitted into the Garden has attained felicity. The life of this world is but the enjoyment of illusion.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the cup of death is set upon the palm of every living being: whoever drinks it with a soul at peace inherits the rapture of ecstasy; whoever swallows it in the bitterness of refusal falls into the abyss of rejection and bears the brand of estrangement. On the promised Day, whoever is saved from the Fire attains the supreme repose; whoever burns in the blaze falls into the supreme affliction. And "the life of this world is but a deceptive enjoyment," for that which is to come is near.

mots.racine: ذ و ق / م و ت
م ت ع / غ ر ر

versets.ar: لَتُبْلَوُنَّ فِي أَمْوَالِكُمْ وَأَنفُسِكُمْ وَلَتَسْمَعُنَّ مِنَ الَّذِينَ أُوتُوا الْكِتَابَ مِن قَبْلِكُمْ وَمِنَ الَّذِينَ أَشْرَكُوا أَذًى كَثِيرًا ۚ وَإِن تَصْبِرُوا وَتَتَّقُوا فَإِنَّ ذَٰلِكَ مِنْ عَزْمِ الْأُمُورِ
versets.tr: la-tublawunna fī amwālikum wa-anfusikum wa-la-tasmaʿunna mina lladhīna ūtū l-kitāba min qablikum wa-mina lladhīna ashrakū adhan kathīran wa-in taṣbirū wa-tattaqū fa-inna dhālika min ʿazmi l-umūri
versets.trad: You shall surely be tried in your wealth and in your persons; and you shall surely hear much that is painful from those who were given the Book before you and from the idolaters. Yet if you are patient and fear God, that is among the matters demanding firm resolve.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: He spares them the greater part of the onslaughts of harm by forewarning them that it will reach them before it comes to pass, and lets them know that the better of the two courses is to choose patience and to opt for serenity beneath the flowing of the decree. To know the trial beforehand is already to disarm half of it.

mots.racine: ب ل و
mots.def: balā: to put to the test.

mots.racine: ع ز م / أ م ر

versets.ar: وَإِذْ أَخَذَ اللَّهُ مِيثَاقَ الَّذِينَ أُوتُوا الْكِتَابَ لَتُبَيِّنُنَّهُ لِلنَّاسِ وَلَا تَكْتُمُونَهُ فَنَبَذُوهُ وَرَاءَ ظُهُورِهِمْ وَاشْتَرَوْا بِهِ ثَمَنًا قَلِيلًا ۖ فَبِئْسَ مَا يَشْتَرُونَ
versets.tr: wa-idh akhadha llāhu mīthāqa lladhīna ūtū l-kitāba la-tubayyinunnahu li-n-nāsi wa-lā taktumūnahu fa-nabadhūhu warāʾa ẓuhūrihim wa-shtaraw bihi thamanan qalīlan fa-biʾsa mā yashtarūna
versets.trad: When God took the covenant of those to whom the Book was given: "You shall make it clear to mankind and shall not conceal it," they cast it behind their backs and bartered it for a paltry price. How wretched is what they barter!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: He relates that they had sealed their pledges never to swerve from their fidelity; but they broke the bonds of the covenant by the disbelief into which they fell. Then it became plain that what they took in exchange for the loss of their religion was but base and fleeting goods, holding no blessing for them whatsoever.

mots.racine: و ث ق
ن ب ذ / ظ ه ر
mots.sens: they cast it behind their backs
mots.def: nabadha: to cast off, to abandon.

versets.ar: لَا تَحْسَبَنَّ الَّذِينَ يَفْرَحُونَ بِمَا أَتَوا وَّيُحِبُّونَ أَن يُحْمَدُوا بِمَا لَمْ يَفْعَلُوا فَلَا تَحْسَبَنَّهُم بِمَفَازَةٍ مِّنَ الْعَذَابِ ۖ وَلَهُمْ عَذَابٌ أَلِيمٌ
versets.tr: lā taḥsabanna lladhīna yafraḥūna bi-mā ataw wa-yuḥibbūna an yuḥmadū bi-mā lam yafʿalū fa-lā taḥsabannahum bi-mafāzatin mina l-ʿadhābi wa-lahum ʿadhābun alīmun
versets.trad: Think not that those who exult in what they have done and love to be praised for what they have not done — think not that they are safe from chastisement! A painful chastisement is reserved for them.
themes: the chastisement of the veil, here below
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī unveils the inner meaning: the one who has installed in his heart the regard of creatures, who watches for them from the depths of his secret — do not think his chastisement is deferred to the Day of Resurrection; no, even now they are not safe from the torment. And what chastisement is harsher than to be turned back toward creatures and to have the veil cast over the Real?

mots.racine: ف ر ح / أ ت ي
mots.sens: they exult in what they have done

mots.racine: ف و ز / ع ذ ب
mots.def: mafāza: a place of safety, an escape.

versets.ar: وَلِلَّهِ مُلْكُ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ ۗ وَاللَّهُ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ
versets.tr: wa-li-llāhi mulku s-samāwāti wa-l-arḍi wa-llāhu ʿalā kulli shayʾin qadīrun
versets.trad: To God belongs the Sovereignty of the heavens and the earth, and God has power over all things.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the allusion of this verse bears here upon His absolute richness over all that the universe contains: how should He have need of them? Whereas they find in Him no substitute, no replacement. The Sovereignty is wholly His, and the creature has no subsistence save through Him.

mots.racine: م ل ك
mots.sens: the sovereignty of the heavens and the earth

mots.racine: ق د ر

versets.ar: إِنَّ فِي خَلْقِ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ وَاخْتِلَافِ اللَّيْلِ وَالنَّهَارِ لَآيَاتٍ لِّأُولِي الْأَلْبَابِ
versets.tr: inna fī khalqi s-samāwāti wa-l-arḍi wa-khtilāfi l-layli wa-n-nahāri la-āyātin li-ulī l-albābi
versets.trad: Truly in the creation of the heavens and the earth, and in the alternation of night and day, there are signs for those endowed with understanding,
themes: the signs for those endowed with understanding
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī distinguishes two orders of signs: the signs by which the Real makes Himself known to the common folk are those upon the horizons — the lessons of beings and the traces of creation; and the signs by which He makes Himself known to the elect are those within their very souls. The one read the universe, the others read themselves to find Him.

mots.racine: خ ل ف / ل ي ل / ن ه ر
mots.sens: the alternation of night and day

mots.racine: ل ب ب
mots.def: lubb: kernel, the core of the intellect.

versets.ar: الَّذِينَ يَذْكُرُونَ اللَّهَ قِيَامًا وَقُعُودًا وَعَلَىٰ جُنُوبِهِمْ وَيَتَفَكَّرُونَ فِي خَلْقِ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ رَبَّنَا مَا خَلَقْتَ هَٰذَا بَاطِلًا سُبْحَانَكَ فَقِنَا عَذَابَ النَّارِ
versets.tr: alladhīna yadhkurūna llāha qiyāman wa-quʿūdan wa-ʿalā junūbihim wa-yatafakkarūna fī khalqi s-samāwāti wa-l-arḍi rabbanā mā khalaqta hādhā bāṭilan subḥānaka fa-qinā ʿadhāba n-nāri
versets.trad: for those who remember God standing, sitting, and lying upon their sides, and who meditate upon the creation of the heavens and the earth: "Our Lord! You have not created this in vain! Glory be to You! Preserve us from the chastisement of the Fire.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī grades meditation according to its degrees: when contemplation and presence arrive, the servant rises beyond thought to the very bounds of remembrance, for the dhikr is everlasting. Then: the thought of the renunciants dwells upon the passing away of the world, and they grow in detachment; that of the worshippers upon the beauty of the reward, and they grow in ardour; that of the gnostics upon the graces and the bounties, and they grow in love for the Real. And "Glory be to You": glorification denotes the swimming of the secrets in the oceans of reverence.

mots.racine: ذ ك ر / ق و م / ق ع د
mots.sens: they remember God standing and sitting

mots.racine: خ ل ق / ب ط ل
mots.sens: You did not create this in vain

versets.ar: رَبَّنَا إِنَّكَ مَن تُدْخِلِ النَّارَ فَقَدْ أَخْزَيْتَهُ ۖ وَمَا لِلظَّالِمِينَ مِنْ أَنصَارٍ
versets.tr: rabbanā innaka man tudkhili n-nāra fa-qad akhzaytahu wa-mā li-ẓ-ẓālimīna min anṣārin
versets.trad: Our Lord! Whomever You cast into the Fire, You have covered with disgrace. For the wrongdoers there are no helpers.
themes: the disgrace of the Fire, the disgrace of separation
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī opens a threefold gradation: the one whom You try in the hereafter by burning, him You have covered with disgrace; the one whom You try here below by separation, him You have made wretched; and the one whom You favour with the felicity of union, him You have sheltered and brought near. The true fire is estrangement, the true felicity nearness.

mots.racine: خ ز ي
mots.sens: You have covered him with disgrace

mots.racine: ظ ل م / ن ص ر

versets.ar: رَّبَّنَا إِنَّنَا سَمِعْنَا مُنَادِيًا يُنَادِي لِلْإِيمَانِ أَنْ آمِنُوا بِرَبِّكُمْ فَآمَنَّا ۚ رَبَّنَا فَاغْفِرْ لَنَا ذُنُوبَنَا وَكَفِّرْ عَنَّا سَيِّئَاتِنَا وَتَوَفَّنَا مَعَ الْأَبْرَارِ
versets.tr: rabbanā innanā samiʿnā munādiyan yunādī li-l-īmāni an āminū bi-rabbikum fa-āmannā rabbanā fa-ghfir lanā dhunūbanā wa-kaffir ʿannā sayyiʾātinā wa-tawaffanā maʿa l-abrāri
versets.trad: Our Lord! We have heard a herald calling to faith: "Believe in your Lord!", and we have believed. Our Lord! Forgive us our sins! Efface our evil deeds! And take us back to You with the righteous!
themes: the call and the response of faith
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: we have responded to the One who calls, but it is You who are the Guide; do not abandon us to ourselves, do not withdraw from us the shade of Your solicitude. And he defines: faith is the entry into that which makes safety necessary; none believes in the Real save the one whom the Real has set in safety — His safeguarding of the servant, which is His protection, makes necessary the servant's faith, which is his assent and his knowledge.

mots.racine: ن د و / أ م ن
و ف ي / ب ر ر
mots.sens: take us back with the righteous

versets.ar: رَبَّنَا وَآتِنَا مَا وَعَدتَّنَا عَلَىٰ رُسُلِكَ وَلَا تُخْزِنَا يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ ۗ إِنَّكَ لَا تُخْلِفُ الْمِيعَادَ
versets.tr: rabbanā wa-ātinā mā waʿadtanā ʿalā rusulika wa-lā tukhzinā yawma l-qiyāmati innaka lā tukhlifu l-mīʿāda
versets.trad: Our Lord! Grant us what You have promised us through Your messengers; and disgrace us not on the Day of Resurrection. Truly You do not fail Your promise."
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: fulfil for us what You have promised us by the tongue of the intermediaries: the perfect completion of grace, and the forgiveness of all that has escaped us in submission to passion. The One who never fails His promise will fulfil what He has announced through His messengers.

mots.racine: و ع د / ر س ل
mots.sens: what You promised us through Your messengers

mots.racine: خ ل ف / و ع د
mots.sens: You do not break the promise
mots.def: akhlafa: to fail in; mīʿād: an engagement.

versets.ar: فَاسْتَجَابَ لَهُمْ رَبُّهُمْ أَنِّي لَا أُضِيعُ عَمَلَ عَامِلٍ مِّنكُم مِّن ذَكَرٍ أَوْ أُنثَىٰ ۖ بَعْضُكُم مِّن بَعْضٍ ۖ فَالَّذِينَ هَاجَرُوا وَأُخْرِجُوا مِن دِيَارِهِمْ وَأُوذُوا فِي سَبِيلِي وَقَاتَلُوا وَقُتِلُوا لَأُكَفِّرَنَّ عَنْهُمْ سَيِّئَاتِهِمْ وَلَأُدْخِلَنَّهُمْ جَنَّاتٍ تَجْرِي مِن تَحْتِهَا الْأَنْهَارُ ثَوَابًا مِّنْ عِندِ اللَّهِ ۗ وَاللَّهُ عِندَهُ حُسْنُ الثَّوَابِ
versets.tr: fa-stajāba lahum rabbuhum annī lā uḍīʿu ʿamala ʿāmilin minkum min dhakarin aw unthā baʿḍukum min baʿḍin fa-lladhīna hājarū wa-ukhrijū min diyārihim wa-ūdhū fī sabīlī wa-qātalū wa-qutilū la-ukaffiranna ʿanhum sayyiʾātihim wa-la-udkhilannahum jannātin tajrī min taḥtihā l-anhāru thawāban min ʿindi llāhi wa-llāhu ʿindahu ḥusnu th-thawābi
versets.trad: Their Lord answered them: "I let not the work of any worker among you be lost, whether male or female; you proceed one from another. As for those who emigrated, who were driven from their homes, who suffered harm in My way, who fought and were slain — I shall surely efface their evil deeds and admit them into Gardens beneath which rivers flow, a reward from God. And with God lies the most beautiful reward!"
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: how should He not answer them, He who inspired in them the invocation, He who guaranteed them the response, and promised them a beautiful reward for the very prayer, beyond what they asked for their needs? He unveils the inner sense of the migration (hijra): "those who emigrated" — the dwellings and familiar places, and all that distracts them from the Real; "driven from their homes" — toward the severance of their habits; "persecuted in My way" — tried by poverty, blame, and every sort of torment; "they fought and were slain" — they tasted, in the alternation of states, the sweet and the bitter. And "I shall efface their faults": I shall give them above their hopes, more than their works and states deserved.

mots.racine: ج و ب / ر ب ب
mots.def: istajāba: to respond to the invocation.

mots.racine: ه ج ر
mots.def: hijra: to forsake for God's sake.

mots.racine: ك ف ر / س و أ
mots.sens: I shall surely efface their misdeeds
mots.def: kaffara: to cover, to efface (the faults).

versets.ar: لَا يَغُرَّنَّكَ تَقَلُّبُ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا فِي الْبِلَادِ
versets.tr: lā yaghurrannaka taqallubu lladhīna kafarū fī l-bilādi
versets.trad: Let not the comings and goings of the disbelievers throughout the land deceive you [as they parade their prosperity].
themes: not to be deluded by the prosperity of the impious
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: let no suspicion seize you that they hold with Us any worth or any rank; these are but numbered days and counted breaths, followed by cascading regrets and redoubled sorrows. Their triumphant going to and fro is but a reprieve.

mots.racine: ق ل ب
mots.sens: the going to and fro, the bustling
mots.def: taqallub: to move about freely, to prosper.

mots.racine: ب ل د

versets.ar: مَتَاعٌ قَلِيلٌ ثُمَّ مَأْوَاهُمْ جَهَنَّمُ ۚ وَبِئْسَ الْمِهَادُ
versets.tr: matāʿun qalīlun thumma maʾwāhum jahannamu wa-biʾsa l-mihādu
versets.trad: It is but a brief enjoyment for them! Thereafter Hell shall be their abode: how wretched a resting-place!
themes: the brief enjoyment, the dismal abode
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī, prolonging the preceding verse: it is but a paltry enjoyment, rare days and counted breaths; then, after them, regrets succeeding one another and sorrows multiplying. The bed they have prepared for themselves is the worst of beds.

mots.racine: م ت ع / ق ل ل
mots.def: matāʿ: a fleeting good; qalīl: little.

mots.racine: ب أ س / م ه د
mots.def: mihād: a couch, a place of settling.

versets.ar: لَٰكِنِ الَّذِينَ اتَّقَوْا رَبَّهُمْ لَهُمْ جَنَّاتٌ تَجْرِي مِن تَحْتِهَا الْأَنْهَارُ خَالِدِينَ فِيهَا نُزُلًا مِّنْ عِندِ اللَّهِ ۗ وَمَا عِندَ اللَّهِ خَيْرٌ لِّلْأَبْرَارِ
versets.tr: lākini lladhīna ttaqaw rabbahum lahum jannātun tajrī min taḥtihā l-anhāru khālidīna fīhā nuzulan min ʿindi llāhi wa-mā ʿinda llāhi khayrun li-l-abrāri
versets.trad: But those who fear their Lord shall dwell for ever in Gardens beneath which rivers flow. This shall be their abode in the presence of God; and what is with God is the better portion reserved for the righteous.
themes: the abode of the pious in the presence of God
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī sets the two destinies side by side: those upon whom We have set the humiliation of separation, how wretched is their state! And those who, for Our sake, have advanced a single step, how blessed is their state and their nearness: they have attained the abiding reward and dwell in union and felicity. And "what is with God," of what We have reserved for them, is better than what they hoped for by their own choosing.

mots.racine: و ق ي / ر ب ب
ن ز ل
mots.def: nuzul: a dwelling and welcome prepared.

versets.ar: وَإِنَّ مِنْ أَهْلِ الْكِتَابِ لَمَن يُؤْمِنُ بِاللَّهِ وَمَا أُنزِلَ إِلَيْكُمْ وَمَا أُنزِلَ إِلَيْهِمْ خَاشِعِينَ لِلَّهِ لَا يَشْتَرُونَ بِآيَاتِ اللَّهِ ثَمَنًا قَلِيلًا ۗ أُولَٰئِكَ لَهُمْ أَجْرُهُمْ عِندَ رَبِّهِمْ ۗ إِنَّ اللَّهَ سَرِيعُ الْحِسَابِ
versets.tr: wa-inna min ahli l-kitābi la-man yuʾminu bi-llāhi wa-mā unzila ilaykum wa-mā unzila ilayhim khāshiʿīna li-llāhi lā yashtarūna bi-āyāti llāhi thamanan qalīlan ulāʾika lahum ajruhum ʿinda rabbihim inna llāha sarīʿu l-ḥisābi
versets.trad: Among the People of the Book there are some who believe in God, in what has been revealed to you and in what has been revealed to them, who are humble before God, and who do not sell the signs of God for a paltry price. These shall find their reward with their Lord. God, truly, is swift in reckoning.
themes: the believers among the People of the Book
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī, concise: He intends those whom the apportionment has favoured with the fairest share: they are with the friends of God by grace as they were with them by the lot assigned to them. Humility before God and the refusal to barter away His signs place them among the blessed.

mots.racine: خ ش ع
mots.def: khushūʿ: inward recollection, humility of heart.

mots.ar: لَا يَشْتَرُونَ بِآيَاتِ اللَّهِ ثَمَنًا قَلِيلًا
mots.tr: lā yashtarūna bi-āyāti llāhi thamanan qalīlan
mots.racine: ش ر ي / أ ي ي
mots.sens: they do not barter away the signs of God
mots.def: not to trade the truth for a base profit.

versets.ar: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا اصْبِرُوا وَصَابِرُوا وَرَابِطُوا وَاتَّقُوا اللَّهَ لَعَلَّكُمْ تُفْلِحُونَ
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā lladhīna āmanū ṣbirū wa-ṣābirū wa-rābiṭū wa-ttaqū llāha laʿallakum tufliḥūna
versets.trad: O you who believe! Be patient! Vie with one another in patience! Hold firmly to God and fear Him! That you may attain felicity...
versets.commentaire: The verse that seals the sūra with a call to graded patience: to endure (ṣabr), to outvie the adversary in endurance (muṣābara), and to stand watch upon the path of God (ribāṭ). Reverential awe of God is its crown, and felicity — al-falāḥ — the promise offered to whoever perseveres in cleaving to the Real.

mots.racine: ص ب ر
ص ب ر
mots.def: muṣābara: to outdo the adversary in endurance.

mots.racine: ر ب ط
mots.def: ribāṭ: to take post on the frontier, to bind one's heart to God.
